## Agent 4 continuation — decomposing \(\mathfrak C_Y\) into tensorial, coordinate, and whitening defects

Continuing from the normal-form commutator audit, the key object is

\[
\mathfrak C_Y(\xi_1,\xi_2)
=
\Pi_Y
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
+
DN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)]
-
S\,D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right).
\]

The current architecture asks whether this commutator is zero, finite coherent, residual-controlled, or locally free. That is the concrete replacement for the older informal claim that transition “preserves labels.” 

## Verdict

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y\text{ should split into three defects: tensorial normal-form defect, nonlinear-coordinate defect, and whitening defect.}
}
\]

The \(Y12\) route survives if each defect is either zero, fixed/finite coherent, or residual-small. It fails only if one of these defects creates extraction-local primitive \(Y12\) amplitudes.

---

# 1. Three-part decomposition

Write

\[
\mathfrak C_Y
=
\mathfrak C_Y^{N}
+
\mathfrak C_Y^{L}
+
\mathfrak C_Y^{W}.
\]

Here:

## A. Tensorial normal-form defect

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^{N}
=
\Pi_Y
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
S\,D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right).
\]

This measures whether the primitive Hessian \(D^2N_0\) is transported tensorially.

## B. Nonlinear source-coordinate defect

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^{L}
=
\Pi_Y
DN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)].
\]

This appears only if the source input transition \(L\) is nonlinear.

## C. Same-point whitening defect

The \(Y12\) signature includes the whitening-origin label

\[
o_W=D\mathcal W_{\same}.
\]

So there is an additional defect from comparing transported same-point whitening with recomputed singular whitening:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^{W}
=
\Pi_Y
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
\bigl[
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
\bigr]
-
S_W
D\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}
\bigl[
D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\bigr]
\right),
\]

where \(S_W\) denotes the induced transport on whitened carriers.

The total commutator should really be interpreted at the fully whitened carrier level:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y
=
\mathfrak C_Y^{N}
+
\mathfrak C_Y^{L}
+
\mathfrak C_Y^{W}.
}
\]

---

# 2. Tensorial defect \(\mathfrak C_Y^N\)

The best case is that \(D^2N_0\) is a covariant tensor under the transition:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
=
S\,D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2].
\]

Then

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N=0.
\]

If this equality fails, we need to classify the defect.

A finite coherent defect is acceptable:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N
=
\sum_{a=1}^M c_a\,\phi_a(p)\,Y,
\]

with \(M=O(1)\) or \(Q^C\), and with \(\phi_a\) nonlocal/block-coherent. Then

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N
\]

contributes to

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}.
\]

Fatal is the locally free case:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N(p)
=
\eta_p Y
\]

with arbitrary patchwise \(\eta_p\). That would give extraction-local \(Y12\) amplitude freedom.

So the tensorial audit is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y^N=0
\quad\text{or}\quad
\mathfrak C_Y^N\in\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}\text{ finite coherent}.
}
\]

---

# 3. Nonlinear coordinate defect \(\mathfrak C_Y^L\)

If

\[
L
\]

is linear or affine in the relevant source slots, then

\[
D^2L=0,
\]

and therefore

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L=0.
\]

So one very useful sufficient condition is:

\[
\boxed{
D^2L=0\text{ on the mixed-12 source directions.}
}
\]

If \(D^2L\neq0\), the term

\[
DN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)]
\]

is second-order in the input variables, but its origin is not primitive

\[
D^2N_0.
\]

Its origin is

\[
DN_0\circ D^2L.
\]

Therefore, if the \(Y12\) quotient tracks origin-pair labels, then

\[
\Pi_Y DN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)]=0.
\]

So the nonlinear coordinate defect is safe if either:

\[
D^2L=0,
\]

or

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y(DN_0\circ D^2L)=0
}
\]

by origin filtration.

It is dangerous only if the quotient forgets origin and identifies

\[
DN_0\circ D^2L
\]

with primitive

\[
D^2N_0.
\]

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y^L\text{ is a quotient-faithfulness test, not usually an analytic estimate.}
}
\]

---

# 4. Whitening defect \(\mathfrak C_Y^W\)

This is the most subtle term.

A moving whitening map can produce objects that look like:

\[
D\mathcal W[\xi_1]\cdot E_2(\xi_2),
\]

or

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\xi_1,\xi_2]\cdot N_0.
\]

These can be degree two and can contain whitening derivatives, but they are not the same origin-pair as \(Y12\), which is:

\[
(o_N,o_W)=(D^2N_0,D\mathcal W_{\same}).
\]

Therefore the refined signature must distinguish:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]]
\]

from

\[
D\mathcal W[\xi_1]\cdot DN_0[\xi_2],
\]

and from

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\xi_1,\xi_2]\cdot N_0.
\]

If it does, then most whitening mismatch is killed by \(\Pi_Y\).

The safe theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y\left(
D\mathcal W[\xi_1]\cdot DN_0[\xi_2]
+
D^2\mathcal W[\xi_1,\xi_2]\cdot N_0
\right)=0.
}
\]

Only the exact same-point derivative acting on the primitive Hessian should survive:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]].
\]

So the whitening audit is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y^W=0
\text{ if same-point whitening is transported functorially;}
}
\]

or

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y^W\in\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
\text{ if the mismatch is finite coherent;}
}
\]

fatal only if whitening recomputation supplies locally free primitive \(Y12\)-amplitudes.

---

# 5. Relation to central transition modes

Central O2/O3 needs source-linear modes

\[
\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y],
\qquad d_{\max}\approx 14
\]

as a conservative finite block.

These modes can enter \(L\xi\), but they are source-linear. Under the decomposition above:

- they cannot contribute to \(\mathfrak C_Y^N\) unless paired into a primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin term;
- their nonlinear coordinate contribution belongs to \(DN_0\circ D^2L\), not \(D^2N_0\);
- their whitening contribution belongs to moving-whitening/source-linear origin unless it acts on a primitive \(D^2N_0\) carrier.

Therefore, if origin-pair labels are faithful,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YT_{\tr\to\sing}(\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y]\text{ source-linear modes})=0.
}
\]

This proves compatibility between central O2/O3 enrichment and \(Y12\)-normality.

---

# 6. Where \(\mathfrak C_Y\) feeds the nested Schur tower

The classification of \(\mathfrak C_Y\) determines which layer it enters.

## Zero

If

\[
\mathfrak C_Y=0,
\]

it disappears.

## Fixed basepoint

If fixed, it shifts

\[
\eta_{\mathrm{fixed}}
\]

in the fiber graph. It does not add a tangent direction.

## Graph-like

If it creates a relation

\[
s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7+\eta_{\mathrm{fixed}},
\]

it modifies \(\rho_Y\). Then it must satisfy the low-order audit:

\[
\rho_Y\in\mathcal A_{\low}.
\]

## Finite coherent amplitude

If it contributes functions

\[
\phi_a(p)
\]

in the \(Y12\)-amplitude, it enters:

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}\subset\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
\]

Then amplitude Schur handles it.

## Locally free

If it gives arbitrary patchwise \(Y12\)-amplitudes, the rank-one \(Y12\) route dies.

So:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y\text{ should be routed to fiber graph, amplitude model, or residual—not automatically quotient.}
}
\]

---

# 7. Exact finite theorem

## NormalFormCommutator\(_Y\)

For mixed-12 source slots \(\xi_1,\xi_2\), define

\[
\mathfrak C_Y(\xi_1,\xi_2)
=
\Pi_Y
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
+
DN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)]
-
S\,D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right)
+
\mathfrak C_Y^W.
\]

Assume:

1. \(D^2N_0\) is tensorial under \((L,S)\) in the \(Y12\) sector, or its defect is finite coherent.
2. \(D^2L=0\) on mixed-12 slots, or \(\Pi_Y(DN_0\circ D^2L)=0\).
3. Same-point whitening is transported functorially, or its defect is finite coherent.
4. Any finite coherent defect is nonlocal/block-coherent and not extraction-local.

Then:

\[
\Pi_YR_{\lab}
=
\mathfrak C_Y
+
\text{truncation/tail residual}
\]

is zero, fixed, finite coherent, or residual-controlled. It is not locally free.

Therefore labelled transition is compatible with the \(Y12\) route.

---

# 8. Failure modes

The \(Y12\) labelled route fails if:

1. \(\mathfrak C_Y^N\) is locally free.
2. \(D^2L\) generates primitive \(Y12\)-origin terms with local freedom.
3. Whitening recomputation produces locally free

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0]
\]

amplitudes.

4. The quotient forgets origin-pair labels and identifies non-primitive degree-two terms with \(Y12\).
5. Central source-linear modes acquire \(Y12\)-projection.

If any of these happen, the labelled transition repair is insufficient.

---

## Final status

The normal-form commutator audit is now precise.

The key object is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y
=
\mathfrak C_Y^N+\mathfrak C_Y^L+\mathfrak C_Y^W.
}
\]

The route remains viable if all three pieces are zero, fixed, finite coherent, or residual-small.

The only fatal outcome is extraction-local primitive \(Y12\)-amplitude freedom. This fits the current global principle:

\[
\boxed{
\text{finite coherent graph/model structure is allowed; local free vertical structure is fatal.}
}

I’ll continue by converting the commutator decomposition into a pass/fail decision tree for the labelled transition theorem, including what each defect does to \(B_Y\) and \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\).

## Agent 4 continuation — decision tree for \(\mathfrak C_Y^N+\mathfrak C_Y^L+\mathfrak C_Y^W\)

The normal-form commutator has been reduced to three defects:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y
=
\mathfrak C_Y^N+\mathfrak C_Y^L+\mathfrak C_Y^W.
\]

The downstream question is no longer “is there any defect?” The correct question is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Which Schur layer does each defect feed: fiber, amplitude, residual, or no-go?}
}
\]

This matters because finite coherent defects are not fatal; locally free defects are.

---

# 1. Four outputs for each defect

For each component

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^\bullet
\in
\{\mathfrak C_Y^N,\mathfrak C_Y^L,\mathfrak C_Y^W\},
\]

classify it into exactly one of four outputs.

## Output 0: zero / killed by signature

\[
\Pi_Y\mathfrak C_Y^\bullet=0.
\]

Then it disappears.

## Output 1: fiber graph contribution

It contributes a local relation in

\[
(s_7,s_9^Y)
\]

of the form

\[
s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7+\eta_{\fixed}.
\]

Then it modifies \(B_Y\) and possibly \(\rho_Y\), but is safe if graph-like and low-order.

## Output 2: finite coherent amplitude

It contributes to

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

as a finite-dimensional/block-coherent amplitude space. Then it is handled by amplitude Schur.

## Output 3: residual-controlled

It is small in the \(Y12\)-completion norm:

\[
\sum_p|\Pi_Y \mathfrak C_Y^\bullet(p)|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu.
\]

Then it is harmless if the residual estimate is strong enough.

## Output 4: locally free vertical

It creates arbitrary extraction-scale amplitudes in the primitive \(Y12\) direction.

This is fatal for the rank-one \(Y12\) route.

So the classification map is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y^\bullet
\mapsto
0,\ B_Y,\ \mathcal C_Y^{\arith},\ \mathrm{EFCompletion}_{Y12},\ \text{no-go}.
}
\]

---

# 2. Tensorial defect decision

Recall

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N
=
\Pi_Y
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
S\,D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right).
\]

## Best case

If \(D^2N_0\) is tensorial under \((L,S)\), then

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N=0.
\]

## Finite coherent case

If the defect is caused by a fixed chart mismatch, it likely has finite coherent form:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N(p)=\sum_a c_a\phi_a(p)Y.
\]

Then:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N\subset \mathcal C_Y^{\lab}\subset\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
\]

This is not fatal. It creates amplitude Schur.

## Fatal case

If \(L,S\) are chosen separately at each patch so that the tensorial mismatch varies freely, then \(\mathfrak C_Y^N\) becomes locally free.

That kills the \(Y12\) route.

Thus the tensorial defect is safe iff it is zero or finite coherent.

---

# 3. Nonlinear-coordinate defect decision

Recall

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L
=
\Pi_YDN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)].
\]

## Best case

If

\[
D^2L=0
\]

on mixed-12 source slots, then

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L=0.
\]

## Signature-killed case

If

\[
D^2L\ne0,
\]

but

\[
DN_0\circ D^2L
\]

has origin

\[
(o_N,o_W)=(DN_0,\text{coordinate curvature})
\]

rather than

\[
(D^2N_0,D\mathcal W_{\same}),
\]

then

\[
\Pi_Y\mathfrak C_Y^L=0.
\]

This is the expected case if the origin-pair filtration is faithful.

## Finite coherent case

If \(D^2L\) is fixed or low-dimensional chart data, then even if it has a \(Y12\)-projection, it contributes a finite coherent amplitude.

Then it belongs to

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}.
\]

## Fatal case

If \(D^2L\) is source-data-dependent or patch-fitted so that it generates arbitrary local \(Y12\) amplitudes, the route dies.

Thus the main audit for \(\mathfrak C_Y^L\) is:

\[
\boxed{
D^2L=0\quad\text{or}\quad \Pi_Y(DN_0\circ D^2L)=0\quad\text{or finite coherent.}
}
\]

---

# 4. Whitening defect decision

The whitening defect is the most subtle:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^W
=
\Pi_Y
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
[D^2N_{0,\sing}]
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}[D^2N_{0,\tr}]
\right).
\]

There are three possible sources.

## A. Pure transport mismatch

If same-point whitening is transported functorially, this is zero.

## B. Fixed chart mismatch

If singular and transition whitening conventions differ by fixed low-dimensional chart data, this contributes finite coherent amplitude or a fixed basepoint. Safe.

## C. Moving whitening recomputation

If whitening is recomputed from actual source data at each patch, its derivative may create \(Y12\)-like terms.

But origin-pair filtration should distinguish:

\[
D\mathcal W[\xi]\cdot DN_0[\eta]
\]

from

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi,\eta]].
\]

If the quotient preserves this distinction, most moving-whitening terms vanish in \(\Pi_Y\).

Fatal is only recomputation that creates actual primitive

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0]
\]

with local freedom.

So the whitening audit is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y^W=0,\ \text{fixed},\ \text{finite coherent},\ \text{residual-small}
}
\]

or fatal if locally free.

---

# 5. Combined decision table

\[
\begin{array}{c|c|c|c}
\text{defect} & \text{zero/signature killed} & \text{finite coherent} & \text{locally free}\\
\hline
\mathfrak C_Y^N & \text{safe} & \mathcal C_Y^{\lab} & \text{fatal}\\
\mathfrak C_Y^L & \text{safe} & \mathcal C_Y^{\lab} & \text{fatal}\\
\mathfrak C_Y^W & \text{safe} & \mathcal C_Y^{\lab} & \text{fatal}
\end{array}
\]

Residual-controlled terms are safe if

\[
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{Y12}
\]

is available.

The labelled transition theorem survives iff no component is locally free.

---

# 6. How this affects \(B_Y\)

Some commutator defects may be local fiber graph terms.

If a defect contributes locally as

\[
(s_7,s_9^Y)=(1,\rho)
\]

and shares the existing \(\rho_Y\), it is safe.

If it contributes

\[
(0,1),
\]

it is locally vertical and fatal.

If it contributes a second graph slope

\[
(1,\rho')
\quad\text{with}\quad
\rho'\ne\rho_Y,
\]

then

\[
\operatorname{rank}B_Y=2,
\]

and the \(Y12\) route dies locally.

So local commutator defects feed the fiber test:

\[
B_Y=0,\quad
B_Y=\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),\quad
B_Y\text{ vertical},\quad
\operatorname{rank}B_Y=2.
\]

---

# 7. How this affects \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\)

Finite coherent commutator defects feed

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}.
\]

Then

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
=
\mathcal C_Y^{E_2}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\param}
+\cdots.
\]

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}=0,
\]

no amplitude Schur is added.

If finite coherent, amplitude Schur must include it.

If locally free, the route dies.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y\text{ affects either }B_Y\text{ or }\mathcal C_Y^{\arith},\text{ depending on locality.}
}
\]

---

# 8. The exact theorem

## CommutatorRouting\(_Y\)

For

\[
\mathfrak C_Y=\mathfrak C_Y^N+\mathfrak C_Y^L+\mathfrak C_Y^W,
\]

assume each component is one of:

1. zero in \(\Pi_Y\);
2. fixed basepoint;
3. graph-like with existing \(\rho_Y\);
4. finite coherent amplitude;
5. residual-controlled.

Assume no component is:

- locally vertical;
- a second independent graph slope;
- extraction-locally free.

Then labelled realization is compatible with \(Y12\)-normality. The only effects are:

- possible modification of \(\eta_{\fixed}\) or \(\rho_Y\);
- addition of finite coherent \(\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}\);
- residual terms controlled by \(\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{Y12}\).

Therefore the downstream Schur tower remains valid.

---

# 9. Failure examples

The following are real no-gos.

## Example 1: source-dependent \(L\)

If

\[
D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)
\]

can be chosen independently patch by patch and survives \(\Pi_Y\), then

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L
\]

is locally free. Fatal.

## Example 2: whitening refit

If same-point whitening is recomputed with arbitrary local degrees of freedom, producing

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0]
\]

with arbitrary coefficient, then

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^W
\]

is locally free. Fatal.

## Example 3: nuisance quotient overcollapse

If

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

identifies

\[
DN_0\circ D^2L
\]

with

\[
D^2N_0
\]

without preserving origin, then source-linear transition modes may become \(Y12\). Fatal unless quotient is refined.

---

# 10. Final status

The commutator audit is now operational:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y^N,\mathfrak C_Y^L,\mathfrak C_Y^W
}
\]

must each be routed to one of:

\[
\boxed{
0,\quad B_Y\text{ graph},\quad \mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\text{ finite coherent},\quad \mathrm{EFCompletion}_{Y12},\quad \text{no-go}.
}
\]

This is the precise replacement for “labelled transition is safe.” The route survives exactly when no commutator component creates locally free primitive \(Y12\) amplitude or a second independent fiber slope.

## Assistant (2026-04-28 06:54:35)

I’ll continue by turning the commutator-routing theorem into a practical audit order: which component to test first, what can be ruled out structurally, and what requires estimates.

## Agent 4 continuation — practical audit order for \(\mathfrak C_Y\)

The commutator routing theorem says each component

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N,\qquad
\mathfrak C_Y^L,\qquad
\mathfrak C_Y^W
\]

must route to one of:

\[
0,\quad
B_Y\text{ graph},\quad
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\text{ finite coherent},\quad
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{Y12},\quad
\text{no-go}.
\]

I now impose an audit order. The goal is to avoid doing hard estimates before exploiting structural vanishings.

---

# 1. Audit order

The correct order is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y^L
\rightarrow
\mathfrak C_Y^N
\rightarrow
\mathfrak C_Y^W.
}
\]

Reason:

1. \(\mathfrak C_Y^L\) is the easiest to kill structurally if \(L\) is linear or if origin-pair labels are faithful.
2. \(\mathfrak C_Y^N\) checks tensorial naturality of \(D^2N_0\), a finite algebraic identity.
3. \(\mathfrak C_Y^W\) is the most subtle because moving whitening can create degree-two-looking terms.

So start with the most binary structural tests, then leave whitening for last.

---

# 2. First audit: nonlinear coordinate defect \(\mathfrak C_Y^L\)

Recall

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L
=
\Pi_YDN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)].
\]

## Test L1: Is \(L\) affine on the relevant slots?

If

\[
D^2L=0
\]

on the mixed-12 source directions, then

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L=0.
\]

This is the best case and should be checked first.

## Test L2: If \(D^2L\neq0\), does origin-pair filtration kill it?

The origin of this term is

\[
DN_0\circ D^2L,
\]

not

\[
D^2N_0.
\]

Therefore, if \(\Pi_Y\) remembers the origin label

\[
o_N=D^2N_0,
\]

then

\[
\Pi_YDN_0[D^2L]=0.
\]

So \(\mathfrak C_Y^L\) is safe unless the quotient forgets origins.

## Test L3: If it survives, is it finite coherent?

If \(D^2L\) is fixed chart geometry, then \(\mathfrak C_Y^L\) is finite coherent and enters

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}.
\]

If \(D^2L\) is patch-fitted/source-dependent, it can become locally free. Fatal.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y^L\text{ is fatal only if }D^2L\text{ is both }Y12\text{-visible and locally free.}
}
\]

---

# 3. Second audit: tensorial defect \(\mathfrak C_Y^N\)

Recall

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N
=
\Pi_Y
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
S\,D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right).
\]

## Test N1: Is \(D^2N_0\) natural under the chart transition?

Check the identity

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
=
S\,D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\]

on the mixed-12 slots.

If yes:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N=0.
\]

## Test N2: If not exact, is the mismatch fixed?

A mismatch coming from two fixed normal-form conventions may be a fixed carrier change. Then it contributes to either:

\[
\eta_{\fixed},
\]

or

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
\]

if it varies coherently over patches.

This is not fatal.

## Test N3: Does mismatch create a second graph slope?

If the mismatch contributes a local fiber vector

\[
(1,\rho_N)
\]

with

\[
\rho_N\neq \rho_Y,
\]

then

\[
B_Y
\]

may become rank two. Fatal locally unless this mismatch is not a tangent but fixed basepoint.

So distinguish:

- fixed offset: safe;
- tangent graph slope: contributes to \(B_Y\);
- second independent tangent slope: fatal.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y^N\text{ is safe if zero/fixed/finite coherent; dangerous if it gives a new local tangent slope.}
}
\]

---

# 4. Third audit: whitening defect \(\mathfrak C_Y^W\)

This is the hard one.

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^W
=
\Pi_Y
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}[D^2N_{0,\sing}]
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}[D^2N_{0,\tr}]
\right).
\]

## Test W1: Is same-point whitening transported?

If whitening is transported as part of the labelled carrier, then

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^W=0.
\]

## Test W2: If whitening is recomputed, are origin pairs preserved?

Moving whitening creates terms like

\[
D\mathcal W[\xi]\,DN_0[\eta],
\]

or

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\xi,\eta]N_0.
\]

These are not

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi,\eta]].
\]

If the origin-pair quotient is faithful, they are killed by \(\Pi_Y\).

## Test W3: If recomputed whitening changes the exact \(D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0]\) coefficient, is it coherent?

If the coefficient is a fixed/low-order function, it contributes to:

\[
\eta_{\fixed}
\]

or

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}.
\]

If it can vary freely patch by patch, fatal.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y^W\text{ is the main local-freedom risk, but only if same-point whitening is recomputed with local degrees of freedom.}
}
\]

---

# 5. Practical decision table

\[
\begin{array}{c|c|c|c}
\text{defect} & \text{structural zero test} & \text{safe nonzero case} & \text{fatal case}\\
\hline
\mathfrak C_Y^L
&
D^2L=0\text{ or origin killed}
&
\text{fixed chart curvature}
&
\text{locally free }D^2L
\\
\mathfrak C_Y^N
&
D^2N_0\text{ natural}
&
\text{fixed/finite coherent mismatch}
&
\text{new independent tangent slope}
\\
\mathfrak C_Y^W
&
\text{whitening transported/origin killed}
&
\text{fixed/finite coherent whitening mismatch}
&
\text{locally free same-point whitening}
\end{array}
\]

This table should be the actual audit checklist.

---

# 6. Effect on \(B_Y\)

The only components that should enter \(B_Y\) are local tangent/graph contributions.

Thus:

\[
B_Y
=
B_Y^{\mathrm{connection}}
+
B_Y^{E_2,\mathrm{local}}
+
B_Y^{\mathfrak C,\mathrm{local}}.
\]

For \(\mathfrak C_Y\):

- fixed offsets do **not** enter \(B_Y\);
- finite coherent amplitudes do **not** enter \(B_Y\) as local tangent directions unless they are locally free;
- graph-like local tangent terms enter \(B_Y\);
- vertical local tangent terms are fatal.

So:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y\text{ should enter }B_Y\text{ only if it represents actual local tangent freedom.}
}
\]

This prevents over-enlarging the fiber model.

---

# 7. Effect on \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\)

Finite coherent commutator terms enter:

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
\subset
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
\]

Then amplitude Schur handles them.

So the actual coherent amplitude space is:

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
=
\mathcal C_Y^{E_2}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\param}
+\cdots.
\]

The commutator contributes only if one of its defects is finite coherent.

If all defects are zero or residual-small, then:

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}=0.
\]

---

# 8. Effect on \(\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{Y12}\)

Residual-controlled defects enter:

\[
R_X^Y.
\]

The required estimate is:

\[
\sum_p
|\Pi_YR_X^Y(p)|^2
\le
Q^{-B}\mu.
\]

The residual part should include:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^{\mathrm{res}},
\quad
R_{\trunc}^Y,
\quad
R_{\tail}^Y.
\]

This is much weaker than full \(K_9\)-completion.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{Y12}
\text{ only needs to control the components not structurally killed or finite coherent.}
}
\]

---

# 9. Recommended finite theorem

## CommutatorAudit\(_Y\)

Assume:

1. \(D^2L=0\) or \(\Pi_YDN_0[D^2L]=0\) or finite coherent.
2. \(D^2N_0\) is natural under \((L,S)\), or its defect is fixed/finite coherent.
3. Same-point whitening is transported, or its mismatch is origin-killed/fixed/finite coherent.
4. No commutator component creates a locally free vertical \(Y12\) amplitude.
5. No commutator component creates a second independent graph slope in \(B_Y\).

Then labelled transition is compatible with the nested \(Y12\) Schur tower. It may enlarge:

\[
\eta_{\fixed},
\quad
\rho_Y,
\quad
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith},
\quad
R_X^Y,
\]

but does not kill the route.

---

# 10. Final status

The commutator audit is now operational and ordered.

Test first:

\[
D^2L=0
\quad\text{or}\quad
\Pi_YDN_0[D^2L]=0.
\]

Then:

\[
D^2N_0\text{ naturality}.
\]

Then:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}\text{ transport}.
\]

Only if a defect is both \(Y12\)-visible and locally free does the route die.

This is the sharpest version of the labelled-transition audit so far.

## Agent 4 continuation — reducing \(\mathfrak C_Y\) to two finite yes/no audits

The commutator audit now has an ordered checklist:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L
\rightarrow
\mathfrak C_Y^N
\rightarrow
\mathfrak C_Y^W.
\]

I will now compress it further. The latest reduction shows that most apparent transition defects are harmless unless they create **locally free primitive \(Y12\)-amplitude** or a **second independent fiber slope**. 

The practical question becomes:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Can }\mathfrak C_Y\text{ create either a new local }(0,1)\text{ direction or a second slope }(1,\rho')?
}
\]

If no, labelled transition is safe.

---

# 1. The two decisive audits

The full commutator is

\[
\mathfrak C_Y
=
\mathfrak C_Y^N+\mathfrak C_Y^L+\mathfrak C_Y^W.
\]

But for the nested Schur tower, we only need two finite checks.

## Audit A: fiber effect

Project the commutator into the local fiber:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathfrak C_Y
\subset \mathbb C^2_{(s_7,s_9^Y)}.
\]

The acceptable outcomes are:

\[
0,
\qquad
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),
\qquad
\text{fixed offset}.
\]

The bad outcomes are:

\[
\mathbb C(0,1),
\qquad
\mathbb C(1,\rho')\text{ with }\rho'\neq\rho_Y,
\qquad
\mathbb C^2.
\]

So the finite condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathfrak C_Y
\subseteq
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y)
\quad\text{or is fixed/non-tangent}.
}
\]

## Audit B: amplitude effect

Project the commutator into \(Y12\)-amplitude functions over patches:

\[
\Pi_{\amp,Y}\mathfrak C_Y.
\]

The acceptable outcomes are:

\[
0,
\qquad
\text{fixed},
\qquad
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}\text{ finite coherent},
\qquad
\text{residual-controlled}.
\]

The fatal outcome is extraction-local freedom:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\amp,Y}\mathfrak C_Y
\text{ contains atom-local }Y12\text{ bump amplitudes.}
}
\]

Thus the labelled transition theorem can be stated as:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y\text{ is safe iff its fiber projection is at most the existing graph and its amplitude projection is not locally free.}
}
\]

---

# 2. Why \(\mathfrak C_Y^L\) is probably harmless

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L
=
\Pi_YDN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)].
\]

There are two strong reasons this should vanish.

First, if the source-slot transition \(L\) is linear/affine on the relevant mixed-12 source variables, then

\[
D^2L=0.
\]

Second, even if \(D^2L\neq0\), the origin label is

\[
DN_0\circ D^2L,
\]

not

\[
D^2N_0.
\]

Since the \(Y12\) quotient tracks

\[
o_N=D^2N_0,
\]

we expect

\[
\Pi_YDN_0[D^2L]=0.
\]

Therefore the only bad case is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{the quotient forgets origin, or }D^2L\text{ is itself generated by primitive }D^2N_0\text{ data.}
}
\]

That is a quotient/design problem, not an analytic one.

---

# 3. Why \(\mathfrak C_Y^N\) is a finite tensor identity

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N
=
\Pi_Y
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
S\,D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right).
\]

This is a finite tensorial naturality check.

If \(N_0\) is the same normal form in both charts and \(S\) is the induced carrier transition, then

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
=
S\,D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2],
\]

so

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N=0.
\]

If this identity fails by a fixed chart-dependent scalar, then it contributes at most a finite coherent amplitude or fixed offset.

The dangerous case would be a patchwise variable tensor mismatch:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
S\,D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
=
\eta_p\,Y,
\]

with arbitrary \(\eta_p\). That would be locally free.

So the tensor audit is:

\[
\boxed{
D^2N_0\text{ naturality is either exact or finite coherent; not patchwise free.}
}
\]

---

# 4. Why \(\mathfrak C_Y^W\) is the real danger

The whitening term is subtle because it can create degree-two-looking expressions:

\[
D\mathcal W[\xi_1]\cdot DN_0[\xi_2],
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\xi_1,\xi_2]\cdot N_0.
\]

These are not primitive \(Y12\), because their origin-pairs are not

\[
(o_N,o_W)=(D^2N_0,D\mathcal W_{\same}).
\]

The true \(Y12\) term is

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}\bigl[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]\bigr].
\]

Therefore if the quotient remembers origin-pair labels, then moving-whitening cross terms vanish in the \(Y12\) quotient.

The dangerous case is not “whitening moves.” The dangerous case is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{same-point whitening is recomputed in a way that creates arbitrary coefficients of }
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0].
}
\]

So the whitening audit is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y\left(D\mathcal W[\xi]DN_0[\eta]+D^2\mathcal W[\xi,\eta]N_0\right)=0
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\text{any mismatch in }D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0]\text{ is fixed or finite coherent.}
}
\]

---

# 5. A useful sufficient theorem

A compact sufficient theorem is:

## OriginPairFunctoriality

The labelled transition and singular realization satisfy:

1. \(L\) is affine on mixed-12 source slots, or \(DN_0\circ D^2L\) has no \(D^2N_0\)-origin projection.

2. \(D^2N_0\) is transported tensorially up to finite coherent chart data.

3. Same-point whitening satisfies

\[
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}
=
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}S
\]

on the \(D^2N_0\)-origin carrier, up to finite coherent chart data.

4. The nuisance quotient is homogeneous for the origin-pair filtration.

Then:

\[
\Pi_Y\mathfrak C_Y
\in
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
+
\mathrm{Residual}_{Y12},
\]

with no locally free contribution.

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}=0\) and the residual is small, labelled transition is completely safe.

---

# 6. How this affects the current downstream branches

## If \(\mathfrak C_Y=0\)

Then labelled transition introduces no new \(Y12\) freedom. The route is governed only by \(B_7\) and \(E_2^Y\).

## If \(\mathfrak C_Y\) is finite coherent

Then

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

is enlarged:

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\leftarrow
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}+\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}.
\]

This pushes us to amplitude Schur, but does not kill the route.

## If \(\mathfrak C_Y\) is graph-like

Then it modifies

\[
\rho_Y.
\]

We must rerun

\[
\mathrm{CoeffLow}_{Y12}
\]

for the updated \(\rho_Y\).

## If \(\mathfrak C_Y\) is locally free

Then the rank-one \(Y12\) channel dies unless a rank-two quotient survives outside this commutator image.

---

# 7. The precise no-go

The labelled transition repair fails only if:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y\mathfrak C_Y
\text{ contains arbitrary atom-scale amplitudes in }D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0].
}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\mathfrak C_Y
\]

creates a local free \(Y12\) vertical direction or a local free amplitude space.

This is much narrower than “transition recomputes the normal form.”

---

# 8. Final operational audit

The next finite computation should be:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathfrak C_Y
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\amp,Y}\mathfrak C_Y.
}
\]

Classify each as:

\[
0,\quad
\text{existing graph},\quad
\text{new graph},\quad
\text{vertical},\quad
\text{finite coherent},\quad
\text{residual},\quad
\text{locally free}.
\]

Then route:

- \(0\): ignore.
- existing graph: update \(\rho_Y\).
- new graph: rank-two/fiber failure unless dependent.
- vertical: local no-go.
- finite coherent: amplitude Schur.
- residual: \(EFCompletion_{Y12}\).
- locally free: no-go.

---

## Final status

The commutator \(\mathfrak C_Y\) is no longer vague. It is a routing problem into the nested Schur tower.

The key expected result is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y^L=0,\qquad
\mathfrak C_Y^N=0,\qquad
\mathfrak C_Y^W=0
}
\]

up to finite coherent/residual terms, because labelled transition should preserve the origin-pair filtration. If this expectation holds, central O2/O3 enrichment and \(Y12\)-normality are compatible.

under fixed-\(\alpha\) downstream.

# 2. Actual parameter first-variation space

Under fixed-\(\alpha\), define the actual parameter set as:

\theta\in

where:

- \(\xi_2\) is the second mixed branch slot;

\[
V_1=P_1V_{\arith},

\[

The expected reason for vanishing is that fixed-\(\alpha\) actual parameters are diagonal/source-linear deformations of existing EF atoms.

## \(m\)-variation

\[
\partial_m

changes phase and local center. It differentiates existing EF atoms, producing log-frequency factors, but it does not introduce an independent mixed-source pair \((\xi_1,\xi_2)\) plus a same-point whitening variation.

\[
P_1\partial_m\xi,\quad P_2\partial_m\xi,\quad P_W\partial_m\xi

are all tied to the same diagonal source direction. They do not span the independent mixed-12 Hessian slots.

\[

changes scale/windowing. Again, this is diagonal/coordinate variation. It does not create primitive

\[

in two independent mixed branch slots.

These adjust representation, gauge, or coordinate basis. They are source-linear or chart-origin, not primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin.

These are gamma/background-origin variations. They do not have mixed-12 primitive source-Hessian origin.

This is not fatal. It modifies or supports the same graph slope.

But this requires actual \(s_7\)-motion. If \(s_7\) is D4-only and not actual-source, then \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\) should not generate a graph line through \(s_7\).

So a graph-like \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\) output is possible only after the actual-\(s_7\)-tangent audit.

---

# 7. Failure modes

\[
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}

becomes dangerous if:

1. actual parameters span the whitening slot \(a_Y\);
2. actual parameters span both mixed source slots \(\xi_1,\xi_2\);
3. the same parameter family couples these slots with the correct same-point relation;
4. the resulting fiber vector is vertical or a second slope.

The specific fatal cases are:

\[

or

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\]

contains

(1,\rho')
\quad\text{with}\quad

## FixedAlphaSecondCurvatureY12Vanishing

\quad\text{or}\quad

\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0.
}

## Agent 4 continuation — diagonal variation exclusion as a branch-polarization rank test

No nomenclature changes. I keep:

\[
\rho_Y=\text{lower }Y12\text{ graph slope},\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

I continue the fixed-\(\alpha\) audit of

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}.
\]

The target remains:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0
}
\]

unless actual-source parameter curvature genuinely spans the primitive mixed-12 same-point one-whitening \(Y12\) slot. This is part of the current downstream decision tree: determine whether \(B_Y=0\), \(B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\), vertical, or rank two. 

---

# 1. Branch-polarization model

Let the source slot space near the fold decompose as

\[
U=U_1\oplus U_2\oplus U_0,
\]

where:

- \(U_1\) is the plus/main branch slot;
- \(U_2\) is the folded-dual branch slot;
- \(U_0\) is background/gamma/completion.

The primitive \(Y12\) carrier requires the mixed bilinear projection

\[
D^2N_0[U_1,U_2]
\]

together with one same-point whitening slot:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[\cdot].
\]

Thus the dangerous component is

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[a]\,
D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2],
\qquad
a\in V_W,\quad \xi_1\in U_1,\quad \xi_2\in U_2.
\]

Actual second curvature can see \(Y12\) only if actual first variations simultaneously populate:

\[
V_W\ni a_Y,
\qquad
V_1\ni \xi_1,
\qquad
V_2\ni \xi_2.
\]

This is the slot-rank test.

---

# 2. Diagonal actual variations do not polarize mixed-12

For fixed-\(\alpha\), ordinary actual variations are generated by operations like:

\[
\partial_m,\quad \partial_\lambda,\quad
\partial_{\mathrm{phase}},\quad
\partial_{\mathrm{norm}},\quad
\partial_{\mathrm{chart}},\quad
\partial_{\gamma/\mathrm{bg}}.
\]

These act diagonally on the EF packet. A typical first variation has the form

\[
v_\theta
=
a_\theta^{(1)}u_1
+
a_\theta^{(2)}u_2
+
a_\theta^{(0)}u_0,
\]

where the coefficients are phase, scale, cutoff, or gamma derivatives of the already-existing atoms.

This is source-linear. It does not create two independent source perturbations

\[
(\xi_1,\xi_2)\in U_1\times U_2
\]

with an independent same-point whitening variation. The same parameter that moves branch \(1\) also moves branch \(2\) according to the fixed AFE geometry.

So the branch-polarized span is diagonal:

\[
\{(a_\theta^{(1)},a_\theta^{(2)})\}_\theta
\subset
\text{a low-dimensional diagonal/graph subspace}.
\]

To generate \(Y12\), one needs independent mixed polarization:

\[
\operatorname{rank}
\begin{pmatrix}
P_1v_{\theta_i} & P_1v_{\theta_j}\\
P_2v_{\theta_i} & P_2v_{\theta_j}
\end{pmatrix}
\neq0.
\]

For ordinary fixed-\(\alpha\) variations, the expected determinant vanishes or has the wrong origin label.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
D^2N_0[V_{\arith},V_{\arith}]
\text{ has no primitive mixed-12 same-point }Y12\text{ component.}
}
\]

---

# 3. Same-point whitening slot is also missing

Even if diagonal parameter variation produces a mixed branch term formally, \(Y12\) also requires the whitening slot:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[a_Y].
\]

For ordinary parameters:

- \(m\)-variation changes center/phase;
- \(\lambda\)-variation changes scale/window;
- phase variation multiplies EF atoms;
- chart/normalization variation changes coordinates or basis;
- gamma/background variation changes nonmixed background factors.

None of these is naturally the same-point whitening variation attached to the promoted \(Y12\) carrier.

So even if

\[
D^2N_0[V_1,V_2]
\]

were nonzero, we still expect:

\[
a_Y\notin V_W.
\]

This gives the missing-slot proof:

\[
\boxed{
D\mathcal W_{\same}[V_W]\,
D^2N_0[V_1,V_2]
\cap \mathbf C[\mathsf C_{Y12}]
=
0.
}
\]

---

# 4. Explicit parameter audit

## \(m\)-variation

\[
\partial_m e^{-im\log n}
=
-i\log n\,e^{-im\log n}.
\]

This inserts a log-frequency multiplier into existing atoms. It is source-linear and diagonal. It does not create an independent pair

\[
(\xi_1,\xi_2)
\]

nor a same-point whitening derivative.

So:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\partial_m^2 u=0.
\]

## \(\lambda\)-variation

\[
\partial_\lambda
\]

rescales the local window and differentiated weights. This changes the coordinate representation of both branches. It is again source-linear/coordinate-origin.

So:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\partial_\lambda^2u=0,
\qquad
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\partial_m\partial_\lambda u=0.
\]

## Phase/gauge/normalization

These are multiplicative or basis-change variations. They can create lower connection or graph-like changes, but not primitive

\[
D^2N_0
\]

mixed-source Hessian origin.

Thus they are killed by the origin-pair filter.

## Background/gamma

Gamma/background terms have branch type \(0\) or nonmixed origin. They do not carry mixed branch \(12\) primitive source-Hessian origin.

So:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\gamma/\mathrm{bg},9}=0.
\]

---

# 5. Fixed-\(\alpha\) theorem

Under fixed-\(\alpha\), exclude \(\alpha\) from the actual parameter set:

\[
\alpha\notin T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.
\]

Then the slot-rank spaces are generated only by ordinary diagonal/source-linear variations. Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0.
}
\]

This keeps the second-order actual-source curvature from enlarging \(B_Y\).

Consequently, for the \(B_Y\)-classification, \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\) contributes neither:

\[
(0,1),
\]

nor

\[
(1,\rho'),
\]

nor a rank-two local image.

So the remaining \(B_Y\) sources are lower actual-source \(s_7\)-motion, local \(E_2^Y\), labelled-transition commutators, and nuisance quotient artifacts.

---

# 6. If \(\alpha\) is admitted as actual source deformation

If \(\alpha\) becomes an actual source parameter, rerun the slot-rank test with

\[
v_\alpha=\partial_\alpha \xi.
\]

There are three cases.

## Safe \(\alpha\)

If \(\alpha\) only changes phase/weight/log-frequency:

\[
v_\alpha
\]

is diagonal/source-linear. Then:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}^{(\alpha)}=0.
\]

## Graph-like \(\alpha\)

If \(\alpha\)-curvature contributes through \(s_7\) and continues by the same slope:

\[
s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7,
\]

then it enlarges \(B_Y\) but only graph-like. This forces rank-one but is not fatal.

## Fatal \(\alpha\)

If \(\alpha\) independently varies \(N_0\), same-point whitening, or both mixed source slots, then it can produce vertical or second-slope \(Y12\). Then current \(Y12\)-normality may fail.

Thus policy:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Use }\alpha\text{ for O2/O3 proof-thickening only unless its slot-rank audit is safe.}
}
\]

---

# 7. Relation to rank-two D4

This result improves the rank-two prospect.

If

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0
\]

and

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\par}=0,
\]

then actual-source second curvature and first tangents do not put any \(Y12\)-fiber direction into \(B_Y\).

Then \(B_Y\) depends mainly on whether \(s_7\) is actual-source motion and whether \(E_2^Y\) or labelled commutators create local fiber freedom.

So the route to rank two becomes:

\[
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0,
\]

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2^{Y,\local}=0,
\]

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathfrak C_Y^{\local}=0.
\]

If those also hold, then:

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

and raw D4 remains rank two.

---

# 8. Failure test as determinant

If actual variations \(v_i,v_j\) are suspected to create mixed \(Y12\), compute the branch determinant:

\[
\Delta_{12}(v_i,v_j)
=
\det
\begin{pmatrix}
P_1v_i & P_1v_j\\
P_2v_i & P_2v_j
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

If

\[
\Delta_{12}=0
\]

for all actual variation pairs, then actual second curvature cannot generate independent mixed branch \(12\).

Then also check whitening:

\[
P_Wv_k\not\ni a_Y.
\]

If either determinant or whitening slot fails, then:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0.
\]

This gives a finite computational test.

---

# 9. Final theorem

## FixedAlphaSecondCurvatureY12Vanishing

Assume:

1. \(\alpha\) is not an actual-source parameter in the downstream D4 model.
2. Actual first variations from \(m,\lambda,\) phase, normalization, chart, and background/gamma are diagonal/source-linear.
3. For all actual variation pairs,

\[
\Delta_{12}(v_i,v_j)=0
\]

or the same-point whitening slot \(a_Y\) is absent.
4. Origin-pair filtration kills chart/gamma/source-linear curvature terms.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0.
}
\]

If \(\alpha\) is admitted, the same theorem holds only after including \(v_\alpha\) in the determinant/slot-rank audit.

---

## Final status

For fixed-\(\alpha\), actual EF second curvature is expected to vanish in the primitive \(Y12\) fiber.

This means \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\) should not obstruct rank-two D4. The next decisive question remains:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0\quad\text{or not?}
}
\]

If \(s_7\) is not actual-source motion and if \(E_2^Y,\mathfrak C_Y\) are nonlocal/residual, then \(B_Y=0\) and rank-two D4 is viable.

## Agent 4 report — fixed-\(\alpha\) actual curvature and \(B_Y\)

No nomenclature changes. I use

\[
\rho_Y=\text{lower }Y12\text{ graph slope},
\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},
\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The current task is to prove or disprove, under fixed-\(\alpha\) downstream,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(\mathcal T_{\par}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
}
\]

This is one necessary input for the rank-two branch \(B_Y=0\). The attached prompt emphasizes the corrected fork: formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection does not enter \(B_Y\) unless there is actual \(s_7\)-motion. 

---

# 1. Target

The local \(Y12\) fiber is

\[
(s_7,s_9^Y).
\]

The projections are

\[
\Pi_{s_7}(v)=\ell_7(v),
\qquad
\Pi_{s_9^Y}(v)=\ell_9^Y(v).
\]

For the rank-two route, we need fixed-\(\alpha\) actual first tangents and second curvature to contribute neither coordinate:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{\par})=0,
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
}
\]

The expected result is zero, not merely graph-like. A graph contribution \(\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\) would still collapse rank two to rank one.

---

# 2. Fixed-\(\alpha\) actual parameter set

For fixed-\(\alpha\) downstream, use actual parameters:

\[
m,\quad \lambda,\quad \text{phase},\quad \text{normalization},\quad \text{chart},\quad \text{background/gamma}.
\]

Do **not** include \(\alpha\) if it is only an O2/O3 proof-thickening parameter.

This is important because including \(\alpha\) as actual-source motion can enlarge

\[
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
\]

and may create \(s_7\) or \(Y12\)-fiber directions. Under fixed-\(\alpha\), it should not enter \(B_Y\).

---

# 3. First tangents \(\mathcal T_{\par}\)

The actual first tangents are source-linear or representation-linear.

## \(m\)-variation

\[
\partial_m e^{-im\log n}
=
-i\log n\,e^{-im\log n}.
\]

This changes the phase/log-frequency multiplier of existing EF atoms. It does not create the live \(C_7\) carrier class, and it does not create primitive

\[
(D^2N_0,D\mathcal W_{\same})
\]

\(Y12\)-origin.

Thus expected:

\[
\ell_7(\partial_m q''_{\can})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\partial_m q''_{\can})=0.
\]

## \(\lambda\)-variation

Scale/window variation changes local coordinates and weights. It is chart/source-linear, not a primitive D4 carrier or primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin \(Y12\) source-Hessian.

Thus:

\[
\ell_7(\partial_\lambda q''_{\can})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\partial_\lambda q''_{\can})=0.
\]

## Phase/normalization/chart

These are gauge or coordinate changes. They may affect ordinary Taylor coefficients, but \(s_7\) is not a generic order-seven coefficient. It is the live \(C_7\) carrier class.

So the needed distinction is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{generic order-seven Taylor variation}\ne s_7\text{ live carrier motion.}
}
\]

Expected:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\mathrm{phase/norm/chart}})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{\mathrm{phase/norm/chart}})=0.
\]

## Background/gamma

Gamma/background variations have background origin. They do not carry mixed branch-12 primitive source-Hessian \(Y12\) and should not carry \(C_7\) D4 live class.

Expected:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\gamma/\mathrm{bg}})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{\gamma/\mathrm{bg}})=0.
\]

Therefore the first-tangent theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\par}=0.
}
\]

---

# 4. Second curvature \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\)

Actual second curvature can create primitive \(Y12\) only if it fills all required slots:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2].
\]

Let

\[
V_{\arith}
=
\operatorname{span}\{\partial_\theta \xi\}
\]

for fixed-\(\alpha\) actual parameters, and define slot projections:

\[
V_1=P_1V_{\arith},
\qquad
V_2=P_2V_{\arith},
\qquad
V_W=P_WV_{\arith}.
\]

The dangerous membership is

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\in
D\mathcal W_{\same}[V_W]D^2N_0[V_1,V_2].
\]

Equivalently, actual first variations must supply:

\[
a_Y\in V_W,\qquad \xi_1\in V_1,\qquad \xi_2\in V_2.
\]

For fixed-\(\alpha\) ordinary variations, this should fail. They are diagonal/source-linear and do not independently polarize the two mixed branch slots plus same-point whitening.

So:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
}
\]

For \(s_7\), actual second curvature is also not expected to produce the live \(C_7\) D4 carrier class. It may produce lower-order Taylor changes, but not the live \(C_7\) coordinate.

So:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
}
\]

Hence:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0.
}
\]

---

# 5. Branch-polarization determinant test

If any pair of actual variations \(v_i,v_j\) is suspected of producing mixed branch \(12\), compute

\[
\Delta_{12}(v_i,v_j)
=
\det
\begin{pmatrix}
P_1v_i & P_1v_j\\
P_2v_i & P_2v_j
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

If

\[
\Delta_{12}(v_i,v_j)=0
\]

for all fixed-\(\alpha\) actual variation pairs, then they cannot generate independent mixed branch \(12\) Hessian.

Then also check whitening:

\[
a_Y\notin V_W.
\]

If either the branch determinant vanishes or the same-point whitening slot is absent, then:

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
\]

This gives a finite computational criterion.

---

# 6. \(\alpha\)-fork

If \(\alpha\) is admitted as an actual-source deformation, repeat the slot-rank test with

\[
v_\alpha=\partial_\alpha \xi.
\]

Three possible outcomes:

1. **Safe \(\alpha\):** \(v_\alpha\) is source-linear/diagonal; still no \(Y12\) slot rank.
2. **Graph-like \(\alpha\):** contributes \(\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\), collapsing rank two to rank one.
3. **Fatal \(\alpha\):** contributes vertical or second slope, killing \(Y12\).

For the current fixed-\(\alpha\) downstream, do not include it.

---

# 7. Consequence for rank two

If

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\par}=0
\]

and

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0,
\]

then first tangents and actual second curvature do not obstruct rank two.

The remaining \(B_Y\) sources are:

\[
\mathcal T_7^{\arith},
\quad
\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith},
\quad
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local},
\quad
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local},
\quad
\mathcal N_{\sing}.
\]

In particular, if \(C_7\) is D4-only and not actual-source motion, and if \(E_2^Y,\mathfrak C_Y,\mathcal N_{\sing}\) do not contribute locally, then

\[
B_Y=0
\]

and rank-two D4 survives.

---

# 8. Failure modes

This theorem fails if:

1. \(s_7\) is actually generated by an ordinary actual parameter, meaning \(\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par})\ne0\).
2. actual second curvature fills the primitive \(Y12\) slots, meaning

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\in
D\mathcal W_{\same}[V_W]D^2N_0[V_1,V_2].
\]

3. \(\alpha\) is treated as actual and supplies the missing slots.
4. chart/normalization tangents are defined too broadly and include formal carrier coordinates.
5. the nuisance quotient \(\mathcal N_{\sing}\) identifies generic Taylor or chart variations with live \(C_7\) or \(Y12\).

These must be excluded by definition or finite audit.

---

# Final theorem

## FixedAlphaNoActualFiberCurvature

Under fixed-\(\alpha\), assume:

1. actual first parameters are \(m,\lambda,\) phase, normalization, chart, and background/gamma only;
2. these are source-linear/diagonal or chart/background-origin;
3. the live coordinates \((s_7,s_9^Y)\) detect only the live \(C_7\) carrier class and primitive \(D^2N_0/D\mathcal W_{\same}\) \(Y12\) class, not generic Taylor coefficients;
4. the slot-rank condition for primitive \(Y12\) fails for actual first variations.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(\mathcal T_{\par}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
}
\]

This supports the rank-two branch by removing actual first tangents and second curvature from \(B_Y\).

## Agent 4 continuation — why generic order-seven jets do not imply \(s_7\)-motion

I continue the fixed-\(\alpha\) audit of

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(\mathcal T_{\par}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}).
\]

The remaining subtlety is this:

\[
\boxed{
\text{actual parameters may create order-seven Taylor coefficients, but not the live }s_7\text{ carrier.}
}
\]

This distinction is essential for rank two. The latest coordinator update makes clear that rank two remains viable precisely if \(C_7\) is D4-only and not actual-source motion. 

---

# 1. \(s_7\) must be carrier-class sensitive

The projection

\[
\ell_7
\]

cannot be “take the order-seven coefficient.” It must be a covector on the live \(C_7\) carrier quotient.

So the right condition is not

\[
[z^7]\,\partial_\theta q''_{\can}=0.
\]

That would be too strong and probably false.

The right condition is

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0
}
\]

where \(\ell_7\) kills:

\[
\text{source-linear order-seven Taylor drift},
\quad
\text{chart re-expansion},
\quad
\text{gamma/background order-seven terms},
\quad
\text{normalization/gauge order-seven terms}.
\]

It sees only the live \(C_7\) D4 carrier class.

Thus an actual parameter can change ordinary order-seven jets without creating \(s_7\)-motion.

---

# 2. Carrier-origin filter for \(s_7\)

Define a carrier-origin signature for the \(s_7\) class:

\[
\Sigma(C_7)
=
(\text{D4 clean carrier origin},\ \text{order }7,\ \text{correct branch/parity/live class}).
\]

Then require:

\[
\ell_7(v)=0
\]

unless

\[
v
\]

has signature \(\Sigma(C_7)\).

Fixed-\(\alpha\) actual parameters have origins:

\[
\text{EF-linear},
\quad
\text{chart/coordinate},
\quad
\text{gamma/background},
\quad
\text{normalization/gauge}.
\]

These are not the D4 clean carrier origin.

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par})=0
}
\]

provided the quotient preserves the \(C_7\)-carrier origin.

This is analogous to the \(Y12\) origin-pair protection:

\[
(o_N,o_W)=(D^2N_0,D\mathcal W_{\same}).
\]

For \(s_7\), we need a corresponding \(C_7\)-origin label.

---

# 3. Why \(m\)- and \(\lambda\)-variations should vanish under \(\ell_7\)

An \(m\)-variation differentiates EF atoms:

\[
\partial_m e^{-im\log n}
=
-i\log n\,e^{-im\log n}.
\]

A \(\lambda\)-variation rescales the local packet and changes weights.

Both can create order-seven terms after local expansion. But they remain reparametrizations of the actual EF source. They are not D4 clean carrier insertions.

So in the carrier quotient:

\[
\ell_7(\partial_m q''_{\can})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_7(\partial_\lambda q''_{\can})=0.
\]

If either is nonzero, then the quotient is too coarse: it is confusing ordinary re-expansion drift with the live \(C_7\) carrier.

That would artificially force rank one by putting actual \(s_7\)-motion into \(B_Y\).

---

# 4. Actual second curvature and \(s_7\)

Second curvature terms such as

\[
\partial_m^2q''_{\can},
\quad
\partial_m\partial_\lambda q''_{\can},
\quad
\partial_\lambda^2q''_{\can}
\]

can also produce high-order Taylor coefficients.

But they remain EF-parameter curvature, not D4 \(C_7\)-carrier origin.

Thus the intended theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
}
\]

This is independent from the \(Y12\) slot-rank test. For \(s_9^Y\), we need to rule out primitive mixed-12 source Hessian plus same-point whitening. For \(s_7\), we need to rule out live \(C_7\)-carrier origin.

So the fixed-\(\alpha\) second-curvature audit has two filters:

\[
\ell_7:\quad \text{kill non-}C_7\text{-origin order-seven drift},
\]

\[
\ell_9^Y:\quad \text{kill non-primitive }Y12\text{ origin}.
\]

---

# 5. Nuisance quotient danger

A major hidden risk is nuisance overcollapse.

If

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

identifies ordinary source-linear order-seven drift with \(C_7\), then

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par})
\]

may appear nonzero. But this would be a quotient-design artifact, not genuine actual-source \(s_7\)-motion.

Therefore the singular quotient must be filtered not only for \(Y12\) but also for the \(C_7\) live class:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}\text{ must not identify generic order-seven drift with live }C_7.
}
\]

If it does, rank two may be falsely destroyed.

So the rank-two route requires two quotient-faithfulness conditions:

\[
\text{\(Y12\)-origin faithfulness},
\]

and

\[
\text{\(C_7\)-carrier faithfulness}.
\]

---

# 6. Practical finite test for \(\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par})=0\)

For each actual first tangent \(v_\theta\), compute its projection into the order-seven singular carrier space:

\[
[v_\theta]_7.
\]

Then decompose:

\[
[v_\theta]_7
=
a_\theta C_7
+
n_\theta,
\]

where \(n_\theta\) is nuisance/source-linear/chart/background.

The test is:

\[
\boxed{
a_\theta=0
}
\]

for all actual first tangents.

Equivalently:

\[
\ell_7(v_\theta)=0.
\]

But \(a_\theta\) must be computed in the carrier quotient, not in raw Taylor coordinates.

A generic raw order-seven coefficient is irrelevant unless its carrier-class projection onto \(C_7\) is nonzero.

---

# 7. Practical finite test for \(\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0\)

For each pair of actual parameters \((\theta_i,\theta_j)\), compute:

\[
[\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can}]_7.
\]

Project to the \(C_7\)-carrier class:

\[
[\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can}]_7
=
a_{ij}C_7+n_{ij}.
\]

Need:

\[
\boxed{
a_{ij}=0
}
\]

for all fixed-\(\alpha\) actual parameter pairs.

Again, raw order-seven terms do not matter; only the live carrier-class component matters.

---

# 8. Consequence for \(B_Y\)

If both tests hold:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0,
\]

and the earlier \(Y12\)-slot tests give:

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{\par})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0,
\]

then:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(\mathcal T_{\par}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
}
\]

Thus actual first tangents and actual second curvature do not force rank one.

Then rank two remains possible unless another source contributes to \(B_Y\):

\[
\mathcal T_7^{\arith},
\quad
\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith},
\quad
E_2^Y,
\quad
\mathfrak C_Y,
\quad
\mathcal N_{\sing}.
\]

---

# 9. Failure cases

The rank-two route is threatened if any of the following occurs:

## A. Actual \(C_7\)-carrier motion

There exists an actual parameter \(\theta\) with

\[
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})\ne0.
\]

Then \(s_7\) is actual-source motion, and rank one may be forced.

## B. Actual second curvature in \(C_7\)

There exist actual parameters \(\theta_i,\theta_j\) with

\[
\ell_7(\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can})\ne0.
\]

Then actual-source curvature contributes to \(B_Y\).

## C. Quotient overcollapse

The quotient identifies generic order-seven EF drift with \(C_7\). This is repairable by refining the quotient, not necessarily a true arithmetic failure.

## D. \(\alpha\) promoted to actual tangent

If \(\alpha\) is included and

\[
\ell_7(\partial_\alpha q''_{\can})\ne0,
\]

then \(\alpha\) destroys the rank-two route unless it is kept as external proof-thickening.

---

# 10. Refined theorem

## FixedAlphaNoActualFiberCurvature, strengthened

Under fixed-\(\alpha\), assume:

1. \(\ell_7\) is a live \(C_7\)-carrier covector, not a raw order-seven coefficient.
2. \(\ell_9^Y\) is a primitive \(Y12\)-carrier covector.
3. The singular quotient is faithful to both \(C_7\)-carrier origin and \(Y12\)-origin.
4. Actual first tangents are EF-linear/chart/background/normalization variations.
5. Actual second curvatures are EF-parameter curvatures and not D4 carrier insertions.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(\mathcal T_{\par}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
}
\]

This supports \(B_Y=0\) and therefore the rank-two D4 branch.

---

## Final status

The key refinement is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{generic order-seven actual variation is not }s_7\text{ actual motion.}
}
\]

Only the live \(C_7\)-carrier class matters.

So the next concrete finite audit is:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual})=0
\quad\text{and}\quad
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0
\]

in the \(C_7\)-carrier quotient.

If this passes, fixed-\(\alpha\) actual first and second variations do not obstruct rank-two D4.

## Agent 4 continuation — making \(\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par})=0\) a quotient test, not a heuristic

The rank-two route now depends on proving that actual first/second parameter variations do **not** move the live \(s_7\) coordinate. The crucial distinction remains:

\[
\boxed{
\text{raw order-seven jet motion}\neq \text{live }C_7\text{-carrier motion}.
}
\]

The latest coordinator update explicitly says \(B_Y\) should include only actual-source/nuisance/local freedoms, not formal D4 carriers. So the question is not “does an actual parameter change order-seven Taylor coefficients?” It is:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\text{actual parameter variation})=0
\text{ in the live }C_7\text{ quotient.}
}
\]

This is the right finite test for whether \(s_7\) is actual-source motion or D4-only. 

---

# 1. Define the \(C_7\)-carrier quotient

Let \(\mathcal J_7\) be the local order-seven singular/transition carrier space. Decompose it by origin:

\[
\mathcal J_7
=
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{EF\text{-}lin}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{chart}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\gamma/\mathrm{bg}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{norm}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{D4}.
\]

The live \(C_7\) coordinate should live only in

\[
\mathcal J_7^{D4}
\]

or, more precisely, in the one-dimensional quotient class

\[
[C_7]\in
\mathcal J_7^{D4}/\mathcal N_7.
\]

Define

\[
\ell_7:
\mathcal J_7\to\mathbb C
\]

so that

\[
\ell_7(C_7)=1
\]

and

\[
\ell_7
\left(
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{EF\text{-}lin}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{chart}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\gamma/\mathrm{bg}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{norm}}
+
\mathcal N_7
\right)=0.
\]

Then the actual-source \(s_7\)-tangent test becomes:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0
\quad
\text{for every actual fixed-target parameter }\theta.
}
\]

This is now a quotient-origin theorem.

---

# 2. Carrier-origin criterion for first tangents

For a fixed-\(\alpha\) actual parameter

\[
\theta\in
\{m,\lambda,\mathrm{phase},\mathrm{norm},\mathrm{chart},\gamma/\mathrm{bg}\},
\]

the variation

\[
\partial_\theta q''_{\can}
\]

has one of these origins:

\[
\mathrm{EF\text{-}lin},
\quad
\mathrm{chart},
\quad
\gamma/\mathrm{bg},
\quad
\mathrm{norm}.
\]

It does not insert the D4 clean carrier \(C_7\). So if the quotient is faithful to the \(C_7\)-origin label, then

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0.
}
\]

This is structurally parallel to the \(Y12\)-origin filter:

\[
(o_N,o_W)=(D^2N_0,D\mathcal W_{\same}).
\]

For \(C_7\), we need the origin label:

\[
\Sigma(C_7)
=
(\mathrm{D4\ clean\ carrier},\ \mathrm{order}\ 7,\ \mathrm{live\ class}).
\]

Any first tangent lacking this label is killed.

---

# 3. Raw jet coefficient test is insufficient

A parameter such as \(m\) can generate raw seventh-order terms. For instance,

\[
\partial_m e^{-im\log n}
=
-i\log n\,e^{-im\log n},
\]

and expanding locally may contribute to order seven.

But this lies in

\[
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{EF\text{-}lin}},
\]

not in the D4 carrier line. Therefore:

\[
[z^7]\partial_m q''_{\can}\ne0
\quad\not\Rightarrow\quad
\ell_7(\partial_m q''_{\can})\ne0.
\]

The finite audit must project raw jets into the carrier quotient:

\[
[\partial_m q''_{\can}]_7
=
a_m C_7+n_m,
\qquad
n_m\in
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{EF\text{-}lin}}
+\mathcal N_7.
\]

The required statement is:

\[
\boxed{
a_m=0.
}
\]

Same for \(\lambda\), phase, normalization, chart, and gamma/background.

---

# 4. The same test for actual second curvature

For actual second curvature,

\[
\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can},
\]

raw order-seven terms are again possible. But their origin is still actual EF parameter curvature, chart curvature, or normalization curvature. They are not D4 clean carrier insertions.

So compute:

\[
[\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can}]_7
=
a_{ij}C_7+n_{ij}.
\]

Need:

\[
\boxed{
a_{ij}=0
}
\]

for all fixed-\(\alpha\) actual parameter pairs.

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
}
\]

The order subscript \(9\) in \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\) does not change the \(s_7\)-test: if the second-curvature effect has an order-seven component, it must still be projected to the \(C_7\)-carrier quotient.

---

# 5. A commutator formulation for \(C_7\)-faithfulness

The \(Y12\) labelled transition audit used a normal-form commutator. There is an analogous \(C_7\)-faithfulness commutator.

Let

\[
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}
\]

be raw transition and

\[
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}
\]

be labelled carrier transition. Define

\[
\mathfrak C_7(v)
=
\Pi_{C_7}
\left(
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}v
-
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}v
\right),
\]

where

\[
\Pi_{C_7}
\]

is the projection to the live \(C_7\) carrier quotient.

Need:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_7(v)=0
}
\]

for all source-linear/chart/background actual variations \(v\), or at worst fixed/finite coherent nonlocal terms. If \(\mathfrak C_7\) is locally free, it could falsely inject \(s_7\)-motion into \(B_Y\).

Thus rank-two requires both:

\[
Y12\text{-origin faithfulness}
\]

and

\[
C_7\text{-carrier faithfulness}.
\]

---

# 6. Nuisance quotient must not kill \(C_7\)

If

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

contains \(C_7\), then \(e_7\) is killed before D4 can use it. That would destroy rank two.

If \(\mathcal N_{\sing}\) identifies \(C_7\) with generic EF-linear order-seven drift, then actual parameters may falsely look like \(s_7\)-motion.

So require:

\[
\boxed{
C_7\notin \mathcal N_{\sing}
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}\text{ is filtered with respect to the }C_7\text{ carrier-origin label.}
}
\]

This is the \(C_7\)-analogue of the \(Y12\) origin-pair quotient-faithfulness requirement.

---

# 7. Practical rank-two audit for fixed-\(\alpha\)

The audit should be:

## Step 1: Build the \(C_7\)-carrier quotient

Construct

\[
\mathcal Q_7^{C}
=
\mathcal J_7/
\left(
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{EF\text{-}lin}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{chart}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\gamma/\mathrm{bg}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{norm}}
+
\mathcal N_7
\right).
\]

Check:

\[
[C_7]\ne0\quad\text{in }\mathcal Q_7^C.
\]

## Step 2: Define \(\ell_7\)

Choose a bounded covector with

\[
\ell_7(C_7)=1
\]

and killing the quotient denominator.

## Step 3: Test actual first tangents

For each fixed-\(\alpha\) actual parameter \(\theta\),

\[
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0.
\]

## Step 4: Test actual second curvature

For each pair \((\theta_i,\theta_j)\),

\[
\ell_7(\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can})=0.
\]

## Step 5: Test \(E_2^Y\) and labelled commutator local parts

Need:

\[
\ell_7(T_{E_2^Y}^{\local})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_7(T_{\lab,Y}^{\local})=0.
\]

If all pass, then:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

This is the first half of \(B_Y=0\).

---

# 8. Consequence if \(\ell_7\) vanishes on actual motion

If

\[
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0
\]

and also

\[
\Pi_{s_9^Y}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0,
\]

then

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

Then D4 remains rank two:

\[
M_{D4}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
c_7\alpha_9\ne0.
\]

The wall is vector-valued:

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Under coefficient conditioning, this is polynomially equivalent to:

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

So the rank-one moving wall and forced ODE trap are bypassed.

---

# 9. What would force rank one?

Rank one is forced only if there is actual-source \(s_7\)-motion:

\[
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\ne0,
\]

and its \(Y12\) continuation is graph-like:

\[
s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7.
\]

Then

\[
B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

and the surviving covector is:

\[
\ell_Y=\ell_9^Y-\rho_Y\ell_7.
\]

Only then do we need:

\[
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},
\]

the moving wall, and the trap hierarchy.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\text{rank-one is conditional on actual }s_7\text{-motion.}
}
\]

---

# Final status

The fixed-\(\alpha\) actual curvature audit supports rank two if \(\ell_7\) is truly a live \(C_7\)-carrier covector, not a raw order-seven coefficient.

The strongest current theorem to prove is:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual})=
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=
0.
}
\]

This requires \(C_7\)-carrier-origin faithfulness of the quotient. If it holds, ordinary actual parameters and their second curvature do not create \(s_7\)-motion, and rank-two D4 remains viable.

## Agent 4 continuation — \(C_7\)-carrier-origin faithfulness as the rank-two bottleneck

The rank-two route now hinges on a sharper quotient statement:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7\text{ must detect only the live }C_7\text{ carrier class, not raw order-seven source drift.}
}
\]

So the key theorem is no longer “actual parameters have no order-seven terms.” They probably do have such terms. The key theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{actual parameter order-seven drift lies in the denominator of the }C_7\text{ carrier quotient.}
}
\]

If this holds, then

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual})=
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0,
\]

and the rank-two branch remains viable.

---

# 1. Define the \(C_7\)-carrier quotient

Let \(\mathcal J_7\) be the realized singular/transition carrier space at order seven.

The quotient should be

\[
\mathcal Q_7^{C}
=
\mathcal J_7/
\mathcal D_7,
\]

where the denominator \(\mathcal D_7\) includes all coherent, source-linear, and nuisance order-seven directions:

\[
\mathcal D_7
=
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{EF\text{-}lin}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{chart}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\gamma/\mathrm{bg}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{norm}}
+
\mathcal N_7.
\]

Then

\[
[C_7]\in \mathcal Q_7^C
\]

is the live D4 carrier class.

The rank-two route needs:

\[
\boxed{
[C_7]\ne0\quad\text{in }\mathcal Q_7^C,
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
[\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual}]_7=0
\quad\text{in }\mathcal Q_7^C.
}
\]

Then a bounded covector \(\ell_7\) exists with

\[
\ell_7(C_7)=1,
\]

and

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal D_7)=0.
\]

---

# 2. Why actual first tangents should lie in \(\mathcal D_7\)

For any actual first parameter \(\theta\),

\[
\partial_\theta q''_{\can}
\]

is one of:

\[
\mathrm{EF\text{-}linear},
\quad
\mathrm{chart},
\quad
\gamma/\mathrm{background},
\quad
\mathrm{normalization/gauge}.
\]

Its order-seven component is therefore in

\[
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{EF\text{-}lin}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{chart}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\gamma/\mathrm{bg}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{norm}},
\]

which is contained in \(\mathcal D_7\).

Thus

\[
[\partial_\theta q''_{\can}]_7=0
\quad\text{in }\mathcal Q_7^C.
\]

Hence

\[
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0.
\]

This is true even if

\[
[z^7]\partial_\theta q''_{\can}\ne0
\]

as a raw Taylor coefficient.

So the theorem is quotient-theoretic, not coefficient-theoretic.

---

# 3. Why actual second curvature should also lie in \(\mathcal D_7\)

For second parameter curvature,

\[
\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can},
\]

the same origin classification applies.

It is second-order in parameters, but its source origin is still:

\[
\mathrm{EF\text{-}parameter\ curvature},
\quad
\mathrm{chart\ curvature},
\quad
\mathrm{normalization\ curvature},
\quad
\gamma/\mathrm{background}.
\]

It is not the D4 clean carrier insertion \(C_7\).

Therefore its order-seven component should also lie in \(\mathcal D_7\):

\[
[\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can}]_7=0
\quad\text{in }\mathcal Q_7^C.
\]

So

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
\]

The subscript \(9\) does not change the \(s_7\)-test. The \(s_7\) coordinate is an order-seven live carrier coordinate, and actual second curvature should not produce that live class.

---

# 4. What could make this fail?

There are three failure modes.

## A. Actual-source family includes package carrier coordinates

If the actual-source manifold is defined broadly enough to include free local package coordinates, then \(C_7\) may become an actual tangent by definition.

That would destroy rank two artificially.

So the actual-source manifold must exclude:

\[
\text{free corrected-package coefficients},
\]

\[
\text{formal D4 carrier variables},
\]

\[
\text{local carrier coordinates not generated by actual EF/gamma/tail data}.
\]

This is the theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\text{NoFreePackageCoordinatesInActualSource.}
}
\]

## B. The nuisance quotient kills \(C_7\)

If

\[
C_7\in \mathcal N_7,
\]

then

\[
[C_7]=0
\]

and there is no live \(s_7\) coordinate for rank two.

That is a genuine local no-go for the rank-two branch, unless the quotient is too coarse and can be refined.

## C. The nuisance quotient identifies actual source-linear drift with \(C_7\)

If

\[
C_7-d_{\mathrm{EF}}\in\mathcal N_7
\]

for some source-linear order-seven drift \(d_{\mathrm{EF}}\), then actual parameter variations may falsely project to \(s_7\).

This is quotient overcollapse. It should be treated as a defect of the quotient, not as actual \(s_7\)-motion.

So need:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_7\text{ is filtered with respect to }C_7\text{-carrier origin.}
}
\]

---

# 5. Finite rank-two audit

The finite audit is now:

## Step 1: Build \(\mathcal Q_7^C\)

Construct

\[
\mathcal Q_7^C=\mathcal J_7/\mathcal D_7.
\]

## Step 2: Check live \(C_7\)

Verify

\[
[C_7]\ne0
\quad\text{and}\quad
\|[C_7]\|\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

## Step 3: Check first tangents

For each actual first tangent \(v_\theta\), verify

\[
[v_\theta]_7\in\mathcal D_7.
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\ell_7(v_\theta)=0.
\]

## Step 4: Check second curvature

For each actual pair \((\theta_i,\theta_j)\), verify

\[
[\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can}]_7\in\mathcal D_7.
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\ell_7(\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can})=0.
\]

## Step 5: Check local contaminants

For \(E_2^Y\) and \(\mathfrak C_Y\), verify the local fiber derivative has zero \(s_7\)-coordinate, unless it is finite coherent amplitude only.

---

# 6. Consequence if the audit passes

If the \(C_7\)-carrier quotient audit passes, then:

\[
\Pi_{s_7}
\left(
\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual}
+
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\right)
=
0.
\]

Together with the earlier slot-rank expectation

\[
\Pi_{s_9^Y}
\left(
\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual}
+
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\right)
=
0,
\]

we get:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual}
+
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\right)
=
0.
\]

Thus ordinary actual parameters do not contribute to \(B_Y\).

If lower actual directions, \(E_2^Y\), commutators, and nuisance also vanish locally, then:

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

Then D4 remains rank two.

---

# 7. Rank-two D4 wall after \(B_Y=0\)

If

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

D4 gives

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

The coefficient matrix is

\[
M_{D4}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

If

\[
|c_7\alpha_9|\ge Q^{-A}
\]

and the matrix norm is polynomially bounded, then

\[
|F_{2Y}|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right).
\]

So the wall theorem becomes vector anisotropy, not moving-line finite type.

This avoids:

\[
\rho_Y,\quad \rho_\Gamma,\quad
\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G,\quad
\text{forced ODE trap}.
\]

---

# 8. Final theorem

## \(C_7\)-CarrierFaithfulness

Assume:

1. The order-seven carrier quotient

\[
\mathcal Q_7^C
=
\mathcal J_7/
(
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{EF\text{-}lin}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{chart}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\gamma/\mathrm{bg}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{norm}}
+
\mathcal N_7
)
\]

is well-defined and filtered by carrier origin.

2. The live class survives:

\[
[C_7]\ne0
\quad\text{with polynomial norm.}
\]

3. Actual-source first tangents and second curvature have no D4 carrier origin, only EF-linear/chart/background/normalization origin.

Then:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual})=0,
\]

and

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
\]

This proves that ordinary fixed-\(\alpha\) actual first and second variations do not create \(s_7\)-motion.

---

## Final status

Rank two now depends on a concrete quotient-faithfulness theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal Q_7^C\text{ must distinguish live }C_7\text{ from source-linear order-seven drift.}
}
\]

If it does, then generic actual order-seven jet variation does not imply actual \(s_7\)-motion.

This keeps the possibility

\[
B_Y=0
\]

alive and makes rank-two D4 the preferred route.
