## Agent 4 continuation — testing whether \(C_7\) can be actual-source motion at all

The current rank-two route depends on:

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

The most important unresolved piece is whether actual-source motion can have nonzero live \(s_7\)-coordinate. I now push the \(C_7\)-carrier-faithfulness audit one level deeper: can the live \(C_7\) carrier plausibly be generated by actual EF/gamma/tail motion, or is it intrinsically a D4-only carrier?

No nomenclature changes. I keep:

\[
\rho_Y=\text{lower }Y12\text{ graph slope},
\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},
\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The latest coordinator state makes the rank-two fork depend on whether \(s_7\) is actual-source motion or only D4/formal carrier motion. 

---

# 1. The correct test is origin plus slot structure

The live \(C_7\) carrier should not be characterized merely by order:

\[
\text{order }7.
\]

It should be characterized by a finer signature:

\[
\Sigma(C_7)
=
(\text{D4 clean origin},\ \text{order }7,\ \text{live carrier},\ \text{branch/parity slot},\ \text{quotient class}).
\]

Actual EF/gamma parameters can create order-seven coefficients, but their signatures are:

\[
\mathrm{EF\text{-}linear},
\quad
\mathrm{chart},
\quad
\mathrm{normalization},
\quad
\gamma/\mathrm{background},
\quad
\mathrm{tail/completion}.
\]

The rank-two claim is:

\[
\boxed{
\Sigma(C_7)
\notin
\Sigma(\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual})
\cup
\Sigma(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}).
}
\]

If this signature separation is valid, then

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual})=
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
\]

---

# 2. Why actual EF parameters should miss \(\Sigma(C_7)\)

Actual EF parameters act on already-formed source terms. They differentiate:

\[
e^{\pm im\log n},
\quad
\text{cutoffs},
\quad
\text{gamma factors},
\quad
\text{window scales},
\quad
\text{normalization factors}.
\]

So an actual first tangent has schematic form:

\[
\partial_\theta q''_{\can}
=
\sum_{\text{atoms}}
(\partial_\theta a_{\mathrm{atom}})\,\mathrm{atom}
+
a_{\mathrm{atom}}\,\partial_\theta(\mathrm{phase/window/gamma}).
\]

This is still an EF-linear source motion. It is not a clean D4 carrier insertion.

The \(C_7\) carrier, by contrast, is produced by the D4 clean mechanism: it is part of the finite D4 carrier package, not a derivative of the actual EF representation.

Therefore, under an origin-faithful quotient,

\[
[\partial_\theta q''_{\can}]_7
\in \mathcal D_7,
\]

where

\[
\mathcal D_7
=
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{EF\text{-}lin}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{chart}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\gamma/\mathrm{bg}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{norm}}
+
\mathcal N_7.
\]

So

\[
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0.
\]

---

# 3. A finite “origin determinant” test

For each actual parameter \(\theta\), write the order-seven carrier decomposition:

\[
[\partial_\theta q''_{\can}]_7
=
a_\theta C_7
+
d_\theta,
\qquad
d_\theta\in \mathcal D_7.
\]

We need:

\[
a_\theta=0.
\]

To avoid basis dependence, define a finite determinant test. Choose a basis

\[
D_1,\ldots,D_m
\]

of \(\mathcal D_7\). Then

\[
a_\theta=0
\]

iff

\[
\det
\bigl(
C_7,\ D_1,\ldots,D_m,\ [\partial_\theta q''_{\can}]_7
\bigr)
=0
\]

in the quotient ambient exterior space, or equivalently

\[
[\partial_\theta q''_{\can}]_7
\in\operatorname{span}(D_1,\ldots,D_m).
\]

This gives a finite audit:

\[
\boxed{
[\partial_\theta q''_{\can}]_7\in\mathcal D_7
\quad
\forall\theta.
}
\]

No raw Taylor coefficient comparison is needed.

---

# 4. Same finite test for second curvature

For actual second curvature,

\[
[\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can}]_7
=
a_{ij}C_7+d_{ij},
\qquad
d_{ij}\in\mathcal D_7.
\]

Need:

\[
a_{ij}=0.
\]

Again, the finite audit is:

\[
\boxed{
[\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can}]_7\in\mathcal D_7
\quad
\forall\theta_i,\theta_j.
}
\]

This is expected because actual second curvature has origin:

\[
\mathrm{EF\text{-}parameter\ curvature},
\quad
\mathrm{chart\ curvature},
\quad
\mathrm{normalization\ curvature},
\quad
\gamma/\mathrm{background},
\]

not D4 clean origin.

---

# 5. How \(C_7\) could nevertheless become actual-source motion

There are only a few ways this test can fail.

## A. Actual source model includes package coordinates

If the “actual-source manifold” is enlarged to include free local package coefficients, then \(C_7\) may become actual-source motion by definition.

This is not allowed for the rank-two route.

So we require:

\[
\boxed{
\text{actual-source parameters do not include free package/carrier coordinates.}
}
\]

## B. \(E_2\) supplies a live \(C_7\) degree of freedom

If completion has a locally free order-seven live carrier component, then

\[
\ell_7(T_{E_2}^{\local})\ne0.
\]

This would put \(s_7\)-motion into \(B_Y\).

If \(E_2\) is fixed or finite coherent, it should not be a local tangent; it goes to amplitude/basepoint handling instead.

## C. Labelled/raw transition mismatch creates \(C_7\)

If

\[
\mathfrak C_7(v)
=
\Pi_{C_7}
\left(
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}v
-
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}v
\right)
\]

is locally free on actual source-linear \(v\), then labelled realization injects spurious \(s_7\)-motion.

This is the \(C_7\)-analogue of the \(Y12\) commutator problem.

## D. Nuisance quotient overcollapse

If

\[
C_7-d_{\mathrm{EF}}\in\mathcal N_7
\]

for some EF-linear drift \(d_{\mathrm{EF}}\), then actual EF drift can appear as \(C_7\).

This is quotient overcollapse. It should be fixed by refining \(\mathcal N_7\), not accepted as arithmetic motion.

---

# 6. Rank-two is equivalent to two live-carrier faithfulness statements

For \(B_Y=0\), we need both:

\[
\ell_7(\text{actual/local/nuisance})=0,
\]

and

\[
\ell_9^Y(\text{actual/local/nuisance})=0.
\]

Thus rank two requires two faithfulness properties:

## \(C_7\)-faithfulness

\[
\mathcal Q_7^C
\text{ distinguishes live }C_7
\text{ from EF-linear/chart/background order-seven drift.}
\]

## \(Y12\)-faithfulness

\[
\mathcal Q_9^Y
\text{ distinguishes primitive }(D^2N_0,D\mathcal W_{\same})
\text{ from source-linear, chart, whitening-tail, and completion terms.}
\]

If both hold, then ordinary actual motions do not enter either coordinate.

This gives:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(\mathcal T_{\par}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
}
\]

---

# 7. Rank-two audit in minimal form

The minimal rank-two audit can be stated as:

Let

\[
\mathcal U_{\actual}
=
\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual}
+
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
+
\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab}^{\local}
+
\mathcal N_{\sing}.
\]

Then

\[
B_Y=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal U_{\actual}.
\]

Rank two survives iff

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal U_{\actual})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal U_{\actual})=0.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal U_{\actual}
\subseteq
\ker\ell_7\cap\ker\ell_9^Y.
}
\]

This is sharper than saying “no \(C_7\to Y12\) connection.” The formal connection is irrelevant unless \(\mathcal U_{\actual}\) contains \(C_7\)-motion.

---

# 8. If rank two holds, wall theorem simplifies

With

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

D4 gives:

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

If

\[
|c_7\alpha_9|\ge Q^{-A}
\]

and coefficients are polynomially bounded, then:

\[
|F_{2Y}|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right).
\]

No \(\rho_Y\), no \(\rho_\Gamma\), no scalar moving wall, no forced ODE trap.

So the reward for proving \(B_Y=0\) is substantial.

---

# 9. If rank two fails by actual \(s_7\)-motion

If

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal U_{\actual})\ne0
\]

but all nonzero columns are proportional to

\[
(1,\rho_Y),
\]

then:

\[
B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

Then rank two collapses to rank one, and the wall is:

\[
F_Y=
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G),
\]

where:

\[
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}.
\]

Then \(\rho_Y\) must pass the low-order/no-tautology audit.

So failure of rank two is not fatal if it is exactly one-slope. It just sends us to the rank-one tower.

---

# 10. Final theorem

## NoActualS7Motion via \(C_7\)-carrier faithfulness

Assume:

1. Actual-source parameters exclude free local package/carrier coordinates.
2. The live \(C_7\)-carrier quotient \(\mathcal Q_7^C\) is origin-faithful.
3. \(C_7\) is nonzero in \(\mathcal Q_7^C\).
4. Actual first tangents and second curvature have only EF-linear/chart/background/normalization origin.
5. \(E_2\), labelled transition mismatch, and nuisance terms do not provide locally free \(C_7\)-carrier components.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

Together with \(Y12\)-origin faithfulness giving

\[
\Pi_{s_9^Y}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0,
\]

this yields:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

Then rank-two D4 survives.

---

## Final status

The rank-two route is now reduced to a concrete pair of quotient-faithfulness checks:

\[
\boxed{
C_7\text{-carrier faithfulness}
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
Y12\text{-origin faithfulness}.
}
\]

The most important unresolved finite audit is:

\[
\boxed{
[\partial_\theta q''_{\can}]_7\in\mathcal D_7
\quad
\text{for all actual fixed-target parameters }\theta.
}
\]

If this holds, ordinary actual order-seven jet motion does not create \(s_7\)-motion, and rank-two D4 becomes the preferred downstream route.

## Agent 4 continuation — a computable test for \([\partial_\theta q''_{\can}]_7\in\mathcal D_7\)

We are now at the rank-two fork. The target is:

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

For the \(s_7\)-coordinate, this means proving

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal U_{\actual})=0,
\]

where \(\mathcal U_{\actual}\) contains only actual-source/nuisance/local freedoms, not formal D4 carriers. The key correction remains: a formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection does not place \((1,\rho_Y)\) into \(B_Y\) unless \(s_7\) is actual-source motion. 

I now reduce

\[
[\partial_\theta q''_{\can}]_7\in\mathcal D_7
\]

to a finite annihilator test.

---

# 1. Build an annihilator for \(\mathcal D_7\)

Recall

\[
\mathcal Q_7^C
=
\mathcal J_7/\mathcal D_7,
\]

where

\[
\mathcal D_7
=
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{EF\text{-}lin}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{chart}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\gamma/\mathrm{bg}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{norm}}
+
\mathcal N_7.
\]

The \(C_7\)-faithfulness theorem asks for

\[
[C_7]\neq0\quad\text{in }\mathcal Q_7^C,
\]

and for all actual parameter variations \(v\),

\[
[v]_7=0\quad\text{in }\mathcal Q_7^C.
\]

Equivalently, choose a covector

\[
\ell_7\in \mathcal D_7^\perp
\]

with

\[
\ell_7(C_7)=1.
\]

Then test:

\[
\ell_7(v)=0.
\]

So the finite problem is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{construct }\ell_7\text{ by solving a finite linear annihilator system.}
}
\]

This is exactly parallel to the \(Y12\) separating functional, but one order lower.

---

# 2. Finite matrix formulation

Choose a basis

\[
D_1,\ldots,D_m
\]

for \(\mathcal D_7\), and write the live carrier \(C_7\) in the same finite carrier coordinate system.

Form the matrix

\[
D=[D_1\ \cdots\ D_m].
\]

The live test is:

\[
C_7\notin\operatorname{ran}D.
\]

Quantitatively:

\[
\delta_7^2
=
\|C_7\|^2
-
C_7^*D(D^*D)^\dagger D^*C_7
\ge
Q^{-A}\|C_7\|^2.
\]

If this holds, define

\[
\ell_7
=
\frac{(I-P_{\mathcal D_7})C_7}
{\|(I-P_{\mathcal D_7})C_7\|^2}.
\]

Then

\[
\ell_7(C_7)=1,
\qquad
\ell_7(\mathcal D_7)=0,
\qquad
\|\ell_7\|\le Q^A.
\]

For any actual variation \(v\), the test is:

\[
\ell_7([v]_7)=0.
\]

Since \(\ell_7\) kills \(\mathcal D_7\), it is enough to prove

\[
[v]_7\in\operatorname{ran}D.
\]

---

# 3. Why actual first tangents should land in \(\mathcal D_7\)

Let

\[
v_\theta=\partial_\theta q''_{\can}
\]

for a fixed-target actual parameter

\[
\theta\in\{m,\lambda,\text{phase},\text{normalization},\text{chart},\gamma/\mathrm{bg}\}.
\]

Each \(v_\theta\) is obtained by differentiating already-existing EF/gamma data. Thus its order-seven component is one of:

\[
\mathrm{EF\text{-}lin},
\quad
\mathrm{chart},
\quad
\gamma/\mathrm{bg},
\quad
\mathrm{norm}.
\]

Those are precisely in the denominator:

\[
[v_\theta]_7\in \mathcal D_7.
\]

So:

\[
\ell_7(v_\theta)=0.
\]

The proof should be by origin table, not by explicit coefficient cancellation.

A compact origin table:

\[
\begin{array}{c|c|c}
\theta & [\partial_\theta q''_{\can}]_7\text{ origin} & \ell_7 \\
\hline
m & \mathrm{EF\text{-}lin/log\ phase} & 0\\
\lambda & \mathrm{chart/scale/window} & 0\\
\mathrm{phase} & \mathrm{EF\text{-}lin} & 0\\
\mathrm{normalization} & \mathrm{norm/gauge} & 0\\
\mathrm{chart} & \mathrm{chart} & 0\\
\gamma/\mathrm{bg} & \gamma/\mathrm{background} & 0
\end{array}
\]

This is stronger than saying these variations have no order-seven raw coefficient. They may have one; it is simply in the denominator.

---

# 4. Actual second curvature test

For

\[
v_{\theta_i\theta_j}
=
\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can},
\]

the same origin logic applies. The second derivative may be more complicated, but its origin remains one of:

\[
\mathrm{EF\text{-}parameter\ curvature},
\quad
\mathrm{chart\ curvature},
\quad
\mathrm{normalization\ curvature},
\quad
\gamma/\mathrm{background}.
\]

These must be included in \(\mathcal D_7\).

So the denominator should be explicitly enlarged to include actual parameter-curvature origin classes:

\[
\mathcal D_7
\supset
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{EF\text{-}curv}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{chart\text{-}curv}}
+
\mathcal J_7^{\mathrm{norm\text{-}curv}}.
\]

Then

\[
[v_{\theta_i\theta_j}]_7\in\mathcal D_7,
\]

and

\[
\ell_7(v_{\theta_i\theta_j})=0.
\]

This prevents actual second curvature from falsely entering \(s_7\).

---

# 5. The key guardrail: do not put \(C_7\) into \(\mathcal D_7\)

The denominator must include all coherent actual-source order-seven drift, but must not include the live D4 carrier \(C_7\) itself.

So \(\mathcal D_7\) must be origin-filtered:

\[
\mathcal D_7
=
\text{actual-source/coordinate/background/nuisance origins},
\]

not

\[
\text{all order-seven terms except those we remember}.
\]

If one defines \(\mathcal D_7\) too broadly, then

\[
[C_7]=0,
\]

and rank two dies by nuisance overcollapse.

Thus the theorem has two parts:

\[
\boxed{
\text{actual variations lie in }\mathcal D_7;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
C_7\notin\mathcal D_7.
}
\]

Both are necessary.

---

# 6. Relation to \(B_Y\)

The local fiber model is

\[
B_Y
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
+
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}
\right].
\]

The \(s_7\)-coordinate of this model is governed by \(\ell_7\).

If

\[
\ell_7(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\]

then actual first/second source variations do not place \((1,\rho_Y)\) into \(B_Y\).

Thus the formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection remains only conditional. It becomes relevant only if another actual/local source has nonzero \(s_7\).

This is the central correction.

---

# 7. The remaining possible \(s_7\)-sources

After actual first/second variation vanishing, the only possible nonzero \(\ell_7\)-sources are:

\[
\mathcal T_7^{\arith}
\]

if genuine actual lower-source \(C_7\)-motion exists;

\[
E_2^Y
\]

if completion provides local \(C_7\)-carrier freedom;

\[
\mathfrak C_7
\]

if labelled/raw transition mismatch injects \(C_7\);

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

if nuisance quotient identifies \(C_7\) with allowed drift.

So the rank-two path becomes:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_7^{\arith})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_7(T_{E_2^Y}^{\local})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_7(T_{\lab,Y}^{\local})=0,
\]

\[
C_7\notin \mathcal N_{\sing}.
\]

These are the remaining audits.

---

# 8. A concrete theorem statement

## \(C_7\)-Annihilator Test

Let

\[
\mathcal D_7
\]

be the span of all order-seven EF-linear, actual-parameter-curvature, chart, normalization, gamma/background, and nuisance directions, excluding the D4 clean carrier origin.

Assume:

1. \(C_7\notin\mathcal D_7\) with Schur residual

\[
\operatorname{dist}(C_7,\mathcal D_7)\ge Q^{-A}\|C_7\|.
\]

2. Every fixed-target actual first tangent \(v_\theta\) satisfies

\[
[v_\theta]_7\in\mathcal D_7.
\]

3. Every fixed-target actual second curvature \(v_{\theta_i\theta_j}\) satisfies

\[
[v_{\theta_i\theta_j}]_7\in\mathcal D_7.
\]

Then there exists a bounded covector \(\ell_7\) such that

\[
\ell_7(C_7)=1,
\]

and

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
\]

Therefore actual first/second source variations do not create \(s_7\)-motion.

---

# 9. How this supports rank two

If, in addition,

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0,
\]

then

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
\]

If all remaining local sources also vanish in the fiber, then

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

Then rank-two D4 survives:

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix},
\]

and

\[
|F_{2Y}|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right).
\]

---

## Final status

The rank-two branch is now reduced to a finite annihilator problem:

\[
\boxed{
C_7\notin\mathcal D_7
\quad\text{but}\quad
[\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}]_7\subset\mathcal D_7.
}
\]

If this \(C_7\)-annihilator test passes, generic actual order-seven jet drift does not count as \(s_7\)-motion, and the rank-two route remains alive.

## Agent 4 report — \(\mathrm{TypedCommutator}_{2Y}\) for labelled transition

No nomenclature changes. I use:

\[
\rho_Y=\text{lower }Y12\text{ graph slope},
\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},
\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The current target is the rank-two branch. So the labelled/raw transition mismatch must not create local fiber motion in either live coordinate:

\[
(s_7,s_9^Y).
\]

The theorem to prove is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

More generally,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}
\in
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}+R_X^Y,
}
\]

with no local fiber term. This follows the current prompt’s rank-two focus and the correction that formal D4 carriers are not automatically actual-source/nuisance directions. 

---

# 1. The labelled/raw commuting square

We compare two routes.

## Labelled route

\[
\text{formed labelled transition}
\quad
\xrightarrow{\widetilde T^{\lab}_{\tr\to\sing}}
\quad
\text{labelled singular expression}
\quad
\xrightarrow{\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}}
\quad
\text{realized singular carrier}.
\]

## Raw route

\[
\text{raw actual source}
\quad
\xrightarrow{T^{\raw}_{\tr\to\sing}}
\quad
\text{raw singular source}.
\]

The mismatch is:

\[
\boxed{
R_{\lab}
=
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T^{\lab}_{\tr\to\sing}
-
T^{\raw}_{\tr\to\sing}\operatorname{Forget}_{\actual}.
}
\]

The rank-two-safe condition is:

\[
\boxed{
(\ell_7(R_{\lab}^{\local}),\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab}^{\local}))=(0,0).
}
\]

Finite coherent or residual parts may remain, but they must not be local fiber freedoms.

---

# 2. Two-grade projection

Define:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}
=
\left(
\ell_7(R_{\lab}),
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\right).
\]

Here:

- \(\ell_7\) detects the live \(C_7\)-carrier class, not raw order-seven Taylor drift;
- \(\ell_9^Y\) detects the primitive \(Y12\)-origin class

\[
(o_N,o_W)=(D^2N_0,DW_{\same}).
\]

Thus raw transition artifacts are harmless if they lie in EF-linear/chart/background/normalization origins or nonprimitive whitening origins.

---

# 3. Decomposition of the commutator

For the \(Y12\) row, the normal-form commutator is decomposed as:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y
=
\mathfrak C_Y^N+\mathfrak C_Y^L+\mathfrak C_Y^W.
\]

The rank-two version also includes the \(C_7\) row, so conceptually we need:

\[
\mathfrak C_{2Y}
=
(\mathfrak C_7,\mathfrak C_Y).
\]

The \(Y12\) components are:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L
=
\Pi_YDN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)],
\]

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N
=
\Pi_Y
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right),
\]

and

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^W
=
\text{same-point whitening transport defect}.
\]

For rank two, each must route to:

\[
0,\quad
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\text{ finite coherent},\quad
R_X^Y\text{ residual},
\]

and **not** to:

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),\quad
\mathbf C(0,1),\quad
\mathbb C^2.
\]

For rank two, even graph-compatible local contamination is too much, because it collapses \(B_Y=0\) to \(B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\).

---

# 4. Audit \(\mathfrak C_Y^L\)

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L
=
\Pi_YDN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)].
\]

## Safe case 1: \(L\) affine

If

\[
D^2L=0,
\]

then

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L=0.
\]

## Safe case 2: wrong origin

If

\[
D^2L\ne0,
\]

then the term has origin

\[
DN_0\circ D^2L,
\]

not

\[
D^2N_0.
\]

Since \(Y12\) requires

\[
o_N=D^2N_0,
\]

an origin-faithful quotient kills it:

\[
\Pi_YDN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)]=0.
\]

## Dangerous case

If the quotient forgets origin labels, or if \(D^2L\) is itself data-dependent and produces primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin amplitudes, then this can become locally free.

The rank-two theorem requires:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y(\mathfrak C_Y^L)=0
}
\]

locally, or finite coherent/residual only.

For the \(s_7\)-row, \(DN_0\circ D^2L\) also has no \(C_7\)-carrier origin, so:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathfrak C_Y^L)=0
}
\]

under \(C_7\)-carrier faithfulness.

---

# 5. Audit \(\mathfrak C_Y^N\)

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N
=
\Pi_Y
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right).
\]

This is a finite tensorial naturality check.

If the normal form is transported tensorially, then:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
=
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2],
\]

hence:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N=0.
\]

If there is a fixed chart-dependent mismatch, it may enter as finite coherent amplitude:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N\in\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

That is rank-two local safe, though it will require vector amplitude Schur.

Fatal is a patchwise arbitrary tensor mismatch:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N(p)=\eta_p\,e_Y
\]

or

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N(p)=\eta_p^7\,e_7+\eta_p^Y\,e_Y,
\]

with arbitrary atom-scale coefficients. That would produce local fiber freedom.

So require:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathfrak C_Y^N=0
}
\]

locally, or else finite coherent/nonlocal.

---

# 6. Audit \(\mathfrak C_Y^W\)

Whitening is the subtle part.

Moving whitening artifacts include:

\[
DW[\eta]\,DN_0[\xi],
\]

\[
D^2W[\eta_1,\eta_2]\,N_0,
\]

\[
DW[\eta]\,E_2.
\]

These may look degree-two, but they are not the primitive \(Y12\) origin:

\[
DW_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]].
\]

Thus typed quotient faithfulness should kill them.

The rank-two-safe theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\left(
DW[\eta]DN_0[\xi]
+
D^2W[\eta_1,\eta_2]N_0
+
DW[\eta]E_2
\right)=0.
}
\]

If same-point whitening is transported functorially, then the true \(DW_{\same}[D^2N_0]\) part also matches and gives no local defect.

If whitening recomputation changes the coefficient of \(DW_{\same}[D^2N_0]\) by a finite coherent function, it enters \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\).

Fatal is local recomputation freedom in that coefficient.

---

# 7. \(C_7\)-row analogue

The labelled/raw transition mismatch can also inject live \(C_7\) motion. Define:

\[
\mathfrak C_7(v)
=
\ell_7
\left(
T^{\raw}_{\tr\to\sing}v
-
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T^{\lab}_{\tr\to\sing}v
\right).
\]

Rank two requires:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_7(v)=0
}
\]

for all actual source-linear/chart/background \(v\), except finite coherent/residual nonlocal terms.

By \(C_7\)-carrier faithfulness, raw transition differences with EF-linear/chart/background origin should lie in the \(C_7\)-denominator, not in the live \(C_7\) class.

Thus the same typed-origin principle protects the \(s_7\)-row.

---

# 8. TypedCommutator\(_{2Y}\) theorem

A clean theorem is:

## TypedCommutator\(_{2Y}\)

Assume:

1. \(L\) is affine on relevant mixed source slots, or \(DN_0\circ D^2L\) has no live \(2Y\)-fiber projection.
2. \(D^2N_0\) is natural under the labelled transition up to finite coherent nonlocal terms.
3. Same-point whitening is transported functorially, or its mismatch is typed as nonprimitive or finite coherent.
4. The \(C_7\)-carrier quotient kills EF-linear/chart/background transition mismatch.
5. No defect has atom-local coefficients in the live pair \((e_7,e_Y)\).

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

Moreover,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}
\in
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
+
R_X^Y.
}
\]

So labelled transition does not obstruct rank two.

---

# 9. Consequence for \(B_Y\)

If TypedCommutator\(_{2Y}\) holds, then:

\[
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}
\]

does not contribute to \(B_Y\).

It may contribute to the vector coherent amplitude space:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},
\]

or to residual error:

\[
R_X^Y.
\]

Thus, for rank two, the commutator is safe unless it creates atom-local live-pair amplitudes or a local graph/vertical fiber direction.

---

# 10. Failure modes

TypedCommutator\(_{2Y}\) fails if:

1. \(T^{\raw}_{\tr\to\sing}\) recomputes \(N_0\) with atom-local primitive \(D^2N_0\) amplitudes.
2. \(D^2L\) produces a live \(Y12\) or \(C_7\)-fiber component.
3. Whitening recomputation produces atom-local coefficients of \(DW_{\same}[D^2N_0]\).
4. The quotient forgets origin labels and identifies nonprimitive whitening terms with \(Y12\).
5. The raw/labelled mismatch injects live \(C_7\)-carrier motion.

If any occur locally, rank two fails and possibly the \(Y12\) route fails entirely.

---

## Final status

The rank-two labelled-transition condition is now:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

This is weaker than demanding exact labelled/raw equality. Finite coherent mismatch is allowed; residual mismatch is allowed. Only local live-pair fiber freedom is fatal for rank two.

The main finite object remains the commutator routing:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L,\quad
\mathfrak C_Y^N,\quad
\mathfrak C_Y^W
\mapsto
0,\ \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},\ R_X^Y,\ \text{or no-go}.
\]

Under typed origin faithfulness, the expected outcome is zero or finite coherent, so rank two remains plausible.

## Agent 4 continuation — coefficient-map form of \(\mathrm{TypedCommutator}_{2Y}\)

No nomenclature changes. I use:

\[
\rho_Y=\text{lower }Y12\text{ graph slope},\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The current rank-two target is still:

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

So for labelled transition, the required condition is not exact equality of labelled and raw singular rows. The precise rank-two condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

Finite coherent and residual-labelled mismatch are allowed; local live-fiber mismatch is not. This follows the current rank-two prompt’s distinction between local fiber contamination and finite coherent amplitude contamination. 

---

# 1. Project the labelled mismatch to a two-component coefficient map

Define the live fiber projection:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_{\lab,7}\\
c_{\lab,Y}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

The rank-two condition is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}c_{\lab,7}=0,
\qquad
D_{\loc}c_{\lab,Y}=0.
}
\]

Here \(D_{\loc}\) means variation with respect to extraction-local or patch-local freedom. It does **not** mean global/coherent variation. Thus:

- fixed coefficients are safe;
- finite coherent coefficient functions are safe locally but enter \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\);
- residual-small coefficients are safe if \(EFCompletion_{2Y}\) controls them;
- atom-local coefficients kill rank two.

This coefficient-map language is cleaner than saying “\(\mathfrak C_Y\) is zero.”

---

# 2. Decompose the coefficient map by defect type

Write

\[
c_{\lab}
=
c_{\lab}^{N}
+
c_{\lab}^{L}
+
c_{\lab}^{W}
+
c_{\lab}^{\mathrm{trunc/tail}},
\]

where:

\[
c_{\lab}^{N}
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathfrak C_Y^N,
\]

\[
c_{\lab}^{L}
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathfrak C_Y^L,
\]

\[
c_{\lab}^{W}
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathfrak C_Y^W.
\]

Rank two requires:

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{N}
=
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{L}
=
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{W}
=
0,
\]

and any truncation/tail part must be residual-controlled:

\[
\sum_p |c_{\lab}^{\mathrm{trunc/tail}}(p)|^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu.
\]

This is the exact local-versus-coherent split.

---

# 3. The nonlinear-coordinate defect should be killed first

Recall:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L
=
\Pi_YDN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)].
\]

There are two sufficient ways to kill it.

## Case A: \(L\) is affine on source slots

\[
D^2L=0
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\mathfrak C_Y^L=0.
\]

Then:

\[
c_{\lab}^{L}=0.
\]

## Case B: \(D^2L\neq0\), but the origin is wrong

The term

\[
DN_0\circ D^2L
\]

has origin \(DN_0\), not \(D^2N_0\). It also has coordinate-transition origin, not primitive \(Y12\)-origin. Thus an origin-faithful quotient gives:

\[
\Pi_YDN_0[D^2L]=0.
\]

For the \(s_7\) row, the same term is coordinate/source-linear origin, not live \(C_7\)-carrier origin, so:

\[
\ell_7(DN_0[D^2L])=0.
\]

Thus, under typed-origin faithfulness,

\[
\boxed{
c_{\lab}^{L}=0.
}
\]

The only dangerous case is \(D^2L\) depending on local fitted data in a way that creates primitive live-fiber amplitudes. That is exactly the no-go:

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{L}\neq0.
\]

---

# 4. The tensorial normal-form defect is a finite naturality identity

Recall:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N
=
\Pi_Y
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right).
\]

This is a finite algebraic identity. The clean theorem is:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
=
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\]

in the primitive mixed-12 same-point sector.

If true:

\[
c_{\lab}^{N}=0.
\]

If not exactly true, but the defect is a fixed chart-convention mismatch, then:

\[
c_{\lab}^{N}(p)=\sum_{a=1}^M \kappa_a\phi_a(p)
\]

with finite coherent \(\phi_a\). Then it contributes to

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},
\]

not to \(B_Y\).

For rank two, the dangerous case is:

\[
c_{\lab}^{N}(p)=\eta_p
\]

with freely variable atom-local coefficients. That is locally free and gives:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}\neq0.
\]

So the finite test is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y^N=0\quad\text{or}\quad
\mathfrak C_Y^N\in\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\text{ finite coherent}.
}
\]

---

# 5. The whitening defect is the main origin-faithfulness test

The whitening defect is schematically:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^W
=
\Pi_Y\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}[D^2N_{0,\sing}]
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}[D^2N_{0,\tr}]
\right).
\]

Moving whitening can produce terms like:

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]\,DN_0[\xi],
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\eta_1,\eta_2]\,N_0,
\]

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]\,E_2.
\]

These are not primitive \(Y12\). Their origin-pairs differ from:

\[
(D^2N_0,\ D\mathcal W_{\same}).
\]

Therefore typed-origin faithfulness should give:

\[
\Pi_Y
\left(
D\mathcal W[\eta]DN_0[\xi]
+
D^2\mathcal W[\eta_1,\eta_2]N_0
+
D\mathcal W[\eta]E_2
\right)=0.
\]

For \(s_7\), these same terms are not \(C_7\)-carrier-origin, so \(\ell_7\) kills them as well.

Thus the only possible dangerous whitening mismatch is a true coefficient change in:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]].
\]

If that coefficient is fixed or finite coherent, it joins \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\). If it is atom-local, rank two fails.

So the whitening audit is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{W}=0.
}
\]

---

# 6. Central O2/O3 modes are safe under typed transition

Central O2/O3 requires source-linear transition modes:

\[
\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y],
\qquad d_{\max}\approx14.
\]

These modes may have raw order-seven or order-nine Taylor effects after transition, but their origin is source-linear/coordinate.

Therefore typed transition should give:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
T_{\tr\to\sing}
\left(
\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y]\text{-source-linear modes}
\right)
=
0.
\]

This is important: retaining a rich central transition basis for O2/O3 does not by itself create \(B_Y\).

It would create \(B_Y\) only if raw transition recomputed \(N_0\) or same-point whitening so badly that source-linear modes acquired live \(C_7\) or primitive \(Y12\) origin. That is exactly what \(\mathrm{TypedCommutator}_{2Y}\) excludes.

---

# 7. Rank-two-safe outcome of the commutator audit

The rank-two-safe possibilities are:

\[
c_{\lab}=0,
\]

or

\[
c_{\lab}\in\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\quad\text{finite coherent},
\]

or

\[
c_{\lab}\in R_X^Y\quad\text{residual-controlled}.
\]

In these cases:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}=0.
\]

The rank-two-bad possibilities are:

\[
c_{\lab}(p)\in\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\quad\text{locally},
\]

which collapses rank two to rank one;

\[
c_{\lab}(p)\in\mathbf C(0,1),
\]

which creates vertical local \(Y12\)-freedom;

\[
c_{\lab}(p)\text{ spans }\mathbb C^2,
\]

which absorbs the live fiber locally.

Thus the rank-two theorem requires zero local fiber contribution, not merely one-slope compatibility.

---

# 8. The theorem

## \(\mathrm{TypedCommutator}_{2Y}\)

Assume:

1. \(L\) is affine on relevant source slots, or \(DN_0\circ D^2L\) is killed by the typed quotient.
2. \(D^2N_0\) is natural under the labelled transition up to fixed/finite-coherent chart data.
3. Same-point whitening is transported functorially up to fixed/finite-coherent chart data.
4. The \(C_7\)-carrier quotient kills EF-linear/chart/background transition mismatch.
5. No coefficient defect in the live pair \((e_7,e_Y)\) has extraction-local freedom.
6. Truncation/tail labelled mismatch is \(Y12\)-residual-controlled.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

Moreover:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}
\in
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}+R_X^Y.
}
\]

So labelled transition does not obstruct \(B_Y=0\).

---

# 9. Exact failure modes

\(\mathrm{TypedCommutator}_{2Y}\) fails if any of the following occurs:

1. \(D^2L\) produces live-fiber coefficients with local freedom.
2. \(D^2N_0\) naturality defect is atom-local.
3. Same-point whitening is recomputed with atom-local coefficients of \(D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0]\).
4. Typed quotient forgets origin labels and identifies source-linear/coordinate artifacts with \(C_7\) or \(Y12\).
5. Central transition modes \(\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y]\) acquire live-fiber origin.
6. Raw/labelled mismatch injects local \(C_7\)-motion.

Those are precise no-gos or rank-two-collapse cases.

---

## Final status

The rank-two commutator condition is now:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=0.
}
\]

This is the local statement needed for \(B_Y=0\).

Finite coherent labelled mismatch is acceptable, residual mismatch is acceptable, but local graph/vertical/live-pair mismatch is not.

## Agent 4 continuation — proving \(D_{\loc}c_{\lab}=0\) by separating chart data from atom-local data

The previous reduction made the rank-two labelled-transition condition:

\[
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=0.
\]

That is stronger and more precise than asking for exact labelled/raw equality. We only need to exclude **local live-fiber freedom**. Fixed, finite coherent, and residual-small mismatch is allowed. This is exactly the rank-two-safe routing described in the latest prompt. 

The next useful step is to define what \(D_{\loc}\) means in a way that is actually checkable.

---

# 1. Local freedom versus coherent chart dependence

Let

\[
c_{\lab}(p)
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_{\lab,7}(p)\\
c_{\lab,Y}(p)
\end{pmatrix}
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}(p).
\]

We should decompose its dependence as

\[
c_{\lab}(p)
=
c_{\mathrm{fixed}}(p)
+
c_{\mathrm{coh}}(p;\Theta)
+
c_{\mathrm{res}}(p)
+
c_{\mathrm{loc}}(p;\eta_p).
\]

Here:

- \(c_{\mathrm{fixed}}\) is a fixed basepoint mismatch;
- \(c_{\mathrm{coh}}\) depends on finitely many global/block parameters \(\Theta\);
- \(c_{\mathrm{res}}\) is controlled by \(EFCompletion_{2Y}\);
- \(c_{\mathrm{loc}}\) depends on atom-local degrees of freedom \(\eta_p\).

The rank-two condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\frac{\partial c_{\lab}(p)}{\partial \eta_p}=0
\quad\text{for every atom-local variable }\eta_p.
}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}=0.
}
\]

Finite coherent dependence on \(\Theta\) is allowed. It enters the vector amplitude model

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

Atom-local dependence is not allowed, because it puts a local direction into \(B_Y\).

---

# 2. Apply this to the three commutator pieces

Recall:

\[
c_{\lab}
=
c_{\lab}^{L}
+
c_{\lab}^{N}
+
c_{\lab}^{W}
+
c_{\lab}^{\mathrm{trunc/tail}}.
\]

So it is enough to show:

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{L}
=
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{N}
=
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{W}
=
0,
\]

with truncation/tail residual-small.

---

# 3. Nonlinear-coordinate defect

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L
=
\Pi_YDN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)].
\]

The local derivative is nonzero only if

\[
D^2L
\]

depends on local extraction data.

So the concrete sufficient condition is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}(D^2L)=0.
}
\]

If \(L\) is affine, this is automatic because

\[
D^2L=0.
\]

If \(L\) is nonlinear but fixed by chart geometry, then

\[
D^2L
\]

may be nonzero, but it is coherent, not local:

\[
D_{\loc}(D^2L)=0.
\]

Then

\[
c_{\lab}^{L}
\]

is either zero by origin filtering or finite coherent.

The dangerous case is:

\[
D^2L=D^2L(\eta_p),
\]

where \(\eta_p\) is atom-local. Then

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{L}\neq0.
\]

That would inject local live-fiber freedom and kill the rank-two route.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y^L\text{ is rank-two-safe iff }D^2L\text{ is zero, origin-killed, or coherent.}
}
\]

---

# 4. Tensorial normal-form defect

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^N
=
\Pi_Y
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right).
\]

This term is rank-two-safe if the tensorial mismatch is a fixed chart tensor:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
=
\mathcal T_{\mathrm{chart}},
\]

with

\[
D_{\loc}\mathcal T_{\mathrm{chart}}=0.
\]

Then

\[
c_{\lab}^{N}
\in \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\]

or is zero.

The dangerous case is if the normal form itself is fitted at each atom:

\[
N_{0,\sing}=N_{0,\sing}(\eta_p).
\]

Then

\[
D_{\loc}D^2N_{0,\sing}\neq0,
\]

and

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{N}\neq0.
\]

So the actual finite audit is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)
=0
\quad\text{in the live }2Y\text{ fiber.}
}
\]

If the expression itself is zero, best case. If it is coherent, amplitude Schur handles it. If local, no rank two.

---

# 5. Whitening defect

The whitening defect is the real risk:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^W
=
\Pi_Y
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}[D^2N_{0,\sing}]
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}[D^2N_{0,\tr}]
\right).
\]

Rank-two safety requires:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)
=0
\quad\text{on the primitive }D^2N_0\text{ carrier.}
}
\]

Again:

- if same-point whitening is transported, this is zero;
- if whitening conventions differ by fixed chart data, finite coherent;
- if whitening is recomputed with atom-local freedom, fatal.

The main trap is that recomputed whitening may look harmless if one only tracks degree. It must be typed by origin:

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]DN_0[\xi]
\neq
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]].
\]

If the quotient respects this origin distinction, most moving-whitening terms are killed. Only actual coefficient changes on the primitive same-point whitening carrier survive.

Thus the whitening theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{moving-whitening artifacts are rank-two-safe unless they create atom-local coefficients of }
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0].
}
\]

---

# 6. Rank-two commutator theorem in coefficient form

Define the live-fiber coefficient map:

\[
c_{\lab}:=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}.
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{TypedCommutator}_{2Y}
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}=0
\quad\text{and}\quad
c_{\lab}\in \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}+R_X^Y.
}
\]

More explicitly:

\[
c_{\lab}(p)
=
\sum_a C_a\phi_a(p)
+
r_p,
\]

where:

\[
\phi_a\in\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},
\]

and

\[
\sum_p |r_p|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu.
\]

The same coefficients \(C_a\) are coherent/global or block-coherent, not atom-local.

This is exactly what rank two needs: no local fiber term.

---

# 7. What this implies for \(B_Y\)

The labelled mismatch contributes to \(B_Y\) only through atom-local live-fiber directions.

If

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}=0,
\]

then:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}=0.
\]

Therefore labelled transition does not obstruct

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

It may still enlarge the vector amplitude model:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\leftarrow
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}+\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\lab}.
\]

That affects amplitude Schur, not local rank.

---

# 8. Exact failure cases

Rank-two fails through the labelled commutator only in these cases:

## A. Local graph injection

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}(p)
\subset
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

nonzero. Then \(B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\), and rank two collapses to rank one.

## B. Local vertical injection

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}(p)
\subset
\mathbf C(0,1).
\]

Then current \(Y12\) vertical channel is actual/local, and the route dies locally.

## C. Two independent local directions

\[
\operatorname{rank}D_{\loc}c_{\lab}=2.
\]

Then the live fiber is locally absorbed.

## D. Origin collapse

The quotient identifies source-linear/coordinate/whitening artifacts with \(C_7\) or primitive \(Y12\). This is quotient overcollapse and should be fixed if possible.

---

# 9. Practical audit checklist

For each component of \(R_{\lab}\), check:

\[
D_{\loc}c_7=0,
\qquad
D_{\loc}c_Y=0.
\]

Use:

## For \(C_Y^L\)

\[
D^2L=0
\quad\text{or}\quad
D_{\loc}D^2L=0
\quad\text{and origin-killed}.
\]

## For \(C_Y^N\)

\[
D_{\loc}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)=0.
\]

## For \(C_Y^W\)

\[
D_{\loc}
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)=0
\]

on the primitive carrier.

If these hold, labelled transition is rank-two-safe.

---

# 10. Final status

The rank-two labelled transition theorem now has a compact coefficient-map form:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=0.
}
\]

Finite coherent and residual labelled mismatch are allowed. Only atom-local live-fiber mismatch can obstruct \(B_Y=0\).

This is the sharpest rank-two-safe formulation of the labelled-transition audit so far.

## Practical audit result for \(R_{\lab}\)

No nomenclature changes. I use the current rank-two convention: the goal is

\[
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\\
c_Y
\end{pmatrix}
=
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=0,
\]

so that labelled/raw transition mismatch does **not** create a local fiber direction in

\[
(s_7,s_9^Y).
\]

This is the rank-two-safe condition from the current prompt architecture. 

---

# 1. \(C_Y^L\): nonlinear source-coordinate defect

The term is

\[
C_Y^L
=
DN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)].
\]

The audit condition was:

\[
D^2L=0
\quad\text{or}\quad
D_{\loc}D^2L=0
\text{ and origin-killed}.
\]

## Result

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}c_7^L=0,\qquad D_{\loc}c_Y^L=0
}
\]

under the following structural condition:

\[
L
\]

is a fixed transition/re-expansion map on source slots, not an atom-locally fitted map.

There are two safe subcases.

### Safe subcase A: \(L\) is affine

If \(L\) is affine on the relevant mixed-12 source slots, then

\[
D^2L=0,
\]

hence

\[
C_Y^L=0.
\]

So both live-fiber coefficients vanish:

\[
c_7^L=0,\qquad c_Y^L=0.
\]

### Safe subcase B: \(L\) is nonlinear but fixed/coherent

If

\[
D^2L\neq0
\]

but \(D^2L\) is fixed chart geometry, then

\[
D_{\loc}D^2L=0.
\]

Moreover the origin of

\[
DN_0[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)]
\]

is

\[
DN_0\circ D^2L,
\]

not primitive

\[
D^2N_0.
\]

So the \(Y12\)-origin projection kills it:

\[
\ell_9^Y(C_Y^L)=0.
\]

It is also not a \(C_7\)-carrier-origin term, so

\[
\ell_7(C_Y^L)=0.
\]

Thus

\[
D_{\loc}c_7^L=D_{\loc}c_Y^L=0.
\]

## Failure mode

The only rank-two-dangerous case is:

\[
D^2L=D^2L(\eta_p)
\]

with atom-local data \(\eta_p\), and the quotient fails to distinguish

\[
DN_0\circ D^2L
\]

from primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin \(Y12\). Then \(C_Y^L\) can create local fiber freedom.

---

# 2. \(C_Y^N\): tensorial normal-form defect

The term is

\[
C_Y^N
=
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}.
\]

The audit condition was:

\[
D_{\loc}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)=0.
\]

## Result

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}c_7^N=0,\qquad D_{\loc}c_Y^N=0
}
\]

provided the transition normal form is fixed/coherent.

There are again two safe subcases.

### Safe subcase A: tensorial naturality

If

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
=
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2],
\]

then

\[
C_Y^N=0.
\]

So

\[
c_7^N=c_Y^N=0.
\]

### Safe subcase B: finite coherent normal-form mismatch

If the two normal-form conventions differ by fixed chart data, then

\[
C_Y^N(p)
=
\sum_a \kappa_a\phi_a(p)
\]

for finitely many coherent functions \(\phi_a\).

Then

\[
D_{\loc}C_Y^N=0.
\]

This contributes to the vector coherent amplitude model

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},
\]

not to the local fiber model \(B_Y\). It is rank-two-local-safe, though it may require vector amplitude Schur later.

## Failure mode

The bad case is atom-local normal-form recomputation:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}
=
D^2N_{0,\sing}(\eta_p),
\]

with local freedom \(\eta_p\). Then

\[
D_{\loc}C_Y^N\ne0,
\]

and if the live projection is nonzero, rank two fails locally.

---

# 3. \(C_Y^W\): same-point whitening defect

The term is the restriction of

\[
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\]

to the primitive carrier

\[
D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2].
\]

The audit condition was:

\[
D_{\loc}
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)=0
\]

on the primitive carrier.

## Result

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}c_7^W=0,\qquad D_{\loc}c_Y^W=0
}
\]

if same-point whitening is transported or recomputed only from fixed/coherent chart data.

### Safe subcase A: transported whitening

If

\[
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
=
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\]

on the primitive \(D^2N_0\)-carrier, then

\[
C_Y^W=0.
\]

### Safe subcase B: fixed/coherent whitening mismatch

If the mismatch is a fixed low-dimensional function of chart/Gram data, then

\[
D_{\loc}C_Y^W=0.
\]

It enters

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\]

if it has a live projection, not \(B_Y\).

### Origin-killed moving-whitening artifacts

Terms like

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]\,DN_0[\xi],
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\eta_1,\eta_2]\,N_0,
\]

or

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]\,E_2
\]

are not primitive

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]].
\]

They should be killed by the typed quotient:

\[
\ell_9^Y(\text{moving-whitening artifact})=0.
\]

They also do not have live \(C_7\)-carrier origin, so

\[
\ell_7(\text{moving-whitening artifact})=0.
\]

## Failure mode

The bad case is atom-local same-point whitening recomputation:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
=
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}(\eta_p),
\]

with

\[
D_{\loc}
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
\right)\ne0
\]

on the primitive \(D^2N_0\)-carrier.

That would create local live-fiber amplitude and break rank two.

---

# 4. Combined rank-two-safe conclusion

If the three checks pass, then

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^L=
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^N=
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^W=0.
\]

Therefore

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=0.
}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

The remaining labelled mismatch has only the form

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}
\in
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}+R_X^Y.
\]

So labelled transition is rank-two-safe.

---

# 5. What still needs explicit coefficient checking

To turn the audit into a proof, we need three finite verifications.

## A. \(L\)-check

Show either

\[
D^2L=0
\]

on mixed-12 source slots, or

\[
D_{\loc}D^2L=0
\]

and

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}DN_0[D^2L]=0.
\]

## B. \(N_0\)-naturality check

Show

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-SD^2N_{0,\tr}
\]

is either zero or fixed/coherent:

\[
D_{\loc}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-SD^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)=0.
\]

## C. Whitening check

Show

\[
D_{\loc}
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)=0
\]

on the primitive \(D^2N_0\)-carrier, and that all other whitening artifacts are origin-killed.

---

# 6. Failure classification

If a check fails, route it as follows.

\[
\begin{array}{c|c}
\text{failure type} & \text{consequence}\\
\hline
\text{fixed/coherent mismatch} & \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\text{ grows; rank-two local still safe}\\
\text{residual-small mismatch} & EFCompletion_{2Y}\text{ required}\\
\text{local graph } \mathbf C(1,\rho_Y) & \text{rank two collapses to rank one}\\
\text{local vertical } \mathbf C(0,1) & \text{current }Y12\text{ route dies locally}\\
\text{local rank two fiber freedom} & \text{live fiber absorbed locally}\\
\text{origin-pair collapse} & \text{quotient must be refined}
\end{array}
\]

---

## Final status

The practical checklist passes structurally if:

\[
L,\quad D^2N_0,\quad D\mathcal W_{\same}
\]

are fixed/coherent chart objects and not atom-locally fitted.

Under that condition,

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}=0,
\]

so labelled transition does not obstruct \(B_Y=0\). Rank two remains viable.

## Agent 4 report — \(\mathrm{TypedCommutator}_{2Y}\)

No nomenclature changes. I use

\[

\]

The rank-two target is:

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

For labelled transition, the required theorem is

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=0,
}
\]

and more generally

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}
\in
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}+R_X^Y,
}
\]

with **no extraction-local live-fiber term**. This is exactly the local condition needed for rank-two \(D4\), not merely for rank-one \(Y12\). 

---

# 1. Labelled/raw mismatch

Define

\[
R_{\lab}
=
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T^{\lab}_{\tr\to\sing}
-
T^{\raw}_{\tr\to\sing}\operatorname{Forget}_{\actual}.
\]

Project to the two live coordinates:

\[
c_{\lab}(p)
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}(p)
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_{\lab,7}(p)\\
c_{\lab,Y}(p)
\end{pmatrix}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab}(p))\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab}(p))
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Rank two requires

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}=0.
\]

Here \(D_{\loc}\) means derivative with respect to extraction-local or atom-local choice/freedom. It does **not** kill fixed, global, or block-coherent chart data.

Thus the safe outcomes are:

\[
c_{\lab}=0,
\]

\[
c_{\lab}\in \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\quad\text{finite coherent},
\]

or

\[
c_{\lab}\in R_X^Y\quad\text{residual-controlled}.
\]

The unsafe outcomes are local

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),\quad
\mathbf C(0,1),\quad
\mathbb C^2
\]

fiber contributions.

---

# 2. Decomposition

Write

\[
c_{\lab}
=
c_{\lab}^{L}
+
c_{\lab}^{N}
+
c_{\lab}^{W}
+
c_{\lab}^{\trunc/\tail}.
\]

These correspond to:

1. nonlinear source-coordinate defect;
2. tensorial normal-form defect;
3. same-point whitening defect;
4. truncation/tail labelled-realization residual.

The goal is:

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{L}
=
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{N}
=
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{W}
=
0,
\]

with

\[
c_{\lab}^{\trunc/\tail}
\]

absorbed into \(R_X^Y\).

---

# 3. \(C_Y^L\): nonlinear source-coordinate defect

The defect is

\[
C_Y^L
=
DN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)].
\]

This is safe in either of two cases.

## Case A: \(L\) affine

If

\[
D^2L=0,
\]

then

\[
C_Y^L=0
\]

and therefore

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{L}=0.
\]

## Case B: \(D^2L\) fixed and origin-killed

If

\[
D^2L\ne0
\]

but

\[
D_{\loc}D^2L=0,
\]

then \(D^2L\) is fixed/coherent chart geometry, not atom-local freedom.

Moreover

\[
DN_0[D^2L]
\]

has \(DN_0\)-origin, not primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin. It is also not the live \(C_7\)-carrier origin. Therefore the typed quotient kills it:

\[
\ell_9^Y(C_Y^L)=0,\qquad \ell_7(C_Y^L)=0.
\]

So again

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{L}=0.
\]

## Failure

Rank two fails locally if \(D^2L\) is atom-locally fitted and survives in either live coordinate:

\[
D_{\loc}D^2L\ne0
\]

and

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}DN_0[D^2L]\ne0.
\]

Then \(R_{\lab}\) creates local fiber motion.

---

# 4. \(C_Y^N\): tensorial normal-form defect

The defect is

\[
C_Y^N
=
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}.
\]

The required condition is:

\[
D_{\loc}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)=0.
\]

## Best case: naturality

If

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
=
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2],
\]

then

\[
C_Y^N=0.
\]

## Safe nonzero case: finite coherent mismatch

If the mismatch is a fixed chart convention defect, then it may be nonzero but coherent:

\[
c_{\lab}^{N}(p)=\sum_a C_a\phi_a(p),
\]

with \(\phi_a\) nonlocal or block-coherent. Then

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{N}=0.
\]

It enlarges

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},
\]

but not \(B_Y\).

## Failure

If

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}
\]

or \(S\) is locally fitted per atom so that the defect has atom-local coefficients in \(e_7\) or \(e_Y\), then

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{N}\ne0.
\]

This kills rank two locally or collapses it to rank one depending on the image.

---

# 5. \(C_Y^W\): same-point whitening defect

The defect is measured by

\[
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\]

on the primitive \(D^2N_0\)-carrier.

The rank-two condition is

\[
D_{\loc}
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)=0
\]

on that primitive carrier.

## Best case: transported same-point whitening

If same-point whitening is transported functorially,

\[
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
=
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1},
\]

then

\[
C_Y^W=0.
\]

## Safe nonzero case: coherent whitening convention

If the two whitening conventions differ by fixed or block-coherent chart data, then

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^{W}=0.
\]

Again, this can enlarge \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\), but does not enter \(B_Y\).

## Origin-killed artifacts

Terms like

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]\,DN_0[\xi],
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\eta_1,\eta_2]N_0,
\]

or

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]E_2
\]

are not the primitive \(Y12\) object

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]].
\]

They should be killed by the typed quotient in the \(s_9^Y\) row, and they are not \(C_7\)-carrier-origin in the \(s_7\) row.

## Failure

Rank two fails if recomputed same-point whitening creates atom-local coefficients of

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0]
\]

or the \(C_7\)-live carrier.

---

# 6. Central transition modes

Central O2/O3 may require source-linear modes

\[
\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y],
\qquad d_{\max}\approx14.
\]

These modes may create raw Taylor drift, but their origin is source-linear/chart. Therefore:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
T_{\tr\to\sing}
\left(
\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y]\text{-source-linear modes}
\right)=0
\]

under typed quotient faithfulness.

They become dangerous only if raw transition recomputes \(N_0\) or same-point whitening with atom-local primitive output.

Thus a rich transition basis for O2/O3 remains compatible with rank-two \(D4\).

---

# 7. Truncation/tail part

The truncation/tail part does not need exact local zero if it is residual-controlled:

\[
\sum_p
\left\|
c_{\lab}^{\trunc/\tail}(p)
\right\|^2
\le
Q^{-B}\mu.
\]

If it has finite coherent component, include it in

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

If it has atom-local live-fiber freedom, rank two fails.

So the required routing is:

\[
c_{\lab}^{\trunc/\tail}
\in
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}+R_X^Y,
\]

not \(B_Y\).

---

# 8. Final theorem

## \(\mathrm{TypedCommutator}_{2Y}\)

Assume:

1. \(L\) is affine on relevant source slots, or \(D^2L\) is fixed/coherent and origin-killed.
2. \(D^2N_0\) is natural under labelled transition up to fixed/finite-coherent data.
3. Same-point whitening is transported functorially up to fixed/finite-coherent data.
4. The \(C_7\)-carrier quotient kills EF-linear/chart/background transition drift.
5. Truncation/tail labelled mismatch is finite coherent or residual-controlled.
6. No component has extraction-local coefficients in the live pair \((e_7,e_Y)\).

Then:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=0,
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}
\in
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}+R_X^Y.
}
\]

Therefore labelled transition does not obstruct

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

---

# 9. Failure routing

If a local live-fiber component remains, route it as:

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y):
\quad
\text{rank two collapses to rank one};
\]

\[
\mathbf C(0,1):
\quad
\text{\(Y12\) route dies locally};
\]

\[
\mathbb C^2:
\quad
\text{live fiber is locally absorbed};
\]

\[
\text{origin collapse}:
\quad
\text{refine typed quotient}.
\]

---

## Final status

The labelled/raw transition audit is now reduced to checking that the coefficient map

\[
c_{\lab}(p)
=
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab}(p))\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab}(p))
\end{pmatrix}
\]

has no atom-local derivative.

The structural sufficient condition is:

\[
L,\quad D^2N_0,\quad D\mathcal W_{\same}
\]

are fixed/coherent typed chart objects, not atom-locally fitted objects.

Under that condition, labelled transition is rank-two-safe.

## Agent 4 continuation — reducing \(\mathrm{TypedCommutator}_{2Y}\) to a local-Jacobian test

No nomenclature changes. I continue with the rank-two target:

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

For labelled transition, the needed condition is

\[
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=0.
\]

The latest coordinator prompt frames this as part of the rank-two route: formal D4 carriers are not in \(B_Y\), and local labelled-realization mismatch matters only if it creates extraction-local live-fiber freedom. 

---

# 1. Introduce local variables explicitly

Let each atom or patch \(p\) have local nuisance/freedom variables

\[
\eta_p.
\]

These are the variables that would make a contribution **local** rather than fixed or coherent.

Let global or block-coherent parameters be denoted

\[
\Theta.
\]

A labelled/raw mismatch coefficient has the form

\[
c_{\lab}(p)
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_{\lab,7}(p)\\
c_{\lab,Y}(p)
\end{pmatrix}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab}(p))\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab}(p))
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Decompose

\[
c_{\lab}(p)
=
c_{\fixed}(p)
+
c_{\coh}(p;\Theta)
+
c_{\res}(p)
+
c_{\loc}(p;\eta_p).
\]

Then

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}=0
\]

means exactly:

\[
\boxed{
\frac{\partial c_{\lab}(p)}{\partial \eta_p}=0
\quad\text{for every local atom variable }\eta_p.
}
\]

So the practical test is the local Jacobian

\[
\boxed{
J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}
:=
\partial_{\eta_p}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab}(p))\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab}(p))
\end{pmatrix}.
}
\]

Rank two requires

\[
\boxed{
J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}=0
\quad\text{for all }p.
}
\]

---

# 2. Split the Jacobian by defect type

Write

\[
J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}
=
J_{L,p}^{\loc}
+
J_{N,p}^{\loc}
+
J_{W,p}^{\loc}
+
J_{\trunc/\tail,p}^{\loc}.
\]

The intended rank-two-safe theorem is:

\[
J_{L,p}^{\loc}
=
J_{N,p}^{\loc}
=
J_{W,p}^{\loc}
=0,
\]

and

\[
J_{\trunc/\tail,p}^{\loc}
\]

is either zero or residual-controlled, not local live-fiber freedom.

---

# 3. \(L\)-part: source-coordinate transition

For the nonlinear coordinate defect,

\[
C_Y^L
=
DN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)].
\]

The local Jacobian is controlled by

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}D^2L.
\]

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
J_{L,p}^{\loc}=0
}
\]

if either:

\[
D^2L=0,
\]

or

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}D^2L=0
\]

and the origin

\[
DN_0\circ D^2L
\]

is killed by the typed quotient.

Thus the \(L\)-audit reduces to:

\[
\boxed{
\partial_{\eta_p}D^2L=0
\quad\text{or}\quad
\Pi_{\fib,Y}DN_0[D^2L]=0.
}
\]

The bad case is:

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}D^2L\ne0
\]

and this variation has nonzero \((\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)\)-projection.

That would inject local fiber freedom.

---

# 4. \(N\)-part: tensorial normal-form mismatch

For the tensorial defect,

\[
C_Y^N
=
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}.
\]

The local Jacobian is

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right).
\]

So the finite local test is:

\[
\boxed{
\partial_{\eta_p}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)=0
\quad\text{after live-fiber projection.}
}
\]

This holds if the normal forms and transition tensors are fixed/coherent chart data:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}=D^2N_{0,\sing}(\Theta),
\quad
L=L(\Theta),
\quad
S=S(\Theta),
\]

with no dependence on \(\eta_p\).

Then any nonzero mismatch is finite coherent:

\[
c_{\lab}^{N}(p)
=
\sum_a C_a(\Theta)\phi_a(p),
\]

and belongs to

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},
\]

not \(B_Y\).

The bad case is atom-local normal-form fitting:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}=D^2N_{0,\sing}(\eta_p)
\]

or

\[
S=S(\eta_p).
\]

Then rank two can fail.

---

# 5. \(W\)-part: same-point whitening mismatch

For the whitening defect, restrict to the primitive carrier and define

\[
\Delta W_{\same}
=
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}.
\]

The local test is

\[
\boxed{
\partial_{\eta_p}
\Delta W_{\same}
=0
\quad\text{on }D^2N_0\text{-origin carriers.}
}
\]

If whitening is transported, then

\[
\Delta W_{\same}=0.
\]

If whitening is recomputed from fixed/coherent Gram data, then

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}\Delta W_{\same}=0.
\]

If whitening is recomputed from atom-local choices, then

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}\Delta W_{\same}\ne0.
\]

That is the main risk.

Typed origin still kills nonprimitive whitening artifacts such as

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]DN_0[\xi],
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\eta_1,\eta_2]N_0,
\]

and

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]E_2.
\]

The only dangerous whitening term is atom-local variation of the coefficient of

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0].
\]

---

# 6. Truncation/tail part

For

\[
c_{\lab}^{\trunc/\tail},
\]

rank two does not need exact vanishing. It needs one of:

\[
c_{\lab}^{\trunc/\tail}\in \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\]

finite coherent, or

\[
\sum_p
\|c_{\lab}^{\trunc/\tail}(p)\|^2
\le
Q^{-B}\mu.
\]

The bad case is again local freedom:

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}c_{\lab}^{\trunc/\tail}(p)\ne0
\]

with nonzero live-fiber image.

So truncation/tail contributes to either amplitude Schur or \(EFCompletion_{2Y}\), not to \(B_Y\), if it is not locally free.

---

# 7. Compact theorem

## LocalJacobianTypedCommutator\(_{2Y}\)

Assume that for every atom \(p\):

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}D^2L=0
\quad\text{or}\quad
\Pi_{\fib,Y}DN_0[D^2L]=0,
\]

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)=0
\quad\text{after }\Pi_{\fib,Y},
\]

and

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)=0
\quad\text{on primitive carriers}.
\]

Assume also truncation/tail mismatch is finite coherent or \(L^2\)-small.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=0.
}
\]

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

So labelled transition is rank-two-safe.

---

# 8. What remains to check concretely

The checklist is now finite and explicit.

## \(L\)-check

Confirm:

\[
L=L(\Theta)
\]

with no \(\eta_p\)-dependence, and ideally \(D^2L=0\).

## \(N_0\)-check

Confirm:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing},\quad D^2N_{0,\tr},\quad S
\]

are fixed/coherent, not atom-fitted.

## Whitening check

Confirm:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same},\quad D\mathcal W_{\tr,\same},\quad S_W
\]

are fixed/coherent on primitive carriers, or transported exactly.

## Quotient check

Confirm typed quotient kills:

\[
DN_0\circ D^2L,
\]

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]DN_0[\xi],
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\eta_1,\eta_2]N_0,
\]

and all other wrong-origin artifacts.

---

## Final status

The rank-two labelled transition problem is now a local-Jacobian test:

\[
\boxed{
J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}=0.
}
\]

It passes if the transition tensors, normal-form Hessians, and same-point whitening maps are fixed/coherent chart objects rather than atom-locally fitted objects.

Under that condition, labelled transition cannot contribute to \(B_Y\), and rank-two remains viable.

## Agent 4 continuation — closing the local-Jacobian audit under pre-extraction chart fixing

No nomenclature changes. I keep

\[
\rho_Y,\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},
\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

For labelled transition, the rank-two-safe condition is

\[
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=0.
\]

The last reduction made this a local-Jacobian test. I now push the proof mechanism: **pre-extraction chart fixing** should make this Jacobian vanish. This is consistent with the current prompt’s rank-two focus and the warning that only local live-fiber freedom enters \(B_Y\). 

---

# 1. Separate chart variables from extraction-local variables

Let the transition data depend on two kinds of variables:

\[
\Theta=\text{global/block-coherent chart data},
\]

\[
\eta_p=\text{extraction-local atom freedom}.
\]

Rank two only forbids dependence on \(\eta_p\). It does not forbid dependence on \(\Theta\).

Thus the correct condition is not:

\[
\partial_\Theta c_{\lab}=0.
\]

The correct condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\partial_{\eta_p}c_{\lab}(p)=0.
}
\]

So if

\[
L=L(\Theta),\qquad
S=S(\Theta),\qquad
N_0=N_0(\Theta),\qquad
\mathcal W_{\same}=\mathcal W_{\same}(\Theta),
\]

then automatically:

\[
D_{\loc}D^2L=0,
\]

\[
D_{\loc}(D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr})=0,
\]

\[
D_{\loc}(D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}-S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1})=0.
\]

Hence:

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}=0.
\]

This is the key rank-two-safe mechanism.

---

# 2. Pre-extraction chart fixing theorem

The theorem should be:

\[
\boxed{
\text{All transition tensors are fixed before good-set extraction.}
}
\]

More explicitly, the following objects must be determined before atom selection and not refit on extracted atoms:

\[
L,\quad
S,\quad
S_W,\quad
N_{0,\tr},\quad
N_{0,\sing},\quad
\mathcal W_{\tr,\same},\quad
\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}.
\]

They may vary smoothly with the center \(m\), scale \(\lambda\), and chart class, but they must not contain arbitrary local coefficients selected to fit the D4/Y12 live fiber.

Then any mismatch they create is one of:

\[
\text{fixed},
\quad
\text{block-coherent},
\quad
\text{smooth coherent},
\quad
\text{residual-small}.
\]

It is not a local tangent direction. Therefore it belongs to:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}+R_X^Y,
\]

not to \(B_Y\).

---

# 3. Apply to \(C_Y^L\)

The nonlinear-coordinate defect is:

\[
C_Y^L=DN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)].
\]

If \(L=L(\Theta)\), then:

\[
D_{\loc}D^2L=0.
\]

If additionally \(D^2L=0\), the term vanishes. If \(D^2L\neq0\), it is coherent chart curvature.

But its origin is still:

\[
DN_0\circ D^2L,
\]

not primitive

\[
D^2N_0.
\]

Therefore:

\[
\ell_9^Y(C_Y^L)=0
\]

by origin filtering, and

\[
\ell_7(C_Y^L)=0
\]

by \(C_7\)-carrier filtering.

So:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^L=0.
}
\]

Failure requires both:

\[
D_{\loc}D^2L\neq0
\]

and

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}DN_0[D^2L]\neq0.
\]

That means transition is being locally refit in a live-fiber-visible way. That is a quotient/definition error for the rank-two architecture.

---

# 4. Apply to \(C_Y^N\)

The tensorial defect is:

\[
C_Y^N=
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}.
\]

If

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing},\quad L,\quad S,\quad D^2N_{0,\tr}
\]

are all functions of \(\Theta\), then:

\[
D_{\loc}C_Y^N=0.
\]

There are two safe outcomes.

## Exact naturality

\[
C_Y^N=0.
\]

## Finite coherent naturality defect

\[
C_Y^N(p)=\sum_a C_a(\Theta)\phi_a(p).
\]

Then:

\[
C_Y^N\in \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

Rank two remains locally safe. It may require vector amplitude Schur, but it does not enter \(B_Y\).

The no-go is:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}=D^2N_{0,\sing}(\eta_p)
\]

or

\[
S=S(\eta_p).
\]

That would make the normal form atom-fitted and can create live-fiber motion.

---

# 5. Apply to \(C_Y^W\)

The whitening defect is:

\[
\Delta W_{\same}
=
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}.
\]

If same-point whitening is transported:

\[
\Delta W_{\same}=0.
\]

If both whitening maps and transport are fixed/coherent:

\[
D_{\loc}\Delta W_{\same}=0.
\]

Then any surviving primitive carrier coefficient is finite coherent, not local.

Typed origin filtering also kills the common false positives:

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]DN_0[\xi],
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\eta_1,\eta_2]N_0,
\]

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]E_2.
\]

These are not

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0].
\]

Therefore:

\[
\ell_9^Y(\text{wrong-origin whitening artifacts})=0,
\]

and also:

\[
\ell_7(\text{wrong-origin whitening artifacts})=0.
\]

The no-go is atom-local recomputation of same-point whitening:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}=D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}(\eta_p)
\]

on the primitive \(D^2N_0\)-carrier.

That would create:

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}^W\neq0.
\]

---

# 6. Central O2/O3 transition modes remain safe

Central transition enrichment introduces source-linear modes:

\[
\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y].
\]

These can create raw jet changes, but their origin remains:

\[
\text{source-linear / chart-coordinate}.
\]

Under the two-grade typed quotient,

\[
\ell_7(\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y]\text{-modes})=0,
\]

and

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y]\text{-modes})=0.
\]

So central O2/O3 enrichment does not obstruct \(B_Y=0\), provided transition does not locally recompute \(N_0\) or same-point whitening from those modes.

This is a useful compatibility result:

\[
\boxed{
\text{rank-two D4 and central O2/O3 transition enrichment are compatible under typed transition.}
}
\]

---

# 7. Final rank-two-safe labelled theorem

## PreExtractionTypedTransition\(_{2Y}\)

Assume:

1. The transition maps and normal forms

\[
L,\ S,\ S_W,\ N_{0,\tr},\ N_{0,\sing},\ \mathcal W_{\tr,\same},\ \mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
\]

are fixed before extraction and depend only on global/block-coherent chart data \(\Theta\), not atom-local \(\eta_p\).

2. Typed origin quotients distinguish:

\[
DN_0\circ D^2L
\quad\text{from}\quad
D^2N_0,
\]

and distinguish moving-whitening artifacts from:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0].
\]

3. The \(C_7\)-carrier quotient distinguishes EF-linear/chart/background drift from live \(C_7\).

4. Truncation/tail mismatch is finite coherent or \(L^2\)-small.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=0.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

And:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}
\in
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}+R_X^Y.
}
\]

Thus labelled transition is rank-two-safe.

---

# 8. What remains genuinely nontrivial

The remaining checks are finite and concrete:

1. Prove \(L,S,S_W,N_0,\mathcal W\) are pre-extraction chart objects, not atom-fitted.
2. Prove the two-grade typed quotient preserves origin labels.
3. Prove \(C_7\)-carrier quotient kills EF-linear/chart/background transition drift.
4. Bound truncation/tail \(2Y\)-residuals.
5. If finite coherent mismatch remains, include it in \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\) and prove vector amplitude Schur.

---

## Final status

The labelled-transition obstruction has been reduced to a precise locality statement:

\[
\boxed{
\text{transition data may be coherent, but not atom-local.}
}
\]

If all transition tensors are pre-extraction chart objects, then:

\[
D_{\loc}c_{\lab}=0,
\]

and labelled transition does not contribute to \(B_Y\).

This materially supports the rank-two route.

## Agent 4 continuation — exact local obstruction if pre-extraction chart fixing fails

No nomenclature changes. I continue the rank-two labelled-transition audit. The current sufficient theorem is:

\[
L,S,S_W,N_{0,\tr},N_{0,\sing},\mathcal W_{\tr,\same},\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
\text{ are pre-extraction chart objects}
\]

and therefore

\[
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}
=0.
\]

Now I test the converse: if this fails, what is the exact obstruction?

---

# 1. The local obstruction is a live-fiber Jacobian

Let

\[
\eta_p
\]

be local fitting data. The labelled mismatch contributes to \(B_Y\) exactly through the Jacobian

\[
J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}
=
\partial_{\eta_p}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(R_{\lab}(p))\\
\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab}(p))
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Rank two requires:

\[
\boxed{
J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}=0.
}
\]

If not, classify the image of \(J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}\):

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}
\subseteq \mathbb C^2_{(s_7,s_9^Y)}.
\]

There are four cases:

\[
0,\qquad
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),\qquad
\mathbf C(0,1),\qquad
\mathbb C^2.
\]

For rank two:

- \(0\) is safe;
- \(\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\) collapses to rank one;
- \(\mathbf C(0,1)\) gives vertical local \(Y12\)-freedom and kills this route;
- \(\mathbb C^2\) locally absorbs the live fiber and kills this route.

So the exact local obstruction is:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{ran}J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}\neq0.
}
\]

---

# 2. Formula for the Jacobian

Using the decomposition:

\[
R_{\lab}
=
R_L+R_N+R_W+R_{\trunc/\tail},
\]

we have

\[
J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}
=
J_{L,p}^{\loc}
+
J_{N,p}^{\loc}
+
J_{W,p}^{\loc}
+
J_{\trunc/\tail,p}^{\loc}.
\]

The three structural pieces are:

\[
J_{L,p}^{\loc}
=
\partial_{\eta_p}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
DN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)],
\]

\[
J_{N,p}^{\loc}
=
\partial_{\eta_p}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)
-
S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right),
\]

\[
J_{W,p}^{\loc}
=
\partial_{\eta_p}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)
[D^2N_0].
\]

Thus the audit becomes:

\[
\boxed{
J_{L,p}^{\loc}=J_{N,p}^{\loc}=J_{W,p}^{\loc}=0.
}
\]

This is finite.

---

# 3. Failure of \(L\)-safety

Rank-two \(L\)-safety fails if:

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}D^2L\neq0
\]

and

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}DN_0[\partial_{\eta_p}D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)]\neq0.
\]

But \(DN_0\circ D^2L\) is not primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin, and not \(C_7\)-origin. Therefore this can only happen if one of two things is true:

1. the quotient loses the origin distinction;
2. \(D^2L\) itself is built out of the live package variables.

Thus, if \(L\) is a genuine coordinate transition, even nonlinear, this should be killed.

The failure means:

\[
\boxed{
\text{source coordinate transition is being locally fitted in live-package coordinates.}
}
\]

That would be a design failure for rank two.

---

# 4. Failure of \(N_0\)-naturality

Rank-two \(N\)-safety fails if:

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)
-
S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)
\]

has nonzero live-fiber projection.

This means the normal-form Hessian or carrier transition is being locally refit at atom scale.

If the normal-form mismatch is fixed or block-coherent, then

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}=0.
\]

So a nonzero local Jacobian means:

\[
\boxed{
D^2N_0\text{ is not a fixed normal-form tensor but an atom-local fitted tensor.}
}
\]

That would imply actual-source rows have local primitive \(Y12\)-package freedom. This is fatal unless reclassified as an explicit local actual-source family, in which case \(Y12\) is no longer forbidden.

---

# 5. Failure of whitening safety

Rank-two whitening safety fails if:

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)
[D^2N_0]
\]

has nonzero live projection.

This is the most realistic danger, because whitening can be recomputed from local Gram data.

But recomputed Gram data is dangerous only if it is atom-local. If whitening depends on block-coherent chart data \(\Theta\), then:

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}=0.
\]

So the fatal case is:

\[
\boxed{
D\mathcal W_{\same}\text{ has atom-local coefficients on the primitive }D^2N_0\text{ carrier.}
}
\]

If the dependence is fixed/coherent, it belongs to \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\), not \(B_Y\).

---

# 6. Truncation/tail mismatch

For truncation/tail:

\[
J_{\trunc/\tail,p}^{\loc}
=
\partial_{\eta_p}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\trunc/\tail}(p).
\]

This is safe if either:

\[
J_{\trunc/\tail,p}^{\loc}=0,
\]

or

\[
\sum_p
\|\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\trunc/\tail}(p)\|^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu.
\]

If truncation/tail has atom-local live-fiber counterterms, then it enters \(B_Y\) and may destroy rank two.

So the needed theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{tail/completion has no local live-package counterterm;}
}
\]

only fixed/coherent/residual components.

---

# 7. Compact no-go statement

The labelled transition theorem fails for rank two if and only if there exists a local parameter \(\eta_p\) such that:

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}(p)\neq0.
\]

Equivalently, the labelled/raw mismatch induces a local live-pair direction.

The outcome is then:

\[
\begin{array}{c|c}
\operatorname{ran}J_{\lab,p}^{\loc} & \text{consequence}\\
\hline
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y) & \text{rank two collapses to rank one}\\
\mathbf C(0,1) & \text{vertical local }Y12\text{ freedom; route dies}\\
\mathbb C^2 & \text{live fiber locally absorbed; route dies}\\
\text{different line } \mathbf C(1,\rho') & \text{second slope; route dies}
\end{array}
\]

This is an exact failure classification.

---

# 8. Positive theorem

## LocalJacobianTypedCommutator\(_{2Y}\)

Assume:

1. \(D^2L\) has no atom-local live-fiber projection:

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}\Pi_{\fib,Y}DN_0[D^2L]=0.
\]

2. The normal-form Hessian mismatch has no atom-local live-fiber projection:

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr})=0.
\]

3. The same-point whitening mismatch has no atom-local live-fiber projection on primitive carriers:

\[
\partial_{\eta_p}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}-S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1})[D^2N_0]=0.
\]

4. Truncation/tail has no atom-local live-fiber counterterm and is finite coherent or \(L^2\)-small.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

So labelled transition does not obstruct:

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

---

# 9. Final status

The rank-two labelled transition audit has reached a sharp form:

\[
\boxed{
\text{compute the local Jacobian }J_{\lab,p}^{\loc}.
}
\]

If it vanishes, labelled transition is rank-two-safe.

If it is nonzero, its image directly tells us the route:

- existing graph line \(\Rightarrow\) rank-one fallback;
- vertical or second slope \(\Rightarrow\) local \(Y12\) failure;
- full rank \(\Rightarrow\) live fiber absorbed.

This gives an exact finite decision procedure for the labelled transition component of \(B_Y\).
