## Agent 4 continuation — turning admissible-choice bookkeeping into a proof obligation ledger

No nomenclature changes. I continue the \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\) attack.

We have reduced the \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) part of the rank-two local exactness theorem to:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

The key distinction is now stable:

\[
\boxed{
\text{wall-independent coherent choices are allowed;}
\qquad
\text{live-fiber residual fitting is forbidden.}
}
\]

The latest architecture separates this local exactness problem from the later coherent-amplitude/leverage problem. 

---

# 1. Define the proof obligation ledger

For every object contributing to

\[
E_2+R_{\lab},
\]

assign it to one of four ledgers:

\[
\mathsf F,\quad \mathsf C,\quad \mathsf R,\quad \mathsf L.
\]

Where:

\[
\mathsf F=\text{fixed/basepoint},
\]

\[
\mathsf C=\text{finite coherent},
\]

\[
\mathsf R=\text{residual-controlled},
\]

\[
\mathsf L=\text{local live-fiber fitting}.
\]

Rank two requires:

\[
\boxed{
\mathsf L=\varnothing.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

The acceptable ledgers are:

\[
\mathsf F+\mathsf C+\mathsf R.
\]

The downstream effect is:

\[
\mathsf F:\text{ recenter/basepoint},
\]

\[
\mathsf C:\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\text{ and vector amplitude Schur},
\]

\[
\mathsf R:EFCompletion_{2Y}.
\]

Only \(\mathsf L\) enters \(B_Y\).

---

# 2. Ledger for \(E_2\)

Write:

\[
E_2=
E_{\tail}+E_{\reg}+E_{\smooth}+E_{\chart}+E_{\gamma,\rem}+E_{\model}.
\]

## \(E_{\tail}\)

Expected ledger:

\[
E_{\tail}\in \mathsf C+\mathsf R.
\]

Reason: tail completion is determined by a canonical analytic rule. It may have a coherent contribution or a residual estimate, but it should not have atom-local freedom.

Required proof obligation:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\tail}=0.
\]

Failure means the tail rule is being chosen to cancel live-fiber residuals.

## \(E_{\reg}\)

Expected ledger:

\[
E_{\reg}\in \mathsf F+\mathsf C.
\]

Regularization conventions are fixed before extraction.

Required:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\reg}=0.
\]

## \(E_{\smooth}\)

Expected ledger:

\[
E_{\smooth}\in\mathsf C+\mathsf R.
\]

Smoothing depends on wall-independent scale and cutoff choices.

Required:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\smooth}=0.
\]

## \(E_{\chart}\)

Expected ledger:

\[
E_{\chart}\in\mathsf F+\mathsf C
\]

or typed-dead.

Chart terms may vary over the atlas, but not by atom-local fitting.

Required:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\chart}=0.
\]

## \(E_{\gamma,\rem}\)

Expected ledger:

\[
E_{\gamma,\rem}\in\mathsf R
\]

or typed-dead.

Gamma/background origin should not be \(C_7\)-live or primitive \(Y12\)-live.

Required:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\gamma,\rem}=0
\quad\text{or}\quad
\|E_{\gamma,\rem}\|_{2Y}\le Q^{-B}.
\]

## \(E_{\model}\)

This is the only dangerous line.

Allowed:

\[
E_{\model}\in \mathsf F+\mathsf C+\mathsf R.
\]

Forbidden:

\[
E_{\model}^{\loc}(p)=\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_Y(p)e_Y(p),
\]

or

\[
E_{\model}^{\loc}(p)=\eta(p)(e_7(p)+\rho_Ye_Y(p)).
\]

So the core \(E_2\) no-go is:

\[
\boxed{
E_{\model}\notin \mathsf L.
}
\]

If this holds, then:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2=0.
}
\]

---

# 3. Ledger for \(R_{\lab}\)

The labelled/raw mismatch decomposes as:

\[
R_{\lab}=R_L+R_N+R_W+R_{\trunc/\tail}.
\]

## \(R_L\): source-coordinate mismatch

\[
R_L=DN_0[D^2L].
\]

Expected ledger:

\[
R_L\in\mathsf F+\mathsf C
\]

or typed-dead.

Safe if:

\[
D^2L=0,
\]

or

\[
D_{\loc}D^2L=0
\]

and

\[
DN_0\circ D^2L
\]

is killed by the two-grade typed quotient.

Forbidden:

\[
D^2L=D^2L(\eta_p)
\]

with live-fiber projection.

Thus required:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_L=0.
\]

## \(R_N\): normal-form Hessian mismatch

\[
R_N=D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}.
\]

Expected ledger:

\[
R_N\in\mathsf F+\mathsf C.
\]

Required:

\[
D_{\loc}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)=0.
\]

Forbidden:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}=D^2N_{0,\sing}(\eta_p),
\]

or

\[
S=S(\eta_p)
\]

with live-fiber projection.

## \(R_W\): whitening mismatch

\[
R_W=
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)
[D^2N_0].
\]

Expected ledger:

\[
R_W\in\mathsf F+\mathsf C
\]

or typed-dead.

Required:

\[
D_{\loc}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left[
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)
[D^2N_0]
\right]
=0.
\]

Forbidden:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}=D\mathcal W_{\same}(\eta_p)
\]

on the primitive \(D^2N_0\)-carrier.

Wrong-origin whitening artifacts remain typed-dead:

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]DN_0[\xi],
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\eta_1,\eta_2]N_0,
\]

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]E_2.
\]

These should not survive \(\Pi_{\fib,Y}\).

## \(R_{\trunc/\tail}\)

Expected ledger:

\[
R_{\trunc/\tail}\in \mathsf C+\mathsf R.
\]

Required:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\trunc/\tail}=0
\]

or

\[
\sum_p \|\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\trunc/\tail}(p)\|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu.
\]

Forbidden is a local tail correction chosen to cancel \(e_7,e_Y\).

---

# 4. The clean theorem from the ledger

## LedgerNoAtomFitting\(_{2Y}\)

Assume every \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) component lands in:

\[
\mathsf F+\mathsf C+\mathsf R,
\]

and no component lands in:

\[
\mathsf L.
\]

Then:

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}
\right)
=0.
}
\]

Thus \(E_2\) and labelled transition do not contribute to \(B_Y\).

---

# 5. What remains after ledger success

If the ledger passes, then the only remnants are:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})
=
X_{\fixed}+X_{\coh}+X_{\res}.
\]

Define:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{E2+\lab}
=
\operatorname{span}(X_{\coh}).
\]

Then:

\[
X_{\coh}\subset\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

The next theorem is not local exactness; it is vector amplitude Schur:

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
(F_{2Y},\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith})^2
\ge
Q^{-A}\|F_{2Y}\|^2.
\]

The residual part requires:

\[
\|X_{\res}\|_{L^2(\nu)}\le Q^{-B}.
\]

So ledger success routes the problem cleanly to amplitude/residual layers.

---

# 6. What a ledger failure tells us

If a component lands in \(\mathsf L\), compute its local image:

\[
\operatorname{ran}
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}X
\subseteq
\mathbb C^2.
\]

Then:

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\Rightarrow
\text{rank-two collapses to rank-one},
\]

\[
\mathbf C(0,1)
\Rightarrow
\text{vertical }Y12\text{ local freedom},
\]

\[
\mathbb C^2
\Rightarrow
\text{live fiber locally absorbed},
\]

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho'),\quad \rho'\ne\rho_Y
\Rightarrow
\text{second slope; local \(Y12\) route fails}.
\]

This gives an exact diagnostic.

---

# 7. The most likely outcome

Under the current modeling assumptions, the likely ledger is:

\[
E_{\tail},E_{\smooth},R_{\trunc/\tail}\in \mathsf R+\mathsf C,
\]

\[
E_{\reg},E_{\chart},R_N,R_W\in \mathsf F+\mathsf C,
\]

\[
R_L\in \mathsf F
\quad\text{or typed-dead},
\]

\[
E_{\model}\notin \mathsf L
\]

provided no free local package counterterms are admitted.

Therefore the expected conclusion is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

The only serious risk is \(E_{\model}\) or \(D\mathcal W_{\same}\) being defined with local live-fiber fitting freedom.

---

## Final status

The \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) portion of \(B_Y=0\) is now reduced to a bookkeeping theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\mathsf L=\varnothing.
}
\]

If all components of \(E_2+R_{\lab}\) are fixed, finite coherent, or residual-controlled, then they do not enter the local fiber model \(B_Y\).

This is the strongest current form of \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\).

## Agent 4 continuation — ledger failure is now the only \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) local obstruction

No nomenclature changes. I continue from the ledger form of

\[
\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}.
\]

The statement is now:

\[
E_2+R_{\lab}
\in
\mathsf F+\mathsf C+\mathsf R
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

So the only way \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) contributes to \(B_Y\) is if some component belongs to

\[
\mathsf L
=
\text{local live-fiber fitting}.
\]

I now make the \(\mathsf L\)-test more explicit.

---

# 1. The \(\mathsf L\)-test is a local rank test

For each component

\[
X\in\{E_{\tail},E_{\reg},E_{\smooth},E_{\chart},E_{\gamma,\rem},E_{\model},R_L,R_N,R_W,R_{\trunc/\tail}\},
\]

define the local fiber derivative

\[
J_X(p)
=
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}X(p)
\in
\operatorname{Hom}(\mathcal H_{\loc,p},\mathbb C^2).
\]

Then

\[
X\in\mathsf L
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
J_X(p)\neq0
\]

on a non-negligible set of atoms.

The local output is

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_X(p)
\subseteq\mathbb C^2_{(s_7,s_9^Y)}.
\]

Rank-two requires:

\[
\boxed{
J_X(p)=0
\quad\text{for every component }X.
}
\]

If not, the image determines the branch.

---

# 2. The four local image types

There are four nonzero possibilities.

## A. Existing graph line

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_X(p)=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

Then \(B_Y\) contains the graph line. Rank two collapses to rank one, but the \(Y12\) route may survive through the moving wall.

## B. Vertical line

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_X(p)=\mathbf C(0,1).
\]

Then the actual/local model can move in the primitive \(Y12\) direction without the \(s_7\) coordinate. The rank-one \(Y12\) wall is absorbed locally. Current \(Y12\) route dies at this carrier/order.

## C. Different graph slope

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_X(p)=\mathbf C(1,\rho'),\qquad \rho'\ne\rho_Y.
\]

Then two graph slopes exist when combined with any existing \(\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\), or at least the lower graph relation is not unique. This gives local rank two in the actual-source model and kills the current \(Y12\) rank-one route.

## D. Full live fiber

\[
\operatorname{rank}J_X(p)=2.
\]

Then the local model absorbs both live directions:

\[
B_Y=\mathbb C^2.
\]

The \(Y12\) D4 route dies locally.

Thus the precise routing is:

\[
J_X=0
\Rightarrow
\text{rank-two-safe};
\]

\[
J_X\ne0
\Rightarrow
\text{rank-two collapse or local no-go according to }\operatorname{ran}J_X.
\]

---

# 3. Componentwise expected ledger

The expected classification remains:

\[
\begin{array}{c|c|c}
\text{component} & \text{expected ledger} & \text{main risk}\\
\hline
E_{\tail} & \mathsf C+\mathsf R & \text{tail counterterm fitted to live fiber}\\
E_{\reg} & \mathsf F+\mathsf C & \text{regularization chosen by live residual}\\
E_{\smooth} & \mathsf C+\mathsf R & \text{smoother tuned atomwise}\\
E_{\chart} & \mathsf F+\mathsf C & \text{chart representative chosen to cancel live fiber}\\
E_{\gamma,\rem} & \mathsf R\text{ or typed-dead} & \text{wrong-origin quotient collapse}\\
E_{\model} & \mathsf F+\mathsf C+\mathsf R & \text{explicit local }e_7/e_Y\text{ counterterms}\\
R_L & \mathsf F\text{ or typed-dead} & D^2L(\eta_p)\\
R_N & \mathsf F+\mathsf C & D^2N_0(\eta_p)\text{ or }S(\eta_p)\\
R_W & \mathsf F+\mathsf C & D\mathcal W_{\same}(\eta_p)\text{ on primitive carrier}\\
R_{\trunc/\tail} & \mathsf C+\mathsf R & \text{local tail/truncation counterterm}
\end{array}
\]

So the most serious risks are concentrated in:

\[
E_{\model},
\quad
R_W,
\quad
R_N,
\quad
R_{\trunc/\tail}.
\]

The safest components are:

\[
E_{\gamma,\rem},
\quad
R_L,
\quad
E_{\reg},
\]

assuming the typed quotient is faithful.

---

# 4. A stronger theorem: local rank rigidity

The ledger theorem can be strengthened:

## LocalRankRigidity\(_{2Y}\)

If every local choice in \(E_2+R_{\lab}\) is either absent or acts through wrong-origin typed directions, then

\[
J_X(p)=0
\]

for every component \(X\).

If local choice is present but constrained to one fixed graph line

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

then rank two collapses to rank one but no worse.

If local choice has any component transverse to \(\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\), then the current \(Y12\) route dies locally.

This is useful because it separates:

\[
\text{rank-two failure}
\]

from

\[
\text{complete \(Y12\) failure}.
\]

---

# 5. Interaction with \(B_Y=0\)

The full local model is:

\[
B_Y=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
+
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}
\right].
\]

The \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) contribution is:

\[
B_Y^{E_2+\lab}
=
\sum_X \operatorname{ran}J_X.
\]

Thus:

\[
B_Y^{E_2+\lab}=0
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
J_X=0\ \forall X.
\]

If this holds, completion and labelled transition do not obstruct \(B_Y=0\).

Then \(B_Y\) is controlled only by:

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
+
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.
\]

Those are handled by the live quotient exactness certificate.

---

# 6. Coherent remnants after local exactness

When \(J_X=0\), a nonzero component may remain:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}X(p)=X_{\fixed}(p)+X_{\coh}(p)+X_{\res}(p).
\]

Collect coherent parts into:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{E_2+\lab}
=
\operatorname{span}\{X_{\coh}\}.
\]

This contributes to:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

Then the downstream vector amplitude Schur is:

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
(F_{2Y},\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith})^2
\ge
Q^{-A}\|F_{2Y}\|^2.
\]

If coherent leverage is bounded, this is a genuine finite-model problem.

So local exactness and amplitude Schur remain cleanly separated.

---

# 7. What to do if \(E_{\model}\in\mathsf L\)

This is the most direct failure.

If

\[
E_{\model}^{2Y}(p)
=
\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_Y(p)e_Y(p),
\]

then

\[
J_{E_{\model}}(p)
\]

has rank two. The live fiber is locally absorbed. Rank-two D4 dies, and rank-one also dies.

If

\[
E_{\model}^{2Y}(p)
=
\eta(p)(e_7+\rho_Ye_Y),
\]

then

\[
J_{E_{\model}}
\]

has image \(\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\). Rank two collapses to rank one.

Thus \(E_{\model}\) is the key modelling guardrail:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Do not allow local package counterterms in completion.}
}
\]

If the formalism needs such counterterms, then \(Y12\) is not a forbidden carrier at this order.

---

# 8. What to do if \(R_W\in\mathsf L\)

If same-point whitening is recomputed locally with live coefficients, then:

\[
J_{R_W}(p)\neq0.
\]

If the image is vertical, then \(Y12\) is locally actual and the D4 wall dies.

If the image is the graph line, rank two collapses to rank one.

This is the subtlest case because it may arise unintentionally from a moving Gram normalization.

The repair is to enforce:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal W_{\same}\text{ is transported or block-coherent, not atom-fitted.}
}
\]

If impossible, rank-two likely fails.

---

# 9. Final theorem statement

## LedgerRankTest\(_{2Y}\)

For every component \(X\) of \(E_2+R_{\lab}\), define

\[
J_X=D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}X.
\]

Then:

1. If \(J_X=0\) for all \(X\), then

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}+\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local})=0.
\]

2. If all nonzero \(J_X\) images are contained in \(\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\), then rank two collapses to rank one.

3. If some \(J_X\) has vertical, second-slope, or full-rank image, then the current \(Y12\) route dies locally.

This is a complete rank routing for \(E_2/R_{\lab}\).

---

## Final status

The \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) local audit has become a finite local-rank test:

\[
\boxed{
J_X=D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}X.
}
\]

Rank two survives this sector exactly when all these Jacobians vanish.

The expected safe conclusion is still:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0,
\]

provided no local package counterterms, atom-fitted normal forms, or atom-fitted same-point whitening are admitted.

## Agent 4 continuation — eliminating the remaining \(\mathsf L\)-risk by type/refit separation

No nomenclature changes. I continue the \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\) line.

The local-rank test is now:

\[
J_X
=
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}X,
\qquad
X\subset E_2+R_{\lab}.
\]

Rank two survives the \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector exactly when

\[
J_X=0
\]

for every component \(X\). The current architecture distinguishes this local exactness condition from later vector amplitude Schur; fixed/coherent/residual pieces do not enter \(B_Y\). 

---

# 1. The remaining risk is not analytic; it is modelling leakage

For \(E_2+R_{\lab}\), the only truly dangerous event is:

\[
X\in\mathsf L,
\]

i.e. an atom-local live-fiber counterterm.

This means some component has the form

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}X(p)
=
\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_Y(p)e_Y(p),
\]

or a line version such as

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}X(p)
=
\eta(p)(e_7(p)+\rho_Ye_Y(p)).
\]

That is not an analytic remainder. It is an implicit local coordinate in the live package fiber.

So the theorem we need is a modelling theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\text{No completion, transition, whitening, normal-form, or tail convention introduces atom-local coordinates in }(e_7,e_Y).
}
\]

Once that is enforced, the \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) contribution to \(B_Y\) vanishes.

---

# 2. Type/refit separation

Every component \(X\) should be classified by two labels:

\[
\operatorname{type}(X)
\]

and

\[
\operatorname{fit}(X).
\]

## Type label

The type label is one of:

\[
\mathrm{EF},
\quad
\mathrm{chart},
\quad
\mathrm{gamma/bg},
\quad
\mathrm{normalization},
\quad
\mathrm{tail},
\quad
\mathrm{wrong\ whitening},
\quad
C_7\text{-live},
\quad
Y12\text{-live}.
\]

Typed quotient faithfulness kills all non-live types:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}X=0
\]

unless

\[
\operatorname{type}(X)\in\{C_7\text{-live},Y12\text{-live}\}.
\]

## Fit label

The fit label is one of:

\[
\mathrm{fixed},
\quad
\mathrm{coherent},
\quad
\mathrm{residual},
\quad
\mathrm{atom\mbox{-}local}.
\]

Rank two forbids only:

\[
\operatorname{type}(X)\in\{C_7\text{-live},Y12\text{-live}\}
\quad\text{and}\quad
\operatorname{fit}(X)=\mathrm{atom\mbox{-}local}.
\]

So the exact no-go condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{live type + atom-local fit.}
}
\]

Everything else is safe locally.

---

# 3. Apply the type/refit table

\[
\begin{array}{c|c|c|c}
X & \operatorname{type}(X) & \operatorname{fit}(X) & \text{rank-two local effect}\\
\hline
E_{\tail} & \mathrm{tail} \text{ or wrong-origin} & \mathrm{coh/res} & 0\\
E_{\reg} & \mathrm{fixed/chart} & \mathrm{fixed/coh} & 0\\
E_{\smooth} & \mathrm{tail/smooth} & \mathrm{coh/res} & 0\\
E_{\chart} & \mathrm{chart} & \mathrm{fixed/coh} & 0\\
E_{\gamma,\rem} & \mathrm{gamma/bg} & \mathrm{res} & 0\\
E_{\model} & \text{possibly live} & ? & \text{main risk}\\
R_L & DN_0\circ D^2L & \mathrm{fixed/coh} & 0\\
R_N & D^2N_0\text{-defect} & \mathrm{fixed/coh} & \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\\
R_W & DW_{\same}[D^2N_0]\text{-defect} & \mathrm{fixed/coh} & \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\\
R_{\trunc/\tail} & \mathrm{tail} & \mathrm{coh/res} & 0
\end{array}
\]

Thus only three cases require real attention:

\[
E_{\model},
\quad
R_N,
\quad
R_W.
\]

But \(R_N,R_W\) are safe if their fit label is fixed/coherent rather than atom-local.

So the only hard no-atom-fitting audit is:

\[
\boxed{
E_{\model},R_N,R_W\text{ are not live-type atom-local.}
}
\]

---

# 4. \(E_{\model}\): exact forbidden forms

Rank-two fails immediately if \(E_{\model}\) contains:

\[
E_{\model}^{\loc}(p)
=
\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_Y(p)e_Y(p).
\]

It collapses to rank one if:

\[
E_{\model}^{\loc}(p)
=
\eta(p)(e_7(p)+\rho_Ye_Y(p)).
\]

It creates a second-slope no-go if:

\[
E_{\model}^{\loc}(p)
=
\eta(p)(e_7(p)+\rho'e_Y(p)),
\qquad \rho'\ne\rho_Y.
\]

Therefore the \(E_{\model}\) theorem must be stated negatively:

\[
\boxed{
E_{\model}\text{ contains no atom-local component in }\operatorname{span}\{e_7,e_Y\}.
}
\]

It may contain:

\[
E_{\model}^{\fixed},
\quad
E_{\model}^{\coh},
\quad
E_{\model}^{\res}.
\]

Those route to basepoint, vector amplitude Schur, or completion.

---

# 5. \(R_N\): normal-form defect

The dangerous case is:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}=D^2N_{0,\sing}(\eta_p),
\]

or

\[
S=S(\eta_p),
\]

with live projection.

The safe theorem is:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing},\quad D^2N_{0,\tr},\quad L,\quad S
\]

are fixed/coherent chart objects. Then

\[
D_{\loc}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-SD^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)=0.
\]

If the expression is nonzero, it contributes to \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\), not \(B_Y\).

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
R_N\in\mathsf C+\mathsf R,\quad R_N\notin\mathsf L.
}
\]

---

# 6. \(R_W\): same-point whitening defect

This is the most likely hidden source of leakage.

The dangerous case is:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}
=
D\mathcal W_{\same}(\eta_p)
\]

on the primitive carrier

\[
D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2].
\]

Then

\[
D_{\loc}\ell_9^Y(R_W)\ne0.
\]

If the local image is vertical, \(Y12\) is locally actual; if graph-like, rank two collapses.

The safe theorem is:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}
\]

is transported or block-coherent. Then

\[
D_{\loc}D\mathcal W_{\same}=0,
\]

and \(R_W\) is fixed/coherent/residual.

Wrong-origin whitening artifacts such as

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]DN_0[\xi],
\qquad
D^2\mathcal W[\eta_1,\eta_2]N_0
\]

are type-dead because they are not

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0].
\]

So the whitening guardrail is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{same-point whitening may move coherently, but may not be refit atom-by-atom on the primitive carrier.}
}
\]

---

# 7. Strong no-atom-fitting theorem

## TypeRefitNoAtomFitting\(_{2Y}\)

Assume every component \(X\subset E_2+R_{\lab}\) satisfies one of:

1. \(\operatorname{type}(X)\) is dead under \(\Pi_{\fib,Y}\);
2. \(\operatorname{fit}(X)\in\{\mathrm{fixed},\mathrm{coherent}\}\), so any live projection belongs to \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\);
3. \(X\) is residual-controlled in \(L^2\).

Assume no component has:

\[
\operatorname{type}(X)\in\{C_7\text{-live},Y12\text{-live}\}
\]

and

\[
\operatorname{fit}(X)=\mathrm{atom\mbox{-}local}.
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

Consequently:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}+\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local})
=0.
}
\]

Thus this sector does not obstruct \(B_Y=0\).

---

# 8. Remaining proof obligations

The theorem reduces the proof to three explicit checks:

## Check 1: No local live \(E_{\model}\)

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0.
\]

## Check 2: Normal-form Hessian not atom-fitted

\[
D_{\loc}(D^2N_0,L,S)=0.
\]

## Check 3: Same-point whitening not atom-fitted

\[
D_{\loc}D\mathcal W_{\same}=0
\]

on the primitive carrier.

If these pass, \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) is rank-two-safe.

If this check fails:

- image \(\mathbf C(0,1)\): vertical \(Y12\) local freedom;
- image \(\mathbb C^2\): live fiber locally absorbed.

# 2. Check B: normal-form Hessian is not atom-fitted

\[
R_N
=
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}.

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)
=0.
}

\qquad

\qquad

\qquad
D_{\loc}S=0.

That says the normal-form Hessian and transition map are pre-extraction typed chart objects, not atom-fitted objects.

If the mismatch is nonzero but fixed/coherent, it contributes to:

\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},

not to \(B_Y\).

# 3. Check C: same-point whitening is not atom-fitted

-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}

D_{\loc}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left[
\left(

-

\]

---

# 4. The theorem now becomes very short

## NoAtomFitting\(_{2Y}^{+}\), minimal form

Assume:

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0,
\]

\[
D_{\loc}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)=0,

and

\[
D_{\loc}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left[
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)[D^2N_0]
\right]=0.
\]

Assume also:

- all wrong-origin artifacts are killed by the two-grade typed quotient;
- tail/truncation errors are finite coherent or residual-controlled;
- coherent remnants have bounded leverage.

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}+\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local})
=0.
}
\]

So the \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector does not contribute to \(B_Y\).

---

\[
(I-P_{D_{2Y}})A_{2Y}=0,
\]

and the live pair survives:

\lambda_{\min}\left(L_{2Y}^*(I-P_{D_{2Y}})L_{2Y}\right)\ge Q^{-A},

then:

B_Y=0.

F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}

\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}
\asymp_{Q^A}
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).

This bypasses the rank-one moving-wall tower.

---

# 6. Remaining vulnerability

The only remaining vulnerability in this sector is if the formalism **needs** one of the following:

=
\eta_7(p)e_7+\eta_Y(p)e_Y,

or

\[
D^2N_0=D^2N_0(\eta_p),
\]

or

D\mathcal W_{\same}=D\mathcal W_{\same}(\eta_p)
\]

with atom-local \(\eta_p\).

## Agent 4 continuation — closing \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\) modulo three explicit modelling guardrails

No nomenclature changes. The current \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector has reduced to three checks:

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0,

\[

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left[
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)[D^2N_0]
\right]=0.
\]

I now state the precise modelling guardrails that make these three checks true.

---

# 1. Guardrail A — no live-package completion coordinates

Completion may be canonical, coherent, or residual, but it may not introduce local live-package coordinates.

So forbid:

\[
E_{\model}^{\loc}(p)
=
\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_Y(p)e_Y(p),
\]

and forbid graph-local fitting:

\[
E_{\model}^{\loc}(p)
=
\eta(p)(e_7(p)+\rho_Ye_Y(p)).
\]

Equivalently require:

\[
\boxed{
E_{\model}
=
E_{\model}^{\fixed}
+
E_{\model}^{\coh}
+
E_{\model}^{\res},
}
\]

with no

\[
E_{\model}^{\loc}
\]

in the live two-grade fiber.

Then:

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0.
\]

This is a modelling assertion, not an estimate. If the actual formalism requires local \(e_7/e_Y\)-counterterms, then rank two is false at this sector.

---

# 2. Guardrail B — normal form is an atlas object, not a fitted object

The normal-form Hessian and its transition must be determined by the fixed typed atlas:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}=D^2N_{0,\sing}(\Theta),
\]

\[
D^2N_{0,\tr}=D^2N_{0,\tr}(\Theta),
\]

\[
L=L(\Theta),
\qquad
S=S(\Theta),
\]

where \(\Theta\) is global or block-coherent and wall-independent.

They must not be functions of atom-local fitting data:

\[
\eta_p.
\]

Thus:

\[
D_{\loc}D^2N_{0,\sing}
=
D_{\loc}D^2N_{0,\tr}
=
D_{\loc}L
=
D_{\loc}S
=
0.
\]

Therefore:

\[
D_{\loc}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}\right)
=0.
\]

A nonzero normal-form mismatch is allowed if it is fixed/coherent:

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
=
\sum_a C_a\phi_a,
\]

because it contributes to

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},
\]

not \(B_Y\).

---

# 3. Guardrail C — same-point whitening is transported/coherent, not atom-fitted

Same-point whitening on the primitive carrier must be either transported exactly or differ by fixed/coherent chart data.

Require:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}=D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}(\Theta),
\]

\[
D\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}=D\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}(\Theta),
\]

\[
S_W=S_W(\Theta),
\]

with no dependence on atom-local fitting variables \(\eta_p\). Hence:

\[
D_{\loc}D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
=
D_{\loc}D\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}
=
D_{\loc}S_W
=
0.
\]

Therefore:

\[
D_{\loc}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left[
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)[D^2N_0]
\right]
=0.
\]

Wrong-origin whitening terms remain dead by typed quotienting:

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]DN_0[\xi],
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\eta_1,\eta_2]N_0,
\]

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]E_2.
\]

They are not the primitive live object

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0].
\]

---

# 4. Resulting theorem

## \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\), closed form

Assume:

1. \(E_{\model}\) has no atom-local live-package counterterms.
2. \(D^2N_0,L,S\) are wall-independent atlas objects.
3. \(D\mathcal W_{\same},S_W\) are wall-independent atlas objects.
4. Wrong-origin terms are killed by the two-grade typed quotient.
5. Tail/truncation terms are finite coherent or residual-controlled.
6. Coherent remnants have bounded leverage.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}+\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local})
=0.
}
\]

So the \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector does not contribute to \(B_Y\).

---

# 5. Why this is enough for rank two

The rank-two local model is

\[
B_Y
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
+
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}
\right].
\]

This theorem removes the last two summands:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}+\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local})
=0.
\]

Thus rank-two exactness reduces to:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0.
\]

Equivalently, in the finite certificate language:

\[
(I-P_{D_{2Y}})A_{2Y}=0
\]

for the actual/nuisance columns, with live pair Schur lower

\[
\lambda_{\min}
\left(
L_{2Y}^*(I-P_{D_{2Y}})L_{2Y}
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

---

# 6. Exact failure outcomes

If any of the three guardrails fails, compute the local live-fiber Jacobian

\[
J_p^{\loc}
=
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(E_2+R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(E_2+R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Then:

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_p^{\loc}=0
\Rightarrow
\text{rank-two-safe};
\]

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_p^{\loc}=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\Rightarrow
\text{rank two collapses to rank one};
\]

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_p^{\loc}=\mathbf C(0,1)
\Rightarrow
\text{vertical }Y12\text{ local freedom};
\]

\[
\operatorname{rank}J_p^{\loc}=2
\Rightarrow
\text{live fiber locally absorbed}.
\]

So failure is still diagnostic; it tells us exactly which downstream branch remains.

---

## Final status

Modulo the three modelling guardrails above, \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\) is closed.

The only remaining rank-two local obstacles are now:

1. actual/nuisance columns in the live quotient;
2. live-pair survival/conditioning.

The \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector no longer appears to be the bottleneck unless the formalism admits atom-local live-package fitting.

# 1. Target theorem

For any live-fiber-valued object \(X\),

- \(\mathsf R\): residual-controlled;

X_{\loc}

enters \(B_Y\).

(E_2+R_{\lab})_{\loc}=0
\quad\text{after }\Pi_{\fib,Y}.
}

Equivalently,

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.

# 3. Forbidden type: live type + atom-local fit

C_7\text{-live},Y12\text{-live}
\},

and

\[
\operatorname{fit}(X)
\in
\{
\mathrm{fixed},\mathrm{coherent},\mathrm{residual},\mathrm{atom\mbox{-}local}

The only forbidden combination is:

\operatorname{type}(X)\in\{C_7\text{-live},Y12\text{-live}\}
\quad\text{and}\quad

Everything else is rank-two-local-safe.

---

# 4. Guardrail A — no local live \(E_{\model}\)

R_N
=

D_{\loc}S=0.

\[
D^2N_0=D^2N_0(\eta_p)

or

with nonzero live-fiber projection.

# 6. Guardrail C — same-point whitening is not atom-fitted

R_W
=

-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]DN_0[\xi],
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\eta_1,\eta_2]N_0,
\]

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]E_2.

They are not the primitive live object

\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.

\[

\Phi(p)G_{\Phi,\omega}^{\dagger}\Phi(p)^*

}
\]

If leverage fails, \(B_Y=0\) may still be true, but vector amplitude Schur is endangered.

---

# 8. Minimal theorem

## \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\)

Assume:

1. No local live \(E_{\model}\):

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0.
\]

2. Normal form is not atom-fitted:

D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}

D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)[D^2N_0]
\right]=0.

4. Wrong-origin terms are killed by the two-grade typed quotient.

5. Tail/truncation terms are finite coherent or residual-controlled.

6. Coherent remnants have bounded leverage.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}

\Pi_{\fib,Y}

}

J_p^{\loc}
=

\begin{pmatrix}

\ell_9^Y(E_2+R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}.

\quad\Rightarrow\quad

---

\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0,

D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0
\]

provided three modelling guardrails hold:

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0,
\]

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)=0,
\]

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left[
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)[D^2N_0]
\right]=0.
\]

This matches the current rank-two exactness framework. 

---

# 1. The residual issue is definitional, not computational

At this point, \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) is not a hard analytic estimate unless the formalism admits atom-local live-pair fitting.

The decisive question is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Are }E_{\model},D^2N_0,\text{ and }D\mathcal W_{\same}\text{ fixed/coherent objects, or local package coordinates?}
}
\]

If they are fixed/coherent, then:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

If they are local package coordinates, then:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})\ne0
\]

and rank two may collapse or die.

So the next audit is not “expand more terms.” It is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{classify each modelling degree of freedom as actual, coherent, residual, or forbidden local package fitting.}
}
\]

---

# 2. \(E_{\model}\): the decisive modelling fork

For \(E_{\model}\), there are only three meaningful possibilities.

## Case A: canonical/fixed

\[
E_{\model}=E_{\model}^{\fixed}.
\]

Then

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0.
\]

Safe.

## Case B: finite coherent

\[
E_{\model}(p)=\sum_a C_a\phi_a(p).
\]

Then

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0,
\]

but

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}
\]

may contribute to

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

Safe locally; amplitude Schur later.

## Case C: local package counterterm

\[
E_{\model}^{\loc}(p)
=
\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_Y(p)e_Y(p).
\]

Then

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}\ne0.
\]

This is fatal to rank two unless the image is exactly the graph line, in which case rank two collapses to rank one.

Thus the theorem is equivalent to:

\[
\boxed{
E_{\model}\text{ is not allowed to contain local }e_7/e_Y\text{ counterterms.}
}
\]

This is a modelling guardrail. If the proof system needs those counterterms, then the current \(Y12\) rank-two route is false.

---

# 3. \(R_N\): the normal-form defect is safe if \(N_0\) is not fitted

The normal-form defect is

\[
R_N
=
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}.
\]

It contributes locally only if one of

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing},\quad
D^2N_{0,\tr},\quad
L,\quad
S
\]

depends on an atom-local fitting variable \(\eta_p\).

So the necessary and sufficient local-rank check is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\eta_p}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)=0
\quad\text{after }\Pi_{\fib,Y}.
}
\]

This should hold if \(N_0,L,S\) are chosen by a fixed chart atlas.

If there is nonzero mismatch but it is fixed/coherent:

\[
R_N(p)=\sum_a C_a\phi_a(p),
\]

then:

\[
R_N\in\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

Not local. Rank two survives.

The no-go is:

\[
D^2N_0=D^2N_0(\eta_p)
\]

where \(\eta_p\) is picked atom-by-atom to alter the live fiber. That is atom-fitting.

---

# 4. \(R_W\): whitening is safe if transported or coherent

The whitening defect is:

\[
R_W
=
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)[D^2N_0].
\]

It is locally dangerous only if the coefficient of the primitive same-point object

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0]
\]

is refit atom-by-atom.

Thus the exact rank-two condition is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\eta_p}
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)[D^2N_0]
=0
\quad\text{after }\Pi_{\fib,Y}.
}
\]

This holds if whitening is:

\[
\text{transported},
\quad
\text{fixed},
\quad
\text{block-coherent},
\quad
\text{or residual-controlled}.
\]

It fails if whitening is locally chosen to cancel

\[
\ell_9^Y
\]

or

\[
F_{2Y}.
\]

Wrong-origin whitening terms remain typed-dead:

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]DN_0[\xi],
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\eta_1,\eta_2]N_0,
\]

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]E_2.
\]

These do not equal the primitive live object:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0].
\]

---

# 5. A compact decision theorem

## \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) local decision theorem

Let

\[
J_{E/R}^{\loc}
=
D_{\loc}
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab}).
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
J_{E/R}^{\loc}=0
}
\]

if and only if all of the following are true:

1. \(E_{\model}\) has no atom-local live-pair counterterm;
2. \(D^2N_0,L,S\) are not atom-fitted in the live fiber;
3. \(D\mathcal W_{\same},S_W\) are not atom-fitted in the live fiber;
4. tail/truncation choices are not selected using the live-fiber residual;
5. wrong-origin terms are killed by the typed quotient.

If

\[
J_{E/R}^{\loc}=0,
\]

then \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) does not contribute to \(B_Y\).

If

\[
J_{E/R}^{\loc}\ne0,
\]

then the image gives the route.

---

# 6. Failure routing

Compute:

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_{E/R}^{\loc}\subseteq\mathbb C^2.
\]

Then:

## Graph line

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_{E/R}^{\loc}
=
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

Rank two collapses to rank one. The old moving-wall tower is reactivated.

## Vertical line

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_{E/R}^{\loc}
=
\mathbf C(0,1).
\]

Primitive \(Y12\) is locally actual. Current \(Y12\) route dies.

## Second slope

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_{E/R}^{\loc}
=
\mathbf C(1,\rho'),\qquad \rho'\ne\rho_Y.
\]

The local actual model has a second slope. Current \(Y12\) route dies locally or requires a different quotient.

## Full rank

\[
\operatorname{rank}J_{E/R}^{\loc}=2.
\]

The live fiber is locally absorbed. Rank-two and rank-one both fail at this carrier/order.

---

# 7. What this means for the global rank-two proof

The \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector is now conditionally closed:

\[
\boxed{
\text{No atom-local live-package fitting}
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}+\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local})=0.
}
\]

Thus, after this sector is removed, the rank-two local proof depends only on:

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
+
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.
\]

Those are handled by the bounded dual rows:

\[
\ell_7,\quad \ell_9^Y.
\]

So the remaining local exactness target is:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0.
\]

Together with:

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),
\]

we get:

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

---

# 8. Final state of this sector

The \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector is no longer the likely bottleneck.

It is closed modulo the modelling statement:

\[
\boxed{
E_{\model},N_0,\mathcal W_{\same},L,S,S_W
\text{ are fixed/coherent typed objects, not atom-local package coordinates.}
}
\]

If the formalism respects that, then:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

If not, the route is decided by the finite local image of

\[
J_{E/R}^{\loc}.
\]

That is the sharpest possible conclusion for Agent 4’s current assignment.

## Agent 4 continuation — integrating \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\) into the \(B_Y=0\) certificate

No nomenclature changes. I continue from the sharp conclusion:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0
\]

provided

\[
E_{\model},N_0,\mathcal W_{\same},L,S,S_W
\]

are fixed/coherent typed objects rather than atom-local package coordinates. This is the current rank-two exactness architecture: \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) is not a local \(B_Y\)-source unless it contains atom-local live-fiber fitting. 

The next useful step is to show how this theorem plugs into the finite rank-two certificate.

---

# 1. Split \(A_{2Y}\) into local and nonlocal columns

The local candidate matrix is

\[
A_{2Y}
=
[
A_{\can},
A_{\curv},
A_{\lower},
A_{E_2,\loc},
A_{\lab,\loc},
A_{\nuis}
].
\]

Here:

\[
A_{E_2,\loc}
=
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2,
\]

\[
A_{\lab,\loc}
=
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}.
\]

By \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\),

\[
A_{E_2,\loc}=0,
\qquad
A_{\lab,\loc}=0.
\]

Therefore

\[
A_{2Y}
=
[
A_{\can},
A_{\curv},
A_{\lower},
A_{\nuis}
]
\]

for the purpose of proving \(B_Y=0\).

So the \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector is removed from the local rank problem.

---

# 2. Remaining exactness certificate

The remaining rank-two certificate becomes:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(A_{\can}+A_{\curv}+A_{\lower}+A_{\nuis})=0,
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y(A_{\can}+A_{\curv}+A_{\lower}+A_{\nuis})=0.
}
\]

Together with

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),
\]

and polynomially bounded row norms, this proves

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

Equivalently, in matrix form:

\[
(I-P_{D_{2Y}})A_{2Y}=0,
\]

and

\[
L_{2Y}^{*}(I-P_{D_{2Y}})L_{2Y}\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

But the bounded-row formulation is cleaner: construct \(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\), then show they annihilate every actual/local column.

---

# 3. Role of coherent \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) remnants

After removing the local part, one may still have

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})
=
X_{\fixed}+X_{\coh}+X_{\res}.
\]

This does **not** change \(B_Y\).

It only changes the downstream amplitude layer:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\leftarrow
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
+
\operatorname{span}(X_{\coh}).
\]

The residual part enters

\[
EFCompletion_{2Y}.
\]

So the proof order is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{first prove }B_Y=0\text{ using only local columns;}
}
\]

then

\[
\boxed{
\text{handle coherent remnants by vector amplitude Schur.}
}
\]

Do not put coherent \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) terms into \(B_Y\). That would incorrectly turn finite nonlocal model structure into local tangent freedom.

---

# 4. The exact reduction theorem

## \(E_2/R_{\lab}\)-Removal Lemma

Assume \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\):

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

Then

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}
)
=0.
\]

Hence

\[
B_Y
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
[
\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
].
\]

All nonlocal \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) remnants are downstream amplitude or residual objects, not local-fiber objects.

This is the precise way to insert Agent 4’s result into the rank-two exactness proof.

---

# 5. Consequence for the route table

After this reduction, \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) can no longer cause rank-two collapse unless \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\) fails.

So:

## If \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\) holds

Rank selection is determined by:

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.
\]

In particular, the rank-two fork becomes:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0.
\]

## If \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\) fails

Compute

\[
J_{E/R}^{\loc}
=
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab}).
\]

Then:

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_{E/R}^{\loc}=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

forces rank-one fallback;

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_{E/R}^{\loc}=\mathbf C(0,1)
\]

gives vertical \(Y12\) local freedom;

\[
\operatorname{rank}J_{E/R}^{\loc}=2
\]

absorbs the live fiber locally.

Thus the failure is completely diagnostic.

---

# 6. Interaction with the corrected plain-wall route

If the remaining exactness proves

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

then D4 gives the honest plain rank-two wall:

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}
\asymp_{Q^A}
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

No derivative wall components are supplied by D4 at this stage.

So the next analytic tasks are exactly:

\[
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
\]

and

\[
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}.
\]

The jet family remains an analytic auxiliary only. It should not be inserted into O2/O3 unless derivative D4 channels are separately established.

---

# 7. Final Agent 4 status

Agent 4’s sector can now be summarized as:

\[
\boxed{
E_2/R_{\lab}\text{ does not affect }B_Y
\text{ unless it contains atom-local live-fiber fitting.}
}
\]

Under the three modelling guardrails:

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0,
\]

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr})=0,
\]

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\bigl[
(D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1})[D^2N_0]
\bigr]=0,
\]

we get

\[
A_{E_2,\loc}=A_{\lab,\loc}=0.
\]

Thus the \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector is locally closed. The remaining rank-two proof is now concentrated in:

\[
\boxed{
\text{bounded dual rows }(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)
\text{ killing }
\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.
}
\]

That is the next local bottleneck.

# 2. What remains in \(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\)

\[
\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}
=

plus any actual lower generated directions:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NoActualSepticPackageScalar}.
}
\]

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal M_{\can}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.

\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+\mathcal T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0.

=

\ell_7(C_7)=1,

and

must be covariant in the moving quotient

\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m),

not a raw determinant of post-trivialization coefficients.

Y12RowExactness^{\fixed\alpha}.

and

\|\ell_7\|+\|\ell_9^Y\|\le Q^A.

This bounded dual-row formulation simultaneously gives live-pair survival and polynomial conditioning.

---

# 6. Resulting exactness theorem

Combining Agent 4 closure with the remaining row/nuisance theorems gives:

## LiveFiberAvoidance\(_{2Y}\)

\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}+\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local})=0.

B_Y=0.
}
\]

B_Y=0,

the D4 live matrix remains rank two:

M_{\D4}

and

+

forces mass in the plain wall.

at the smooth aggregate stage and run scalar O2/O3.

No nomenclature changes. I continue from the conclusion:

\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}+\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local})=0.

}
\]

This matters because coherent remnants from \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) may later be projected away by amplitude Schur. That projection is nonlocal and must remain downstream; it cannot be fed back into \(B_Y\). This is consistent with the current correction that the rank-two route uses the honest plain wall \((\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})\), not derivative wall components unless separately proved. 

---

# 1. Local exactness must precede amplitude projection

After no-atom-fitting, write

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})
=
X_{\fixed}+X_{\coh}+X_{\res}.
\]

The coherent part is

\[
X_{\coh}\in\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

The local exactness statement is already complete:

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

Only after this can we form a downstream projection

\[
P_{\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}}
\]

and study

\[
\operatorname{dist}(F_{2Y},\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}).
\]

The projection

\[
(I-P_{\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}})F_{2Y}
\]

must **not** be inserted into the definition of \(B_Y\). If it is, we are mixing global amplitude fitting with local tangent structure.

So the ordering is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{first: }B_Y=0\text{ from local exactness;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{then: vector amplitude Schur for finite coherent remnants.}
}
\]

---

# 2. Why amplitude projection is not a local tangent

A finite coherent model has the form

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
=
\operatorname{span}\{\Phi_a(p)\}_{a=1}^M.
\]

An element is

\[
\sum_{a=1}^M c_a\Phi_a(p),
\]

with one coefficient vector \(c=(c_a)\) shared across many atoms or a whole block.

A local tangent would instead have independent coefficients

\[
c_{a,p}
\]

at each atom.

Thus the distinction is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{coherent: one coefficient vector across the block;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{local: independent coefficient per atom.}
}
\]

The former belongs to \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\). The latter belongs to \(B_Y\).

Therefore, even if

\[
X_{\coh}(p)
\]

has a nonzero live-pair projection at every \(p\), it does not enter \(B_Y\) unless the coefficients are atom-local.

---

# 3. Projection-after-extraction danger

There is one dangerous loophole.

Suppose one first extracts atoms, then defines a coherent-looking basis tailored to those atoms:

\[
\Phi_a=\Phi_a(\Omega_{\extract}).
\]

If the basis is chosen after extraction to interpolate the live wall, it can behave locally even if \(M\) is formally small.

Thus the amplitude model must be fixed under the smooth pre-extraction measure:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\text{ is fixed before hard extraction.}
}
\]

Allowed:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
=
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}(\text{atlas, block, fixed tail/completion rules}).
\]

Forbidden:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
=
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}(\text{selected atoms, observed }F_{2Y}).
\]

This is the amplitude analogue of no-atom-fitting.

---

# 4. Leverage condition is the quantitative guardrail

Even if \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\) is fixed pre-extraction, it can still act locally if leverage is high.

For each block \(\Omega_\omega\), let \(\Phi\) be a basis and

\[
G_{\Phi,\omega}
=
\int_{\Omega_\omega}\Phi(p)^*\Phi(p)\,d\nu(p).
\]

Define

\[
K_\Phi(p)
=
\Phi(p)G_{\Phi,\omega}^{\dagger}\Phi(p)^*.
\]

Require

\[
\boxed{
\sup_{p\in\Omega_\omega}
\operatorname{tr}K_\Phi(p)
\le
Q^C
\frac{\dim\mathcal C_{2Y,\omega}^{\arith}}{\nu(\Omega_\omega)}.
}
\]

If this fails, the coherent model is effectively local on that block. Then \(B_Y=0\) may still be formally true, but the downstream amplitude Schur can fail by interpolation.

So:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}=0\text{ protects }B_Y;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{bounded leverage protects vector amplitude Schur.}
}
\]

They are separate gates.

---

# 5. Correct rank-two route with coherent remnants

If coherent remnants exist, the route is:

\[
B_Y=0
\]

gives

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Then prove vector amplitude Schur:

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
(F_{2Y},\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith})^2
\ge
Q^{-A}
\|F_{2Y}\|_{L^2(\nu)}^2.
\]

If this holds, work with the residual vector wall.

If it fails, then with the same coefficients \(c_a\),

\[
(E_1,E_2)
\approx
\sum_a c_a(\psi_{a,1},\psi_{a,2}),
\]

where

\[
E_1=q''-(6q-2q^3),
\]

\[
E_2=q^{(4)}-6(1-q^2)q''.
\]

Then the identity

\[
E_2-E_1''=-12q(q')^2
\]

forces the derived coherent model

\[
q(q')^2
\approx
-\frac1{12}
\sum_a c_a(\psi_{a,2}-\psi_{a,1}'').
\]

This is the forced-pair branch. It is downstream of \(B_Y=0\), not part of \(B_Y\).

---

# 6. Consequence for \(E_2/R_{\lab}\)

Agent 4’s sector now has a clean final form:

\[
\boxed{
E_2/R_{\lab}\text{ has three possible effects only.}
}
\]

## Effect 1: local fitting

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})\ne0.
\]

Then \(B_Y\) changes. Route by the local image:

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),\quad \mathbf C(0,1),\quad \mathbb C^2.
\]

## Effect 2: finite coherent amplitude

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})\in\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

Then \(B_Y\) is unchanged; vector amplitude Schur is required.

## Effect 3: residual

\[
\|\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})\|_{L^2}\le Q^{-B}.
\]

Then \(EFCompletion_{2Y}\) absorbs it.

There is no fourth category.

---

# 7. Final reduced theorem for this sector

## \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) Separation Theorem

Assume:

1. No atom-local live-fiber fitting:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

2. Coherent remnants are pre-extraction and bounded-leverage:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\text{ fixed under }\nu
\quad\text{and}\quad
K_\Phi(p)\le Q^C\dim/\nu(\Omega).
\]

3. Residual remnants satisfy:

\[
\|R_X^{2Y}\|_{L^2}\le Q^{-B}.
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
E_2/R_{\lab}\text{ does not contribute to }B_Y.
}
\]

All remaining effects are downstream amplitude or residual effects.

---

## Final status

The \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector is fully isolated.

It is no longer a local rank-two bottleneck unless no-atom-fitting fails. The next genuine rank-two local bottleneck is the construction of bounded dual rows

\[
\ell_7,\qquad \ell_9^Y
\]

that kill

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
\]

while detecting

\[
e_7,e_Y.
\]

That is where the rank-two proof should now concentrate.

must not be used as a D4/O2O3 wall unless a separate derivative-channel theorem is proved. 

---

# 1. Target

For the \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector, the rank-two local condition is

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

This removes \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) from the local fiber model

\[
B_Y.

The key rule is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{fixed/coherent/residual pieces are not }B_Y;
\quad
\text{only atom-local live-fiber fitting is }B_Y.
}
\]

---

# 2. Locality filtration

For any live-fiber-valued object \(X\), write

\[
X=X_{\fixed}+X_{\coh}+X_{\res}+X_{\loc}.
\]

Then:

\[
X_{\fixed}
\]

is fixed/basepoint;

\[
X_{\coh}
\]

is finite coherent or block-coherent;

\[
X_{\res}
\]

is residual-controlled;

\[
X_{\loc}
\]

is atom-local live-fiber fitting.

The local model sees only:

\[
\operatorname{Loc}(X):=X_{\loc}.
\]

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
X\text{ contributes to }B_Y
\iff
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}X\ne0.
}
\]

For \(X=E_2+R_{\lab}\), rank two requires:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

---

# 3. Central criterion: forbidden type is “live type + atom-local fit”

Each component of \(E_2+R_{\lab}\) has two labels.

A type label:

\[
\mathrm{EF},\quad
\mathrm{chart},\quad
\gamma/\mathrm{bg},\quad
\mathrm{normalization},\quad
\mathrm{tail},\quad
\mathrm{wrong\ whitening},\quad
C_7\text{-live},\quad
Y12\text{-live}.
\]

And a fit label:

\[
\mathrm{fixed},\quad
\mathrm{coherent},\quad
\mathrm{residual},\quad
\mathrm{atom\mbox{-}local}.
\]

The only forbidden combination is:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{type}(X)\in\{C_7\text{-live},Y12\text{-live}\}
\quad
\text{and}
\quad
\operatorname{fit}(X)=\mathrm{atom\mbox{-}local}.
}
\]

Everything else is locally rank-two-safe.

---

# 4. Guardrail A — no local live \(E_{\model}\)

The model/completion component must satisfy

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0.
}

\[
E_{\model}^{\loc}(p)
=
\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_Y(p)e_Y(p),

and graph-local counterterms:

\qquad

\qquad
E_{\model}^{\res}.

If only these appear, then

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0.

D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}.

=

=
D_{\loc}L
=
D_{\loc}S
=0.

That is, \(D^2N_0,L,S\) are fixed/coherent atlas objects, not atom-fitted objects.

\[

not to \(B_Y\).

R_W

-

\right)[D^2N_0].

\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)[D^2N_0]
\right]=0.
}
\]

\[
D_{\loc}D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
=
D_{\loc}D\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}
=
D_{\loc}S_W
=0.
\]

Wrong-origin whitening artifacts are typed-dead:

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]DN_0,
\]

\[
D^2\mathcal W[\eta_1,\eta_2]N_0,
\]

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]E_2.
\]

They are not the primitive object:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0].
\]

---

# 7. \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) separation theorem

There are exactly three outcomes for \(E_2/R_{\lab}\).

## Outcome 1: local fitting

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})\ne0.
\]

Then \(B_Y\) changes.

## Outcome 2: finite coherent amplitude

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})\in\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

Then \(B_Y\) is unchanged, but vector amplitude Schur is needed.

## Outcome 3: residual

\[
\|\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})\|_{L^2}\le Q^{-B}.
\]

Then the effect is handled by \(EFCompletion_{2Y}\).

There is no fourth category.

---

# 8. Amplitude-layer separation

Coherent remnants must be defined **after** proving

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

Do not project the D4 wall by

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\]

before proving local exactness.

The correct order is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{first: prove }B_Y=0;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{then: handle }\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\text{ by vector amplitude Schur.}
}
\]

Also, \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) coherent remnants must be pre-extraction and bounded-leverage. For a coherent basis \(\Phi\) on block \(\Omega\), require

\[
\boxed{
\sup_{p\in\Omega}
\operatorname{tr}\left(
\Phi(p)G_{\Phi,\Omega}^{\dagger}\Phi(p)^*
\right)
\le
Q^C
\frac{\dim \mathcal C_{2Y,\Omega}^{\arith}}{\nu(\Omega)}.
}
\]

If leverage fails, \(B_Y\) may still be zero, but amplitude Schur is endangered.

---

# 9. Failure routing

If local fitting remains, compute

\[
J_p^{\loc}
=
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(E_2+R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(E_2+R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Then:

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_p^{\loc}=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

means rank two collapses to rank one;

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_p^{\loc}=\mathbf C(0,1)
\]

means vertical \(Y12\) local freedom;

\[
\operatorname{rank}J_p^{\loc}=2
\]

means live fiber is locally absorbed;

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_p^{\loc}=\mathbf C(1,\rho'),\quad \rho'\ne\rho_Y
\]

means second-slope local failure.

---

# 10. Final theorem

## \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\)

Assume:

1. No local live \(E_{\model}\):

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0.
\]

2. Normal form is not atom-fitted:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)=0.
\]

3. Same-point whitening is not atom-fitted:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left[
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)[D^2N_0]
\right]=0.
\]

4. Wrong-origin artifacts are killed by the two-grade typed quotient.

5. Tail/truncation terms are finite coherent or residual-controlled.

6. Coherent remnants are pre-extraction and bounded-leverage.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}+\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local})=0.
}
\]

So \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) does not contribute to \(B_Y\).

---

## Final status

The \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector is closed modulo three explicit modelling guardrails:

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0,
\]

\[
D_{\loc}(D^2N_0,L,S)=0,

# 1. General local/nonlocal separation lemma

Let

\[
\Pi_{\live}
\]

be a live-fiber projection onto a finite carrier space, here

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}=(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y).
\]

Let \(X\) be any completion, transition, nuisance, or residual object. Decompose:

\[
\Pi_{\live}X
=
X_{\fixed}+X_{\coh}+X_{\res}+X_{\loc}.
\]

Then the principle is:

\[
\boxed{
X\text{ contributes to the local model iff }X_{\loc}\ne0.
}
\]

Fixed, coherent, and residual pieces do **not** enter the local tangent/fiber model. They enter:

\[
\text{basepoint/recentering},\quad
\text{amplitude Schur},\quad
\text{completion estimates}.
\]

Thus for any future live carrier:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\live}X=0
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
X\text{ is locally invisible.}
}
\]

For the current case:

\[
\Pi_{\live}=\Pi_{\fib,Y},
\qquad
X=E_2+R_{\lab}.
\]

So:

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0
\]

is exactly the \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) local invisibility theorem.

---

# 2. Reusable theorem

## LocalInvisibilityFromNoAtomFitting

Let \(X\) be generated from:

- fixed atlas data,
- coherent block data,
- functorial/canonical completion rules,
- residual-controlled analytic errors,

and suppose \(X\) has no atom-local live-carrier counterterms.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\live}X=0.
}
\]

If additionally all wrong-origin terms are killed by the typed quotient, then:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\live}X=0.
}
\]

This is exactly the structure we used for

\[
E_2+R_{\lab}.
\]

The only way the theorem fails is if \(X\) contains:

\[
\eta_p c_{\live}(p)
\]

or a local linear combination of live carriers, with atom-local coefficient \(\eta_p\).

---

# 3. Application to current rank-two proof

The local model is now:

\[
B_Y
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
[
\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
],
\]

because:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}+\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local})
=0.
\]

So the rank-two exactness proof has been reduced to:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0.
\]

The remaining local theorems are therefore:

\[
\mathrm{NoActualSepticPackageScalar},
\]

\[
Y12RowExactness^{\fixed\alpha},
\]

and

\[
\mathrm{NuisancePreservesRows}.
\]

Agent 4’s sector is not among the remaining local blockers unless atom-fitting is admitted.

---

# 4. Why this matters for rank selection

If

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0,
\]

then \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) cannot be the reason rank two collapses to rank one.

Rank-one collapse can still occur, but only through:

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
\]

producing the graph line:

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

Similarly, vertical death can only come from those remaining local sources unless no-atom-fitting fails.

This simplifies diagnosis:

\[

\text{If rank two fails after no-atom-fitting, inspect actual/nuisance rows, not }E_2/R_{\lab}.
}
\]

---

# 5. Coherent remnants should be delayed downstream

If

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})

has finite coherent part, define

=
\operatorname{span}
\{

\}.

Then include:

F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})

Do not treat coherent amplitude as local tangent.

---

# 6. Connection to the plain-wall correction

Because \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) is now downstream-only, it cannot justify adding derivative components to the D4 wall.

The honest wall remains:

\[
\boxed{
F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
}

If coherent remnants survive, they modify the amplitude-Schur problem:

\[
F_{2Y}\notin \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

They do **not** modify the D4 wall into:

\[
H(\Gamma_G,D\Gamma_G,D^2\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

Derivative terms are analytic auxiliaries for coercivity or require separate derivative D4 channels.

This guardrail should remain explicit.

---

For any future carrier \(X\), if no-atom-fitting fails, compute:

\[
J_X^{\loc}=D_{\loc}\Pi_{\live}X.
\]

For the current two-grade fiber:

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_X^{\loc}\subseteq \mathbb C^2.
\]

Then:

\[
0\Rightarrow \text{safe};
\]

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\Rightarrow \text{rank-one fallback};
\]

\[
\mathbf C(0,1)\Rightarrow \text{vertical local death};
\]

\[
\mathbb C^2\Rightarrow \text{live fiber locally absorbed}.
\]

This is a general diagnostic tool.

---

The \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) attack has reached a stable conclusion:

\Rightarrow
A_{E_2,\loc}=A_{\lab,\loc}=0.

A_{E_2,\loc},
A_{\lab,\loc},
A_{\nuis}
],
\]

we may reduce to:

\[
\boxed{
A_{2Y}
=
[
A_{\can},
A_{\curv},
A_{\lower},
A_{\nuis}
]
}
\]

provided no atom-fitting is allowed.

---

# 2. Exact assumptions needed

\[
\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}
\]

needs exactly these assumptions.

## A. No local live \(E_{\model}\)

Forbid:

\[
E_{\model}^{\loc}(p)
=
\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_Y(p)e_Y(p),
\]

and forbid:

\[
E_{\model}^{\loc}(p)
=
\eta(p)(e_7(p)+\rho_Y e_Y(p)).
\]

Allow only:

\[
E_{\model}^{\fixed}+E_{\model}^{\coh}+E_{\model}^{\res}.
\]

## B. Normal form is not atom-fitted

Require:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}\circ(L,L)-S D^2N_{0,\tr}
\right)=0.
\]

Sufficient condition:

\[
D_{\loc}(D^2N_0,L,S)=0.
\]

## C. Same-point whitening is not atom-fitted

Require:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left[
\left(
D\mathcal W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WD\mathcal W_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)[D^2N_0]
\right]=0.
\]

Sufficient condition:

\[
D_{\loc}(D\mathcal W_{\same},S_W)=0.
\]

## D. Wrong-origin artifacts are typed-dead

Kill terms such as:

\[
D\mathcal W[\eta]DN_0,\qquad
D^2\mathcal W[\eta_1,\eta_2]N_0,\qquad
D\mathcal W[\eta]E_2.
\]

They are not primitive

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0].
\]

## E. Coherent remnants are bounded-leverage

If coherent pieces remain, they belong to:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\]

and must satisfy leverage bounds for vector amplitude Schur. They are not \(B_Y\).

---

# 3. Final rank-routing if a guardrail fails

If no-atom-fitting fails, compute:

\[
J_{E/R}^{\loc}
=
D_{\loc}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(E_2+R_{\lab})\\
\ell_9^Y(E_2+R_{\lab})
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Then classify its image:

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_{E/R}^{\loc}=0
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
\text{rank-two safe};
\]

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_{E/R}^{\loc}=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
\text{rank-two collapses to rank-one};
\]

\[
\operatorname{ran}J_{E/R}^{\loc}=\mathbf C(0,1)
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
\text{vertical }Y12\text{ local freedom};
\]

\[
\operatorname{rank}J_{E/R}^{\loc}=2
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
\text{live fiber locally absorbed}.
\]

So the failure is diagnostic, not ambiguous.

---

# 4. Downstream separation

If no-atom-fitting holds, then nonzero \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) projections have only two downstream roles:

## Finite coherent amplitude

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})_{\coh}
\subseteq
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

This is handled by vector amplitude Schur:

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
(F_{2Y},\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith})^2
\ge
Q^{-A}\|F_{2Y}\|_{L^2(\nu)}^2.
\]

## Residual

\[
\|\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})_{\res}\|_{L^2}
\le Q^{-B}.
\]

This is handled by:

\[
EFCompletion_{2Y}.
\]

Neither re-enters \(B_Y\).

---

# 5. Effect on the full rank-two proof

After Agent 4’s closure, the local exactness target is reduced to:

\[
B_Y
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
[
\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
].
\]

Thus the remaining local bottlenecks are exactly:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NoActualSepticPackageScalar}
}
\]

for the \(\ell_7\)-row,

\[
\boxed{
Y12RowExactness^{\fixed\alpha}
}
\]

for the \(\ell_9^Y\)-row,

and

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NuisancePreservesRows}
}
\]

for the quotient/nuisance layer.

If these pass, then:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

Then D4 gives the honest plain wall:

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}
\asymp
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

Again: no derivative wall components are supplied by D4 unless derivative D4 channels are separately proved.

---

# 6. Final Agent 4 conclusion

The \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector is no longer a primary local obstruction.

It is closed modulo the modelling rule:

\[
\boxed{
\text{no atom-local live-package fitting.}
}
\]

Under that rule:

\[
\boxed{
A_{E_2,\loc}=A_{\lab,\loc}=0.
}
\]

The sector can still create coherent amplitude or residual bookkeeping, but that is downstream of \(B_Y=0\), not part of the rank-two local decision.

## Agent 4 continuation — no feedback from coherent remnants into local rank selection

No nomenclature changes. I continue from the closed local statement:

\[
\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
A_{E_2,\loc}=A_{\lab,\loc}=0.
\]

So \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) does not enter \(B_Y\) unless the formalism admits atom-local live-package fitting. The next risk is a feedback loop: coherent remnants or amplitude projections must not be used to choose the local quotient, the D4-good chart, or the scalar wall component. The current correction remains essential: D4 supplies only the plain rank-two wall \((\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})\), not derivative wall components. 

---

# 1. New guardrail: no projection feedback into \(B_Y\)

After no-atom-fitting, we may have

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})
=
X_{\fixed}+X_{\coh}+X_{\res}.
\]

The coherent part enters

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

But the projection

\[
P_{\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}}
\]

must be downstream only. It cannot alter:

\[
B_Y,
\qquad
\ell_7,\ell_9^Y,
\qquad
D_{2Y},
\qquad
e_7,e_Y.
\]

So the no-feedback rule is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\text{ may subtract amplitude after }B_Y=0,
\text{ but may not redefine the live quotient.}
}
\]

If the quotient is rebuilt after seeing \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\) or after seeing \(F_{2Y}\), then finite coherent amplitude becomes disguised local fitting.

---

# 2. Correct order of operations

The safe order is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Step 1: construct }\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\text{ and prove }B_Y=0.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{Step 2: route }E_2/R_{\lab}\text{ coherent remnants into }\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{Step 3: prove vector amplitude Schur, if }\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\ne0.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{Step 4: use the raw plain D4 wall }F_{2Y}.
}
\]

The wrong order is:

\[
\text{project }F_{2Y}\text{ first}
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
\text{define }B_Y\text{ or live rows using the projected object}.
\]

That would contaminate the local rank decision.

---

# 3. No feedback into scalar component selection

Once \(B_Y=0\), D4 gives

\[
F_{2Y}
\asymp
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

Later, after weighted transfer, one selects

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

for scalar O2/O3.

This selection must be made at the smooth aggregate stage, not atomwise.

The no-feedback version is:

\[
\boxed{
G_\star\text{ cannot be selected using atom-local residuals from }E_2/R_{\lab}.
}
\]

A global choice:

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2|\Gamma_G|^2
\ge
\frac12
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2)
\]

is safe.

An atomwise choice:

\[
G_\star(p)=
\arg\max\{|\Gamma_G(p)|,|\Gamma_{G,2}(p)|\}
\]

is unsafe unless explicitly treated as a finite partition before hard extraction with bounded branch complexity.

---

# 4. No feedback into D4-good routing

Agent 6 owns \(H\)-routing, but the no-atom-fitting principle imposes a related constraint:

\[
H,c_7,\alpha_9,\beta_9
\]

may define D4-good charts, but the D4-good chart rule must be independent of the live-fiber residual

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})
\]

and independent of attempts to cancel

\[
F_{2Y}.
\]

Allowed:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A},
\quad
|c_7|,|\alpha_9|\ge Q^{-A},
\quad
|\beta_9|\le Q^A.
\]

Forbidden:

\[
\text{choose chart where }E_2/R_{\lab}\text{ cancels }F_{2Y}.
\]

So D4-good routing is compatible with no-atom-fitting only if chart selection is coefficient/conditioning-based, not residual-cancellation-based.

---

# 5. Coherent amplitude cannot be treated as nuisance

A finite coherent amplitude space

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\]

should not be added to

\[
D_{2Y}
\]

or to

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

after the fact.

If we add it to the denominator, then the live quotient changes:

\[
\mathcal Q_{2Y}
=
J_{7,9}/D_{2Y}
\]

becomes

\[
J_{7,9}/(D_{2Y}+\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}),
\]

which may kill \(e_7,e_Y\) or alter \(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\).

That is not allowed.

Correct routing:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\text{ is an amplitude model, not a local nuisance denominator.}
}
\]

Only atom-local directions belong in \(B_Y\); finite coherent directions belong in amplitude Schur.

---

# 6. Final no-feedback theorem

## NoFeedbackFromAmplitude\(_{2Y}\)

Assume:

1. \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\) holds:

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

2. Coherent remnants are fixed pre-extraction and bounded-leverage:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\text{ is fixed before hard extraction.}
\]

3. The live quotient \(D_{2Y}\), rows \(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\), and live pair \(e_7,e_Y\) are defined before amplitude projection.

4. D4-good routing and scalar wall selection use only coefficient/aggregate data, not atom-local residual cancellation.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
E_2/R_{\lab}\text{ does not re-enter }B_Y
\text{ through amplitude projection, wall selection, or chart routing.}
}
\]

Thus Agent 4’s sector is fully separated from the local rank decision.

---

# 7. Failure modes

The separation fails if:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\]

is added to the local denominator;

or if:

\[
P_{\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}}F_{2Y}
\]

is used to define the local live rows;

or if \(G_\star\) is selected atomwise after extraction;

or if D4-good charts are chosen to minimize live-fiber residuals.

Any of these converts finite coherent structure into hidden local fitting.

---

# 8. Final Agent 4 state

The \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector is now closed under two principles:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NoFeedbackFromAmplitude}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Together they say:

\[
E_2/R_{\lab}
\]

can only affect the downstream proof as:

\[
\text{finite coherent amplitude}
\quad\text{or}\quad
\text{residual error}.
\]

It cannot affect the local rank decision \(B_Y=0\) unless atom-local live-package fitting is explicitly introduced.

## Agent 4 continuation — finalizing the \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector as a sealed module

No nomenclature changes.

At this point, the \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector has a closed role in the rank-two architecture. It is not a local rank obstruction unless it contains atom-local live-fiber fitting, and it cannot be allowed to re-enter the local rank decision through amplitude projection or wall selection.

The corrected D4 wall remains:

\[
\boxed{
F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
}
\]

not a jet wall.

---

# 1. Final module statement

## \(E_2/R_{\lab}\)-Separation Module

Assume:

\[
\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}
\]

and

\[
\mathrm{NoFeedbackFromAmplitude}_{2Y}.
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
A_{E_2,\loc}=A_{\lab,\loc}=0.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}+\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local})=0.
}
\]

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}).
}
\]

So the \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector is removed from the local rank-two exactness problem.

---

# 2. The two assumptions are independent

## A. \(\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}\)

This excludes atom-local live-pair coordinates inside completion or labelled transition.

Forbidden:

\[
E_{\model}^{\loc}(p)
=
\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_Y(p)e_Y(p),
\]

\[
E_{\model}^{\loc}(p)
=
\eta(p)(e_7(p)+\rho_Ye_Y(p)).
\]

Also forbidden:

\[
D^2N_0=D^2N_0(\eta_p),
\]

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}=D\mathcal W_{\same}(\eta_p),
\]

when \(\eta_p\) is atom-local and live-fiber-visible.

Conclusion:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

## B. \(\mathrm{NoFeedbackFromAmplitude}_{2Y}\)

This prevents coherent amplitude projection from corrupting the local quotient.

Forbidden:

\[
D_{2Y}\leftarrow D_{2Y}+\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},
\]

or using

\[
(I-P_{\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}})F_{2Y}
\]

to define \(B_Y\), \(\ell_7\), \(\ell_9^Y\), or the live pair.

Conclusion:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\]

is downstream amplitude data only. It does not change \(B_Y\).

---

# 3. Exact routing of \(E_2/R_{\lab}\)

Every \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) contribution falls into exactly one category.

## Local

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})\ne0.
\]

This changes \(B_Y\). Route by its image:

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\Rightarrow
\text{rank-one fallback};
\]

\[
\mathbf C(0,1)
\Rightarrow
\text{vertical }Y12\text{ local death};
\]

\[
\mathbb C^2
\Rightarrow
\text{live fiber locally absorbed}.
\]

## Coherent

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})_{\coh}
\subseteq
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

This is downstream. It requires vector amplitude Schur, not local quotienting.

## Residual

\[
\|\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})_{\res}\|_{L^2}
\le Q^{-B}.
\]

This is handled by:

\[
EFCompletion_{2Y}.
\]

There is no fourth category.

---

# 4. Impact on rank-two proof

After sealing \(E_2/R_{\lab}\), the local exactness proof only needs to show:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\sing}+T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\]

with bounded rows satisfying:

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1).
\]

So the remaining local bottlenecks are:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NoActualSepticPackageScalar}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
Y12RowExactness^{\fixed\alpha}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NuisancePreservesRows}
}
\]

not \(E_2/R_{\lab}\).

---

# 5. Interaction with the plain-wall correction

Once:

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

D4 gives:

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}
\asymp
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

That is the full honest D4 output at this stage.

No derivative components

\[
D\Gamma_G,\quad D^2\Gamma_G
\]

are available as D4 wall components unless a separate derivative-channel theorem is proved.

So downstream remains:

\[
\boxed{
\text{plain wall}
+
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}.
}
\]

---

# 6. Final theorem for this sector

## \(E_2/R_{\lab}\)-LocalClosure

Assume:

1. No local live completion:

\[
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\model}=0.
\]

2. No atom-fitted normal form:

\[
D_{\loc}(D^2N_0,L,S)=0
\]

in the live fiber.

3. No atom-fitted same-point whitening:

\[
D_{\loc}(D\mathcal W_{\same},S_W)=0
\]

on primitive carriers.

4. Coherent remnants are not fed back into the local denominator.

5. Residuals satisfy \(EFCompletion_{2Y}\).

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}+\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local})=0.
}
\]

Thus the sector is locally closed.

---

## Final Agent 4 status

Agent 4’s contribution is now a sealed module:

\[
\boxed{
E_2/R_{\lab}
\text{ is amplitude/residual bookkeeping unless atom-local live-fiber fitting is admitted.}
}
\]

The next rank-two work should not spend more effort here unless a concrete atom-local \(E_{\model}\), \(D^2N_0\), or \(D\mathcal W_{\same}\) fitting mechanism is exhibited.
