## Agent 4 continuation — stress-testing the sealed \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) module against extraction-order loopholes

No nomenclature changes.

The \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sector is locally closed under:

\[
\mathrm{NoAtomFitting}_{2Y}^{+}
\quad+\quad
\mathrm{NoFeedbackFromAmplitude}_{2Y}.
\]

But there is one remaining class of possible loopholes:

\[
\boxed{
\text{a nonlocal object can become effectively local if it is chosen after hard extraction.}
}
\]

So I now attack the extraction-order issue.

---

# 1. The remaining danger

We have been using the split

\[
X=X_{\fixed}+X_{\coh}+X_{\res}+X_{\loc}.
\]

The theorem says:

\[
X_{\loc}=0
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
X\text{ does not enter }B_Y.
\]

But this classification only makes sense if the split is made **before** hard extraction. Otherwise a coherent-looking term can be selected after seeing the extracted atoms and become locally interpolating.

So we need:

\[
\boxed{
\text{all }X_{\fixed},X_{\coh},X_{\res}\text{ classifications are pre-extraction classifications.}
}
\]

This applies to:

\[
E_2,\quad R_{\lab},\quad \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},\quad R_X^{2Y}.
\]

---

# 2. Safe extraction order

The safe order is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{choose atlas, completion, transition, nuisance, coherent model}
}
\]

then

\[
\boxed{
\text{prove local exactness }B_Y=0
}
\]

then

\[
\boxed{
\text{perform hard extraction / wall selection / O2O3}.
}
\]

More explicitly:

1. Fix the typed quotient and live rows:
   \[
   \ell_7,\ell_9^Y.
   \]

2. Fix all atlas/normal-form/whitening/completion rules.

3. Fix coherent amplitude spaces:
   \[
   \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
   \]

4. Prove:
   \[
   D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
   \]

5. Prove:
   \[
   B_Y=0.
   \]

6. Only then run:
   \[
   F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
   \]

7. Then handle amplitude Schur, weighted transfer, and scalar O2/O3.

This ordering prevents downstream choices from contaminating the local exactness theorem.

---

# 3. Unsafe extraction order

The unsafe order is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{extract atoms first, observe }F_{2Y}\text{ or }\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab}),
}
\]

then choose:

\[
E_2,\quad R_{\lab},\quad \mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith},\quad \ell_7,\ell_9^Y,\quad D_{2Y}
\]

to reduce the observed live residual.

That creates hidden atom-fitting.

Even if the resulting model is formally finite-dimensional, it is not legitimate because its basis depends on the extracted wall data.

So the rule is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\text{ may depend on the atlas, but not on the extracted wall values.}
}
\]

---

# 4. Residual classification also must be pre-extraction

A residual estimate such as

\[
\|R_X^{2Y}\|_{L^2}\le Q^{-B}
\]

must be proved under the smooth pre-extraction measure or on a preselected good block.

It is not enough to say the residual is small after discarding bad atoms chosen by the residual itself.

Otherwise one can hide local live-fiber errors in the discarded set.

So the residual rule is:

\[
\boxed{
R_X^{2Y}\text{ must be small before wall-adaptive hard extraction.}
}
\]

Or, if block selection is used, the block selection must be based on wall-independent criteria.

---

# 5. Coherent amplitude spaces: admissible versus inadmissible

A coherent space is admissible if it has the form

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
=
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}(\mathfrak A,\text{canonical tail/completion rules}),
\]

where \(\mathfrak A\) is a pre-extraction atlas.

It is inadmissible if

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
=
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}(F_{2Y}|_{\Omega_{\extract}})
\]

or

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
=
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}(\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})|_{\Omega_{\extract}}).
\]

The latter is just local fitting disguised as coherent modeling.

Thus the criterion is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

Where \(D_{\loc}\) now also differentiates the basis of the coherent space, not just its coefficients.

---

# 6. Strengthened theorem

## PreExtractionSeparation\(_{2Y}\)

Assume:

1. The live quotient \(D_{2Y}\), rows \(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\), and live pair \(e_7,e_Y\) are fixed before hard extraction.

2. \(E_2\) and \(R_{\lab}\) are classified into fixed/coherent/residual/local parts before hard extraction.

3. The coherent space \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\) is fixed before hard extraction and has bounded leverage.

4. Residual estimates are proved before wall-adaptive extraction.

5. No later wall selection feeds back into any of the above objects.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
E_2/R_{\lab}\text{ cannot re-enter }B_Y.
}
\]

More precisely:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0
}
\]

remains valid throughout the downstream argument.

---

# 7. Interaction with scalar wall selection

After weighted transfer, we select

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.
\]

This selection must be made at the smooth aggregate level:

\[
\int W_{\Gamma_G}\ge \frac12\int(W_{\Gamma_G}+W_{\Gamma_{G,2}})
\]

or else choose \(\Gamma_{G,2}\).

This is safe.

But atomwise selection

\[
G_\star(p)=\arg\max\{|\Gamma_G(p)|,|\Gamma_{G,2}(p)|\}
\]

is unsafe unless treated as a pre-extraction finite partition with controlled complexity.

So the safe scalar selection theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{choose }G_\star\text{ globally or by a fixed finite pre-extraction partition.}
}
\]

This is not Agent 4’s main wall task, but it matters because atomwise wall selection can mimic local fitting.

---

# 8. Finalized Agent 4 module

Agent 4’s sector now consists of three sealed lemmas:

## Lemma 1 — NoAtomFitting\(_{2Y}^{+}\)

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0.
\]

## Lemma 2 — NoFeedbackFromAmplitude\(_{2Y}\)

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\]

does not alter \(B_Y\), \(D_{2Y}\), \(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\), or \(e_7,e_Y\).

## Lemma 3 — PreExtractionSeparation\(_{2Y}\)

All fixed/coherent/residual classifications are made before hard extraction and are wall-independent.

Together:

\[
\boxed{
A_{E_2,\loc}=A_{\lab,\loc}=0
}

as a stable statement throughout the downstream proof.

=
\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_Y(p)e_Y(p),

or atom-fitted:

or post-extraction coherent bases chosen from observed wall values.

If none of these is present, the sector is closed.

## Final status

The rank-two local proof should now focus on:

\[
\mathrm{NoActualSepticPackageScalar},

Y12RowExactness^{\fixed\alpha},

## Agent 4 report — \(\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}\)

No nomenclature changes.

Critical correction retained: D4 supplies only the **plain** rank-two wall

\[
\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.
\]

Derivative components

\[
D\Gamma_G,\quad D^2\Gamma_G
\]

are analytic auxiliaries only unless a separate derivative-D4-channel theorem is proved. The completion theorem below is therefore not a claim that D4 supplies a jet wall; it is the analytic bridge needed for \(\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}\) and \(\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}\). 

---

# 1. Target theorem

We need an order-four wall completion theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{H^2\times L^2}
\left(
(\Gamma_G(q),\Gamma_{G,2}(q)),
(\Gamma_G(q_X),\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X))
\right)
\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

Here \(q\) is the actual canonical source object, \(q_X\) is its finite atlas/EF model, and

\[
q=q_X+r.
\]

The wall components are:

\[
\Gamma_G(q)=-6q''+36q-12q^3,
\]

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}(q)=q^{(4)}-6(1-q^2)q''.
\]

The required norm is:

\[
\|\Gamma_G(q)-\Gamma_G(q_X)\|_{H^2(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X)\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}.
\]

This is the precise completion needed before applying finite-atlas Riesz and rank-two coercivity.

---

# 2. Expansion of \(R_G\)

Let

\[
R_G=\Gamma_G(q)-\Gamma_G(q_X).
\]

Since

\[
q=q_X+r,
\]

we have

\[
\Gamma_G(q)
=
-6(q_X''+r'')
+36(q_X+r)
-12(q_X+r)^3.
\]

Subtracting

\[
\Gamma_G(q_X)
=
-6q_X''+36q_X-12q_X^3
\]

gives

\[
\boxed{
R_G
=
-6r''
+
(36-36q_X^2)r
-
36q_Xr^2
-
12r^3.
}
\]

This expansion is correct.

To control

\[
R_G
\]

in \(H^2(I)\), one needs at minimum:

\[
r\in H^4(I),
\]

plus product estimates using polynomial bounds on \(q_X\).

---

# 3. Expansion of \(R_{G2}\)

Let

\[
R_{G2}
=
\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X).
\]

Since

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}(q)
=
q^{(4)}-6(1-q^2)q''
=
q^{(4)}-6q''+6q^2q'',
\]

we get

\[
R_{G2}
=
r^{(4)}-6r''
+
6\left((q_X+r)^2(q_X''+r'')-q_X^2q_X''\right).
\]

Expand:

\[
(q_X+r)^2(q_X''+r'')
=
(q_X^2+2q_Xr+r^2)(q_X''+r'')
\]

\[
=
q_X^2q_X''
+
q_X^2r''
+
2q_Xr q_X''
+
2q_Xr r''
+
r^2q_X''
+
r^2r''.
\]

Therefore

\[
\boxed{
R_{G2}
=
r^{(4)}-6r''
+
6q_X^2r''
+
12q_Xr q_X''
+
12q_Xr r''
+
6r^2q_X''
+
6r^2r''.
}
\]

This expansion is also correct.

To control

\[
R_{G2}
\]

in \(L^2(I)\), direct strong control of

\[
r^{(4)}
\]

is needed unless one reformulates the wall weakly.

---

# 4. Sufficient completion hypothesis

A clean sufficient hypothesis is:

\[
\boxed{
\|r\|_{H^4(I)}\le Q^{-B_0},
}
\]

together with polynomial atlas bounds:

\[
\boxed{
\|q_X\|_{C^4(I)}\le Q^{C_0}
}
\]

or equivalent Sobolev bounds strong enough to close products, e.g.

\[
\|q_X\|_{W^{4,\infty}(I)}\le Q^{C_0},
\qquad
\|r\|_{H^4(I)}\le Q^{-B_0}.
\]

Then standard one-dimensional Sobolev/product estimates give:

\[
\|R_G\|_{H^2(I)}
\le
Q^{C_1}\left(
\|r\|_{H^4}
+
\|r\|_{H^4}^2
+
\|r\|_{H^4}^3
\right),
\]

and

\[
\|R_{G2}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le
Q^{C_1}\left(
\|r\|_{H^4}
+
\|r\|_{H^4}^2
+
\|r\|_{H^4}^3
\right).
\]

Therefore, choosing \(B_0\) large enough relative to \(C_1\),

\[
\boxed{
\|R_G\|_{H^2(I)}+\|R_{G2}\|_{L^2(I)}\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

So:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}
\text{ follows from }H^4\text{-small residual plus polynomial }C^4\text{ atlas bounds.}
}
\]

---

# 5. Is \(H^4\) genuinely necessary?

For the **plain wall** as currently defined, yes, in the strong norm formulation.

Reason:

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}(q)
=
q^{(4)}-6(1-q^2)q''
\]

contains

\[
q^{(4)}
\]

linearly. Thus the residual contains

\[
r^{(4)}.
\]

Without some control on \(r^{(4)}\), one cannot bound

\[
R_{G2}
\]

in \(L^2\).

So either:

\[
\boxed{
r\in H^4
}
\]

or one must change formulation.

Possible alternatives:

## A. Weak wall formulation

One could pair \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) against test functions and integrate by parts, moving derivatives onto controlled finite test functions. This may reduce direct \(H^4\) demand, but then the D4 wall lower must also be phrased weakly. That is a different theorem.

## B. Smoothed wall

Use a mollified or projected wall component

\[
P_X\Gamma_{G,2}
\]

where high derivatives are controlled by projection. Again, this changes the wall object and must be justified as preserving D4 mass.

## C. Treat \(q^{(4)}\) as an independent finite observable

Extend the finite atlas to include \(q^{(4)}\) directly. This is essentially an order-four finite atlas theorem.

## D. Stronger EF/AFE truncation

Prove canonical EF/AFE approximation in \(H^4\). This is the cleanest path if available.

For the current rank-two route, the honest conclusion is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{strong }H^4\text{ completion is the clean sufficient condition.}
}
\]

---

# 6. Gamma/background terms

If

\[
q''
\]

contains a canonical gamma/background term such as \(B_2\), then \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) differentiates the corresponding \(q\)-model up to order four.

Equivalently, if one models \(q''\) directly, \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) needs two additional derivatives of the \(q''\)-model.

So the finite model must include the gamma/background derivative data to the needed order.

For example, if \(B_2\) is part of \(q''\), then the wall completion must control:

\[
B_2,\quad B_2',\quad B_2''
\]

at the \(q''\)-level, or the corresponding integrated \(q\)-level derivatives through order four.

Thus add a gamma-background completion requirement:

\[
\boxed{
\|B_2-B_{2,X}\|_{H^2(I)}\le Q^{-B}
}
\]

at the \(q''\)-level, or the equivalent \(q\)-level \(H^4\) statement.

If \(B_2\) is treated exactly/canonically, then this is not a residual problem. If it is approximated, it must be included in \(\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}\).

---

# 7. Recommended chart variable

There are two viable chart choices.

## Option A: finite chart for \(q\)

Let

\[
q_X\in \mathcal V_I,
\]

with \(\mathcal V_I\) closed under derivatives to order four and with product control for \(\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I\) and \(\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot D^2\mathcal V_I\).

Then

\[
\Gamma_G(q_X)\in
D^2\mathcal V_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

and

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X)\in
D^4\mathcal V_I+D^2\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot D^2\mathcal V_I.
\]

This fits Agent 5’s \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\) structure.

## Option B: finite chart for \(q''\)

Let

\[
p=q''
\]

be modeled directly. Then

\[
\Gamma_G=-6p+36q-12q^3
\]

still requires reconstructing \(q\), while

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}=p''-6(1-q^2)p
\]

requires two derivatives of \(p\) and control of \(q\).

This may be natural if the canonical source is fundamentally \(q''\), but it introduces integration constants and reconstruction control.

## Recommendation

Use Option A for the rank-two wall theorem:

\[
\boxed{
q_X\in \mathcal V_I,\qquad \|q-q_X\|_{H^4(I)}\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

It gives the cleanest path to both \(\Gamma_G\) and \(\Gamma_{G,2}\).

If the existing source model is \(q''\)-first, then add a reconstruction lemma:

\[
q''\text{-completion in }H^2
+
\text{controlled integration constants}
\Rightarrow
q\text{-completion in }H^4.
\]

---

# 8. Resulting theorem

## \(\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}\)

Assume:

1. There is a finite atlas model \(q_X\in\mathcal V_I\).
2. Residual satisfies:

\[
\|q-q_X\|_{H^4(I)}\le Q^{-B_0}.
\]

3. Atlas bounds:

\[
\|q_X\|_{C^4(I)}\le Q^{C_0}.
\]

4. Gamma/background components are exact or completed to the corresponding order-four wall norm.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\|\Gamma_G(q)-\Gamma_G(q_X)\|_{H^2(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X)\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

This supplies the wall completion needed before applying \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\), \(\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}\), and \(\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}\).

---

# 9. Failure routing

If \(r^{(4)}\) is uncontrolled, then:

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}
\]

cannot be completed in \(L^2\). The rank-two analytic closure stalls.

If \(q_X\) lacks \(C^4\) or comparable product bounds, the nonlinear terms

\[
q_Xr r'',\quad r^2q_X'',\quad r^2r''
\]

cannot be bounded polynomially.

If gamma/tail fourth-order pieces remain in residual, then completion is too weak. Need stronger gamma/tail modeling or exact inclusion in the finite atlas.

If only weak control is available, one must formulate a weak wall theorem. But that is not the current plain D4 wall theorem.

---

## Final status

\[
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}
\]

is a genuine analytic bottleneck.

The clean sufficient route is:

\[
\boxed{
\|q-q_X\|_{H^4(I)}\le Q^{-B}
}
\]

with polynomial \(C^4\) bounds on \(q_X\), including gamma/background derivative control.

This is narrower than full order-nine completion, but it is nontrivial and must be proved before the corrected plain-wall rank-two analytic route can close.

## Agent 4 continuation — weakening the \(H^4\) burden without smuggling jet-D4 channels

No nomenclature changes. I continue the \(\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}\) attack.

The previous conclusion was:

\[
\|q-q_X\|_{H^4(I)}\le Q^{-B}
\]

is a clean sufficient condition for

\[
\|\Gamma_G(q)-\Gamma_G(q_X)\|_{H^2(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X)\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B'}.
\]

But this may be stronger than what the existing EF/AFE approximation naturally supplies. I now examine whether the order-four burden can be weakened **without** pretending that D4 gives derivative wall channels. The correction remains: D4 honestly supplies only the plain wall \(\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}\); derivative components are analytic auxiliaries only. 

---

# 1. Why \(H^4(q-q_X)\) appeared

The obstruction is entirely the term

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}(q)=q^{(4)}-6(1-q^2)q''.
\]

If

\[
q=q_X+r,
\]

then

\[
R_{G2}
=
r^{(4)}-6r''
+
6q_X^2r''
+
12q_Xr q_X''
+
12q_Xr r''
+
6r^2q_X''
+
6r^2r''.
\]

The term

\[
r^{(4)}
\]

is linear and cannot be perturbatively hidden by nonlinear estimates.

So in a strong \(L^2\)-wall formulation, one of the following must happen:

1. directly control \(r^{(4)}\);
2. include \(q^{(4)}\) as a finite model observable;
3. weaken the wall formulation;
4. replace \(q\)-completion by \(q''\)-completion plus two-derivative control.

There is no free escape.

---

# 2. Better formulation: complete the wall variables directly

Instead of demanding

\[
q-q_X\in H^4,
\]

we can demand direct completion of the pair:

\[
\Gamma_G(q),
\qquad
\Gamma_{G,2}(q).
\]

That is, define finite wall approximants

\[
G_X\approx \Gamma_G(q),
\qquad
G_{2,X}\approx \Gamma_{G,2}(q),
\]

with

\[
G_X\in \mathcal W_G,
\qquad
G_{2,X}\in \mathcal W_{G2},
\]

and require

\[
\boxed{
\|\Gamma_G(q)-G_X\|_{H^2(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-G_{2,X}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

This is weaker conceptually than demanding that both arise from a single \(q_X\), but it must preserve the algebraic relation used by RankTwoCoercivity.

The danger is that if \(G_X,G_{2,X}\) are independent finite observables, then one might lose the identity

\[
E_2-E_1''=-12q(q')^2.
\]

So direct wall completion is safe only if the approximants are still tied to a common \(q_X\), or if the residual in the identity is controlled.

Thus the proper theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{complete the wall pair and the compatibility identity, not necessarily }q\text{ itself in }H^4.
}
\]

---

# 3. Compatibility-residual version

Define

\[
E_1(q)=q''-(6q-2q^3),
\]

\[
E_2(q)=q^{(4)}-6(1-q^2)q''.
\]

The exact identity is:

\[
E_2(q)-E_1(q)''
=
-12q(q')^2.
\]

Suppose we have finite approximants

\[
E_{1,X},\qquad E_{2,X}
\]

such that

\[
\|E_1-E_{1,X}\|_{H^2}
+
\|E_2-E_{2,X}\|_{L^2}
\le Q^{-B}.
\]

Then RankTwoCoercivity can be applied to \(q\) if the residual is small. We do **not** need

\[
E_{2,X}-E_{1,X}''
=
-12q_X(q_X')^2
\]

exactly unless we want the finite wall model itself to satisfy the same identity.

For the actual \(q\), the identity holds exactly. The finite model only needs to approximate the actual \(E_1,E_2\) in the required norms.

So the order-four completion can be weakened to:

\[
\boxed{
E_1\text{ completed in }H^2,\qquad E_2\text{ completed in }L^2.
}
\]

This is equivalent to wall-variable completion, not full \(q\)-completion.

---

# 4. \(q''\)-first formulation

Since the canonical source is often naturally \(q''\), write

\[
p=q''.
\]

Then

\[
\Gamma_G=-6p+36q-12q^3,
\]

and

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}=p''-6(1-q^2)p.
\]

This suggests the finite atlas could model

\[
(q,p,p'')
\]

rather than \(q\) through fourth order.

A sufficient \(q''\)-first completion is:

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I)}\le Q^{-B},
\]

\[
\|q-q_X\|_{L^\infty(I)}+\|q-q_X\|_{H^2(I)}\le Q^{-B},
\]

with

\[
q_X''=p_X
\]

up to controlled integration constants.

Then

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X)
\]

contains

\[
(p-p_X)''
\]

rather than \(r^{(4)}\). This is the same derivative requirement, but placed on \(p=q''\), where the EF/AFE source may naturally live.

Thus a more natural sufficient condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\|q''-q_X''\|_{H^2(I)}\le Q^{-B}
}
\]

plus reconstruction control for \(q,q_X\).

This may be easier than proving \(q-q_X\in H^4\) directly.

---

# 5. Reconstruction issue

If the finite model is built for

\[
p=q'',
\]

then \(q\) is recovered by integrating twice. The wall contains \(q\) and \(q^2\), so integration constants matter.

We need a reconstruction lemma:

## ReconstructionFromSource

Assume:

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I)}\le Q^{-B},
\]

and the two integration constants are matched by normalization conditions, e.g.

\[
q(m_0)=q_X(m_0),
\qquad
q'(m_0)=q_X'(m_0),
\]

or by orthogonality to affine modes. Then:

\[
\|q-q_X\|_{H^4(I)}\le Q^C\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I)}.
\]

So the \(q''\)-first version does not eliminate the order-four burden; it translates it to an \(H^2\) burden on the canonical source \(q''\).

That is likely the right formulation if Gate–1 already controls \(q''\).

---

# 6. Weak wall formulation

Another possible route is to avoid strong \(L^2\) control of

\[
q^{(4)}
\]

by testing \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) against a finite family of smooth test functions.

For a test \(\varphi\),

\[
\langle q^{(4)},\varphi\rangle
=
\langle q'',\varphi''\rangle
\]

after integration by parts, ignoring boundary terms or using compact support.

Then the wall component can be represented weakly using only \(q''\) and lower derivatives of test functions.

This might require only:

\[
q''-q_X''\in L^2
\]

rather than \(H^2\).

But it changes the analytic closure:

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}\text{ small in weak finite-test norm}
\]

does not automatically imply

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}\text{ small in }L^2.
\]

RankTwoCoercivity was stated with

\[
\|\Gamma_{G,2}\|_{L^2}.
\]

So a weak formulation would require a new coercivity theorem:

\[
\text{weak } \Gamma_{G,2}\text{ control}
+
\Gamma_G\text{ control}
\Rightarrow
q''\text{ small}.
\]

This is possible but is a different analytic route. For now, the strong \(H^2(q'')\) / \(H^4(q)\) path is cleaner.

---

# 7. Precise sufficient theorem in \(q''\)-language

The most natural formulation is:

## SourceCompletion\(_{2Y,\wall}^{(2)}\)

Let

\[
p=q''.
\]

Assume a finite model

\[
p_X
\]

satisfies

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I)}\le Q^{-B_0}.
\]

Assume \(q_X\) is the twice-integrated reconstruction of \(p_X\), with affine constants matched to \(q\), and

\[
\|q_X\|_{C^4(I)}\le Q^{C_0}.
\]

Then:

\[
\|\Gamma_G(q)-\Gamma_G(q_X)\|_{H^2(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X)\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}.
\]

This is equivalent to \(\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}\), but it is expressed in the natural source variable.

---

# 8. Boundary and localization terms

If \(I\) has cutoffs, reconstructing \(q\) from \(p=q''\) may introduce boundary terms.

To avoid boundary leakage, work with:

\[
I\Subset I^*
\]

and a buffer:

\[
\operatorname{dist}(I,\partial I^*)\ge R^{-C}|I|.
\]

Then standard interior estimates give:

\[
\|q-q_X\|_{H^4(I)}
\le
Q^C
\left(
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I^*)}
+
\|\text{affine mismatch}\|
\right).
\]

The affine mismatch must be killed by normalization or included in the finite atlas.

Thus the wall completion theorem should include:

\[
\boxed{
\text{affine modes are either matched, dead, or included in the finite atlas.}
}
\]

---

# 9. Gamma/background completion

If

\[
p=q''=B_2+P_2+\cdots,
\]

then the \(p_X\) model must approximate each contribution through \(H^2\), because \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) uses \(p''\).

For the gamma term \(B_2\), this means controlling:

\[
B_2,\quad B_2',\quad B_2''
\]

in \(L^2\) or \(L^\infty\) as appropriate.

If \(B_2\) is included exactly in \(p_X\), no residual cost. If approximated by asymptotic expansion, the expansion must be accurate to two derivatives on \(p\).

This is important because a zero-order accurate \(B_2\) approximation is insufficient for \(\Gamma_{G,2}\).

---

# 10. Refined theorem

## \(\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}\), source form

Assume:

1. \(p=q''\) has finite EF/AFE model \(p_X\) with:

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B_0}.
\]

2. Affine reconstruction constants are matched or included in the finite atlas.

3. The reconstructed \(q_X\) satisfies polynomial bounds:

\[
\|q_X\|_{C^4(I^*)}\le Q^{C_0}.
\]

4. Gamma/background/tail components are exact or completed to \(H^2\) at the \(p=q''\) level.

5. The interior buffer \(I\Subset I^*\) supports reconstruction and product estimates.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\|\Gamma_G(q)-\Gamma_G(q_X)\|_{H^2(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X)\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

This is the best current formulation: it avoids demanding \(q\)-\(H^4\) completion directly, while retaining the same analytic strength.

---

# 11. Final status

The order-four wall completion bottleneck remains real, but the natural formulation is:

\[
\boxed{
q''\text{-completion in }H^2
}
\]

plus reconstruction, not necessarily direct

\[
q\text{-completion in }H^4.
\]

This is likely the right target for the EF/AFE side.

The rank-two analytic chain now needs:

\[
\mathrm{SourceCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(2)}
+
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}.
\]

No derivative D4 channels are being assumed.

## Agent 4 continuation — source-form wall completion with affine-mode control

No nomenclature changes. I continue the \(\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}\) attack in its sharper source form:

\[
p=q'',\qquad p_X=q_X'',
\]

with target

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}
\]

plus controlled reconstruction of \(q\) from \(p\). This is still compatible with the correction that D4 supplies only the plain wall \(\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}\); derivative quantities enter only through analytic completion/Markov, not as D4 wall channels. 

---

# 1. The remaining obstruction is affine reconstruction

If

\[
p=q'',\qquad p_X=q_X'',
\]

and

\[
e:=p-p_X,
\]

then

\[
(q-q_X)''=e.
\]

Thus

\[
q-q_X
\]

is determined by \(e\) only up to an affine function:

\[
a+bt.
\]

But the wall contains \(q\) and \(q^2\), so affine mismatch cannot be ignored.

For example,

\[
\Gamma_G(q)-\Gamma_G(q_X)
=
-6e+36(q-q_X)-12(q^3-q_X^3).
\]

Even if

\[
e=0,
\]

an affine mismatch in \(q-q_X\) can make

\[
\Gamma_G(q)-\Gamma_G(q_X)
\neq0.
\]

Therefore source completion requires one of:

\[
\boxed{
\text{affine constants matched;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{affine modes included in the finite atlas;}
}
\]

or

\[
\boxed{
\text{affine modes killed by normalization/quotient.}
}
\]

The cleanest route is to include affine modes in the local finite chart.

---

# 2. Local reconstruction lemma

Let

\[
I\Subset I^*
\]

with buffer

\[
\operatorname{dist}(I,\partial I^*)\ge R^{-C}|I|.
\]

Assume

\[
(q-q_X)''=e,
\]

and affine mismatch is fixed by two normalization constraints, for example

\[
(q-q_X)(t_0)=0,\qquad
(q-q_X)'(t_0)=0,
\]

or by projection away from affine modes.

Then standard one-dimensional estimates give

\[
\|q-q_X\|_{H^4(I)}
\le
Q^C\|e\|_{H^2(I^*)}.
\]

If affine modes are not fixed, then the correct statement is

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{H^4(I)}
(q-q_X,\mathcal A_1)
\le
Q^C\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I^*)},
\]

where

\[
\mathcal A_1=\operatorname{span}\{1,t\}.
\]

Thus the finite atlas should include

\[
\mathcal A_1
\]

or impose two normalization conditions.

---

# 3. Source completion with affine modes included

Define the local \(q\)-atlas by

\[
\mathcal V_I
=
\mathcal A_1
+
\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I,
\]

where:

- \(\mathcal P_I\) is the finite source model for \(p=q''\);
- \(\mathcal I^2\) means twice-integrated with a fixed interior normalization;
- \(\mathcal A_1\) supplies affine modes.

Then

\[
q_X\in\mathcal V_I
\]

and

\[
q_X''=p_X.
\]

If

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B},
\]

then there exists \(q_X\in\mathcal V_I\) such that

\[
\|q-q_X\|_{H^4(I)}
\le Q^{-B+C}.
\]

This is the precise way to avoid losing affine modes.

So the source-form completion theorem should not merely say:

\[
p_X\text{ approximates }p.
\]

It should say:

\[
\boxed{
p_X\text{ approximates }p,\text{ and affine reconstruction is included or normalized.}
}
\]

---

# 4. Product estimates for wall residuals

Assume

\[
\|q-q_X\|_{H^4(I)}\le \varepsilon,
\qquad
\|q_X\|_{C^4(I)}\le Q^C,
\]

with

\[
\varepsilon=Q^{-B}.
\]

Then:

\[
R_G
=
-6r''
+
(36-36q_X^2)r
-
36q_Xr^2
-
12r^3.
\]

In one dimension, \(H^2\) is an algebra. Since

\[
r\in H^4,
\]

we get

\[
\|R_G\|_{H^2}
\le
Q^C\left(
\|r\|_{H^4}
+
\|r\|_{H^4}^2
+
\|r\|_{H^4}^3
\right).
\]

Similarly,

\[
R_{G2}
=
r^{(4)}-6r''
+
6q_X^2r''
+
12q_Xr q_X''
+
12q_Xr r''
+
6r^2q_X''
+
6r^2r''.
\]

Use:

\[
\|r^{(4)}\|_{L^2}\le \|r\|_{H^4},
\]

\[
\|q_X^2r''\|_{L^2}
\le
\|q_X\|_{L^\infty}^2\|r''\|_{L^2},
\]

\[
\|q_Xr q_X''\|_{L^2}
\le
\|q_X\|_{L^\infty}\|q_X''\|_{L^\infty}\|r\|_{L^2},
\]

\[
\|q_Xr r''\|_{L^2}
\le
\|q_X\|_{L^\infty}\|r\|_{L^\infty}\|r''\|_{L^2},
\]

and analogous estimates for the quadratic terms.

Thus

\[
\|R_{G2}\|_{L^2}
\le
Q^C
\left(
\|r\|_{H^4}
+
\|r\|_{H^4}^2
+
\|r\|_{H^4}^3
\right).
\]

Therefore

\[
\boxed{
\|R_G\|_{H^2}
+
\|R_{G2}\|_{L^2}
\le Q^{-B'}
}
\]

after choosing \(B\) large enough.

---

# 5. Finite atlas spaces generated by \(p=q''\)

If \(\mathcal P_I\) models \(p=q''\), then

\[
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I.
\]

The wall spaces become:

\[
\mathcal W_G
=
D^2\mathcal V_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I.
\]

Since

\[
D^2\mathcal V_I=\mathcal P_I,
\]

this is

\[
\mathcal W_G
=
\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I.
\]

For the second component,

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}=q^{(4)}-6q''+6q^2q''.
\]

Since

\[
q^{(4)}=p'',
\]

we get

\[
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot \mathcal P_I.
\]

This is a more natural formulation than

\[
D^4\mathcal V_I+D^2\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot D^2\mathcal V_I.
\]

They are equivalent if \(\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I\), but the \(p\)-form makes clear where order-four control enters: it is \(D^2\mathcal P_I\).

Thus the rank-two analytic atlas should probably be expressed in terms of \(\mathcal P_I\), the finite model for \(q''\).

---

# 6. Revised finite-atlas requirement

The finite source atlas should satisfy:

\[
p_X\in\mathcal P_I,
\qquad
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B},
\]

with finite Riesz control for:

\[
\mathcal P_I,
\quad
D^2\mathcal P_I,
\quad
\mathcal V_I,
\quad
\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\quad
\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]

The wall spaces are:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal W_G
=
\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
}
\]

This matches the actual structure of \(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\) while keeping the source variable \(q''\) primary.

---

# 7. Gamma/background components in \(p\)-language

If

\[
p=q''=B_2+P_2+\text{dual/tail/error},
\]

then \(\mathcal P_I\) should include:

\[
B_2
\]

exactly or approximate it in \(H^2\).

Since \(\mathcal W_{G2}\) uses \(D^2\mathcal P_I\), the gamma/background piece must be controlled through two derivatives.

For the canonical gamma term

\[
B_2(t),
\]

this means:

\[
\|B_2-B_{2,X}\|_{H^2(I)}\le Q^{-B}
\]

if approximated, or include the exact \(B_2\) in the finite model.

If using an asymptotic approximation such as a truncated expansion, it must be accurate after two derivatives, not merely pointwise.

This is a concrete requirement for the zeta-side scripts/models too.

---

# 8. Final theorem in source language

## SourceEFCompletion\(_{2Y,\wall}^{(2)}\)

Let

\[
p=q''.
\]

Assume:

1. There is a finite source model \(\mathcal P_I\) and \(p_X\in\mathcal P_I\) with

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B_0}.
\]

2. The twice-integrated chart

\[
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I
\]

matches affine constants or includes affine modes.

3. The reconstructed \(q_X\in\mathcal V_I\) satisfies

\[
\|q_X\|_{C^4(I^*)}\le Q^{C_0}.
\]

4. Gamma/background/tail pieces are exact or completed in \(H^2\) at the \(p=q''\) level.

5. \(I\Subset I^*\) has a polynomial buffer.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\|\Gamma_G(q)-\Gamma_G(q_X)\|_{H^2(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X)\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

This is the source-form version of

\[
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}.
\]

It is the most natural current formulation.

---

# 9. Failure routing

If \(\mathcal P_I\) only approximates \(p=q''\) in \(L^2\), not \(H^2\), then \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) is not controlled.

If affine reconstruction is not controlled, then \(\Gamma_G\) can drift even with perfect \(p\)-completion.

If gamma/background terms are only pointwise-completed and not \(H^2\)-completed at the \(p\)-level, then \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) has uncontrolled background residual.

If \(D^2\mathcal P_I\) has poor Riesz conditioning, then finite-atlas Markov/Remez fails.

---

## Final status

The wall-completion bottleneck is now best stated as:

\[
\boxed{
q''\text{-source completion in }H^2,
\text{ plus affine reconstruction control.}
}
\]

This is equivalent in strength to \(q\)-completion in \(H^4\), but better aligned with the canonical source variable \(q''\).

The next analytic target should be:

\[
\mathrm{SourceEFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(2)}
\]

plus finite Riesz control for

\[
\mathcal P_I,\quad D^2\mathcal P_I,\quad
\mathcal V_I,\quad
\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,\quad
\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]

## Agent 4 report — \(\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}\)

No nomenclature changes.

Critical correction retained: D4 supplies only the **plain** wall

\[
\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.
\]

Derivative quantities such as \(D\Gamma_G,D^2\Gamma_G\) may be used only as analytic auxiliaries for \(\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}\) or completion estimates. They are not D4/O2O3 wall components unless a separate \(\mathrm{DerivativeD4Channels}_{2Y}\) theorem is proved. 

The target is:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{H^2}(\Gamma_G,\mathcal W_G)\le Q^{-B},
\qquad
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2}(\Gamma_{G,2},\mathcal W_{G2})\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

This is enough because \(\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}\) applies to the **actual** wall variables. The finite approximants are only used to transfer \(L^2\)-wall mass through \(\mathrm{LocalMarkov}\), Remez, and weighted transfer.

---

# 1. Strong sufficient route: \(H^4(q)\)-completion

Let

\[
q=q_X+r.
\]

The wall components are

\[
\Gamma_G(q)=-6q''+36q-12q^3,
\]

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}(q)=q^{(4)}-6(1-q^2)q''.
\]

Set

\[
R_G=\Gamma_G(q)-\Gamma_G(q_X),
\]

\[
R_{G2}=\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X).
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
R_G
=
-6r''
+
(36-36q_X^2)r
-
36q_Xr^2
-
12r^3.
}
\]

Also:

\[
\boxed{
R_{G2}
=
r^{(4)}-6r''
+
6q_X^2 r''
+
12q_X r q_X''
+
12q_X r r''
+
6r^2 q_X''
+
6r^2 r''.
}
\]

These expansions are the correct residual formulas.

Therefore, if

\[
\|r\|_{H^4(I)}\le Q^{-B_0},
\qquad
\|q_X\|_{C^4(I)}\le Q^{C_0},
\]

then one-dimensional product estimates give

\[
\|R_G\|_{H^2(I)}+\|R_{G2}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le
Q^{C_1}
\left(
\|r\|_{H^4}
+
\|r\|_{H^4}^2
+
\|r\|_{H^4}^3
\right).
\]

Choosing \(B_0\gg C_1+B\), this yields

\[
\boxed{
\|R_G\|_{H^2(I)}+\|R_{G2}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

So full \(H^4(q)\)-completion is a clean sufficient theorem.

But it is stronger than necessary.

---

# 2. Direct wall-variable completion is the sharper target

We do **not** strictly need

\[
\|q-q_X\|_{H^4}\le Q^{-B}.
\]

What the corrected rank-two route actually needs is:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{H^2(I)}
(\Gamma_G(q),\mathcal W_G)
\le Q^{-B},
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(I)}
(\Gamma_{G,2}(q),\mathcal W_{G2})
\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

That is, we need finite-atlas approximation of the wall variables themselves.

This is enough because:

1. \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\) can be applied to the finite approximants in \(\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2}\);
2. the actual wall differs from those approximants by \(Q^{-B}\);
3. \(\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}\) is applied to the actual wall variables, not to the approximants.

So the completion theorem should be stated as \(\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}\), with full \(H^4(q)\)-completion listed as one sufficient proof route.

---

# 3. Source-variable formulation with \(p=q''\)

The natural variable is

\[
p=q''.
\]

Then

\[
\Gamma_G=-6p+36q-12q^3,
\]

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}=p''-6(1-q^2)p.
\]

Let \(\mathcal P_I\) be the finite source model for \(p=q''\). Let

\[
p_X\in\mathcal P_I,
\qquad
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B_0}.
\]

To reconstruct \(q\), set

\[
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I,
\]

where

\[
\mathcal A_1=\operatorname{span}\{1,t\}
\]

accounts for affine integration constants.

If affine constants are matched, normalized, or included in \(\mathcal A_1\), then there is \(q_X\in\mathcal V_I\) with

\[
q_X''=p_X
\]

and

\[
\|q-q_X\|_{H^4(I)}
\le
Q^C\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I^*)}.
\]

Thus \(p=q''\)-completion in \(H^2\), plus affine reconstruction control, implies the strong wall completion theorem.

This is the best source-aligned formulation.

---

# 4. Wall spaces in \(p=q''\) form

With

\[
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I,
\]

the finite wall spaces should be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal W_G
=
\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I.
}
\]

This matches

\[
\Gamma_G=-6p+36q-12q^3.
\]

For the second wall component,

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}=p''-6p+6q^2p,
\]

so

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
}
\]

This is more precise than writing everything in terms of \(D^4\mathcal V_I\), because it makes the source-level derivative burden explicit:

\[
\boxed{
p=q''\text{ must be completed through }H^2.
}
\]

---

# 5. Affine reconstruction is mandatory

If

\[
p=p_X
\]

but

\[
q-q_X=a+bt,
\]

then \(\Gamma_G(q)-\Gamma_G(q_X)\) need not vanish because \(\Gamma_G\) contains \(q\) and \(q^3\).

So \(\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}\) must include one of:

\[
\boxed{
\text{affine constants matched;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal A_1\subset \mathcal V_I;
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{affine modes are quotient-dead or normalized away.}
}
\]

The cleanest finite-atlas version is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I.
}
\]

Then affine uncertainty is part of the finite model, not residual error.

---

# 6. Gamma/background requirement

If

\[
p=q''=B_2+P_2+\text{dual/tail/error},
\]

then \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) uses

\[
p''.
\]

Therefore any gamma/background component included in \(p\) must be either:

1. included exactly in \(\mathcal P_I\); or
2. approximated in \(H^2\) at the \(p\)-level.

For a gamma term such as \(B_2\), this means control of

\[
B_2,\quad B_2',\quad B_2''
\]

in the relevant norm. Pointwise or zeroth-order approximation of \(B_2\) is not enough.

Thus the completion theorem requires:

\[
\boxed{
\|B_2-B_{2,X}\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}
}
\]

if \(B_2\) is not included exactly.

This is a concrete analytic requirement for the gamma/background side.

---

# 7. Weak wall alternatives

If \(H^2(p)\)-completion is unavailable, the only possible alternatives are genuinely different theorems.

## A. Weak \(\Gamma_{G,2}\)

Use

\[
\langle q^{(4)},\varphi\rangle=\langle q'',\varphi''\rangle
\]

to define a weak wall. But then \(\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}\) must be reproved in a weak norm. This is not the current route.

## B. Smoothed wall

Project or mollify \(\Gamma_{G,2}\). Then one must prove D4 wall mass survives smoothing. Again, this is a new theorem.

## C. Add \(q^{(4)}\) as an observable

This is basically direct wall-variable completion by expanding the finite atlas to include \(p''\).

So the honest conclusion is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{strong }H^2\text{-completion of }p=q''
\text{ is the cleanest current route.}
}
\]

---

# 8. The theorem

## \(\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}\)

Assume:

1. \(p=q''\) has finite model \(p_X\in\mathcal P_I\) with

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B_0}.
\]

2. The \(q\)-chart is reconstructed as

\[
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I,
\]

with affine modes included or normalized.

3. The reconstructed \(q_X\in\mathcal V_I\) satisfies polynomial bounds:

\[
\|q_X\|_{C^4(I^*)}\le Q^{C_0}.
\]

4. Gamma/background/tail components are exact or completed in \(H^2\) at the \(p=q''\) level.

5. \(I\Subset I^*\) has a polynomial buffer, so interior reconstruction/product estimates are valid.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{H^2(I)}
(\Gamma_G(q),\mathcal W_G)\le Q^{-B},
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(I)}
(\Gamma_{G,2}(q),\mathcal W_{G2})\le Q^{-B},
}
\]

with

\[
\mathcal W_G
=
\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

\[
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]

This is sufficient for the corrected plain-wall rank-two analytic route.

---

# 9. Failure routing

If \(\mathcal P_I\) approximates \(p=q''\) only in \(L^2\), then \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) is uncontrolled.

If affine modes are not included or matched, \(\Gamma_G\) can drift despite perfect \(p\)-completion.

If \(B_2\) or other gamma/background terms are not completed through two derivatives at the \(p\)-level, \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) retains uncontrolled residuals.

If \(D^2\mathcal P_I\) has bad Riesz conditioning, \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\) fails even if completion is formally true.

---

## Final status

The clean analytic target is not necessarily full \(H^4(q)\)-completion. The sharper target is:

\[
\boxed{
q''\text{-source completion in }H^2
+
\text{affine reconstruction control}
}
\]

or, more abstractly,

\[
\boxed{
\text{direct wall-variable completion}
\quad
\Gamma_G\in H^2,\quad \Gamma_{G,2}\in L^2.
}
\]

This is the wall-completion module needed for the corrected rank-two plain-wall route.

## Agent 4 continuation — reducing wall completion to a finite source-residual ledger

No nomenclature changes.

I continue the \(\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}\) attack. The corrected context remains: D4 supplies only the plain wall

\[
\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\},
\]

so any derivative control is an analytic completion/Riesz matter, not a D4 wall component. 

The current best formulation is:

\[
p=q'',\qquad p_X\in\mathcal P_I,\qquad \|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}.
\]

Then reconstruct

\[
q_X\in \mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I
\]

with affine modes included or normalized, and prove

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{H^2}(\Gamma_G(q),\mathcal W_G)\le Q^{-B},
\]

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2}(\Gamma_{G,2}(q),\mathcal W_{G2})\le Q^{-B}.
\]

The next step is to make the source-residual requirements finite and checkable.

---

# 1. Source residual ledger

Write the source-level decomposition

\[
p=q''=p_X+r_p,
\]

where

\[
p_X\in\mathcal P_I,
\qquad
r_p=p-p_X.
\]

Then the clean sufficient condition is

\[
\boxed{
\|r_p\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

But in the actual zeta-side model, \(p\) is not a single monolithic object. It decomposes schematically as

\[
p
=
B_2+P_2+D_2+E_2,
\]

or more generally:

\[
p
=
p_{\gamma}
+
p_{\mathrm{prime/AFE}}
+
p_{\mathrm{dual}}
+
p_{\mathrm{tail}}
+
p_{\mathrm{smooth}}
+
p_{\mathrm{err}}.
\]

So the correct completion theorem should not merely assert

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2}\le Q^{-B}.
\]

It should provide a ledger:

\[
r_p
=
r_{\gamma}
+
r_{\mathrm{AFE}}
+
r_{\mathrm{dual}}
+
r_{\mathrm{tail}}
+
r_{\mathrm{smooth}}
+
r_{\mathrm{err}},
\]

with

\[
\boxed{
\sum_{\bullet}\|r_{\bullet}\|_{H^2(I^*)}
\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

This turns wall completion into a finite residual bookkeeping theorem.

---

# 2. Gamma/background component

The gamma/background component is the easiest to state and the easiest to get wrong.

If \(B_2\) is included exactly, then

\[
r_{\gamma}=0.
\]

If \(B_2\) is approximated by an asymptotic or truncated expression \(B_{2,X}\), then the required bound is

\[
\boxed{
\|B_2-B_{2,X}\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

Equivalently, one needs control of

\[
B_2-B_{2,X},
\qquad
B_2'-B_{2,X}',
\qquad
B_2''-B_{2,X}''.
\]

Zeroth-order approximation of \(B_2\) is not enough, because

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}=p''-6(1-q^2)p.
\]

Thus any source approximation error is differentiated twice in the second wall component.

So the gamma audit is:

\[
\boxed{
B_2\text{ exact, or }B_2\text{ approximated to two derivatives.}
}
\]

This should be part of \(\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}\), not a later tail estimate.

---

# 3. AFE/prime component

Let the AFE/prime finite model be

\[
p_{\mathrm{AFE},X}\in\mathcal P_I^{\mathrm{AFE}}.
\]

The required completion is

\[
\boxed{
\|p_{\mathrm{AFE}}-p_{\mathrm{AFE},X}\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

This means the AFE truncation error must be controlled after two \(t\)-derivatives.

If the AFE model contains oscillatory atoms

\[
a_n(t)e^{i\phi_n(t)},
\]

then two derivatives produce factors roughly of the form

\[
(\phi_n')^2,\qquad \phi_n'',\qquad a_n',\qquad a_n''.
\]

So the truncation must be strong enough after these derivative weights.

The finite model \(\mathcal P_I\) must therefore include the derivative-closed atom family, not only the zeroth-order source atoms.

A good statement is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal P_I\text{ is derivative-stable through order }2.
}
\]

That is, for the basis atoms \(\varphi_j\),

\[
\varphi_j',\ \varphi_j''
\]

are either in the model span, or are controlled by the finite Riesz structure on \(I^*\).

This is the source-level analogue of LocalMarkov.

---

# 4. Tail component

The tail piece must satisfy

\[
\boxed{
\|p_{\mathrm{tail}}\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}
}
\]

after subtracting whatever tail approximation is included in \(p_X\).

If the tail is only known in \(L^2\), it is insufficient for \(\Gamma_{G,2}\). There are only three possible repairs:

1. include more of the tail in the finite model \(\mathcal P_I\);
2. prove two-derivative tail decay;
3. formulate a weak wall theorem for \(\Gamma_{G,2}\).

For the present plain-wall route, option 2 is the direct requirement.

So the tail ledger must distinguish:

\[
\text{tail included in }\mathcal P_I
\]

from

\[
\text{tail left as residual}.
\]

Only the latter must be \(H^2\)-small.

---

# 5. Smoothing/cutoff errors

Cutoff and smoothing errors often look harmless at zeroth order but become dangerous after differentiation.

If a smooth cutoff \(\chi(t)\) is used, derivatives produce

\[
\chi'(t),\qquad \chi''(t).
\]

Thus the smoothing residual must obey

\[
\boxed{
\|r_{\smooth}\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

Alternatively, cutoff artifacts must be included in \(\mathcal P_I\).

This gives a precise guardrail:

\[
\boxed{
\text{smoothing is allowed only if two cutoff derivatives remain polynomially controlled.}
}
\]

This is likely where the buffer

\[
I\Subset I^*
\]

is useful: choose cutoffs supported in \(I^*\) and equal to one on \(I\), with derivative loss bounded by a polynomial in \(R\).

---

# 6. Affine reconstruction ledger

After completing \(p=q''\), reconstructing \(q\) creates affine ambiguity:

\[
q-q_X=a+bt+\mathcal I^2 r_p.
\]

The wall terms depend on \(q\), so we need one of:

## Route A: match affine constants

Impose

\[
(q-q_X)(t_0)=0,\qquad (q-q_X)'(t_0)=0.
\]

Then

\[
\|q-q_X\|_{H^4(I)}
\le Q^C\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I^*)}.
\]

## Route B: include affine modes

Let

\[
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I,
\qquad
\mathcal A_1=\operatorname{span}\{1,t\}.
\]

Then choose \(q_X\) with the correct affine part.

This is better for finite atlas work.

## Route C: quotient affine modes

Only possible if affine modes are genuinely dead for the wall, but they are not: \(\Gamma_G\) contains \(q\) and \(q^3\). So affine quotienting is generally not valid.

Thus the recommended route remains:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal A_1\subset\mathcal V_I.
}
\]

---

# 7. Wall spaces after residual ledger

With

\[
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I,
\]

define:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal W_G
=
\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I.
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\Gamma_G(q_X)\in\mathcal W_G,
\]

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X)\in\mathcal W_{G2}.
\]

So if the residual ledger gives

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B},
\]

and affine reconstruction is controlled, then:

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{H^2(I)}(\Gamma_G(q),\mathcal W_G)\le Q^{-B'},
\]

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(I)}(\Gamma_{G,2}(q),\mathcal W_{G2})\le Q^{-B'}.
\]

This is the final wall-variable completion statement.

---

# 8. Product control obligation

The nonlinear terms require finite product control:

\[
\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

\[
\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]

Completion itself only says the actual wall is close to those spaces. But later \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\) must show those product spaces have polynomial Riesz/Remez constants.

Thus wall completion and finite atlas Riesz must meet at the product spaces:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal W_G,\ \mathcal W_{G2}.
}
\]

If the product spaces have bad leverage or near-relations, the analytic route fails even if completion is true.

So Agent 4’s theorem should hand these exact spaces to Agent 5.

---

# 9. Refined theorem

## SourceResidualLedger\(_{2Y,\wall}\)

Assume:

1. \(p=q''\) decomposes into finitely modeled pieces plus residuals:

\[
p=p_X+r_p,\qquad p_X\in\mathcal P_I.
\]

2. The residual ledger satisfies:

\[
\|r_p\|_{H^2(I^*)}
\le
\sum_\bullet\|r_\bullet\|_{H^2(I^*)}
\le Q^{-B}.
\]

3. Gamma/background terms are exact or \(H^2\)-completed.

4. Tail/smoothing/cutoff residuals are \(H^2\)-small or included in \(\mathcal P_I\).

5. Affine reconstruction is handled by

\[
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I.
\]

6. \(q_X\) has polynomial \(C^4\)-bounds on \(I^*\).

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{H^2(I)}(\Gamma_G(q),\mathcal W_G)\le Q^{-B'},
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(I)}(\Gamma_{G,2}(q),\mathcal W_{G2})\le Q^{-B'}.
}
\]

This is \(\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}\) in its source-residual ledger form.

---

# 10. Failure routing

If gamma/background is not \(H^2\)-completed:

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}
\]

has uncontrolled residual.

If tail is only \(L^2\)-small:

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}
\]

is uncontrolled.

If affine modes are not included:

\[
\Gamma_G
\]

can drift.

If product spaces have bad Riesz constants:

completion may hold, but LocalMarkov/Remez fails.

Thus the analytic bottleneck splits cleanly into:

\[
\boxed{
\text{source residual ledger}
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\text{finite product Riesz control}.
}
\]

---

## Final status

The wall-completion target is now explicit and source-natural:

\[
\boxed{
p=q''\text{ must be completed in }H^2
}
\]

with gamma/background/tail/smoothing residuals controlled through two derivatives and affine modes included in the reconstructed \(q\)-atlas.

This is sufficient for the corrected plain-wall rank-two route and does not assume any derivative D4 wall channels.

## Agent 4 continuation — separating completion from Riesz: the exact handoff contract

No nomenclature changes.

I continue from the source-residual ledger form of

\[
\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}.
\]

The corrected context remains: D4 supplies only the plain wall

\[
\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\},
\]

and derivative quantities enter only through analytic completion/Riesz, not as D4 wall components. 

The key issue now is to define the exact contract between:

\[
\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}
\]

and

\[
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}.
\]

---

# 1. The completion theorem should output finite wall approximants, not just small residuals

Let

\[
p=q'',
\qquad
p=p_X+r_p,
\qquad
p_X\in\mathcal P_I.
\]

Let

\[
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I.
\]

Choose

\[
q_X\in\mathcal V_I
\]

with

\[
q_X''=p_X
\]

and affine constants matched or included.

Then define the finite wall approximants:

\[
G_X:=\Gamma_G(q_X),
\]

\[
G_{2,X}:=\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X).
\]

They satisfy:

\[
G_X\in\mathcal W_G
=
\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

and

\[
G_{2,X}\in\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]

So the completion theorem should output the concrete pair

\[
(G_X,G_{2,X})
\]

and the residual bound

\[
\boxed{
\|\Gamma_G(q)-G_X\|_{H^2(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-G_{2,X}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

This is stronger and more usable than only saying

\[
\operatorname{dist}(\Gamma_G,\mathcal W_G)\le Q^{-B}.
\]

It hands a concrete finite-atlas representative to the Riesz/Remez theorem.

---

# 2. FiniteAtlasRiesz should never see uncontrolled actual walls

The Riesz theorem should be applied to

\[
G_X\in\mathcal W_G,
\qquad
G_{2,X}\in\mathcal W_{G2},
\]

not directly to

\[
\Gamma_G(q),\Gamma_{G,2}(q),
\]

unless the completion residual has already been absorbed.

Thus the analytic chain should be:

\[
\Gamma_G(q),\Gamma_{G,2}(q)
\]

\[
\approx
G_X,G_{2,X}
\quad
\text{by wall completion},
\]

then

\[
G_X,G_{2,X}
\]

are controlled by finite-atlas Riesz/Remez.

So the handoff contract is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}
\text{ produces finite wall representatives;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
\text{ controls those representatives;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{then residuals are reinserted with polynomial loss.}
}
\]

---

# 3. LocalMarkov with residuals

The desired analytic estimate is:

\[
\|\Gamma_G(q)\|_{H^2(I)}
\le
Q^C\|\Gamma_G(q)\|_{L^2(I^*)}
+
Q^{-B'}.
\]

But Riesz gives only:

\[
\|G_X\|_{H^2(I)}
\le
Q^C\|G_X\|_{L^2(I^*)}.
\]

Using completion:

\[
\|\Gamma_G(q)-G_X\|_{H^2(I)}\le Q^{-B},
\]

and also

\[
\|\Gamma_G(q)-G_X\|_{L^2(I^*)}\le Q^{-B},
\]

we get:

\[
\|\Gamma_G(q)\|_{H^2(I)}
\le
\|G_X\|_{H^2(I)}
+
Q^{-B}
\]

\[
\le
Q^C\|G_X\|_{L^2(I^*)}
+
Q^{-B}
\]

\[
\le
Q^C\|\Gamma_G(q)\|_{L^2(I^*)}
+
Q^{C-B}
+
Q^{-B}.
\]

So:

\[
\boxed{
\|\Gamma_G(q)\|_{H^2(I)}
\le
Q^C\|\Gamma_G(q)\|_{L^2(I^*)}
+
Q^{-B'}
}
\]

for \(B\) chosen large.

Thus \(\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}\) for the actual wall follows from:

1. wall completion for \(\Gamma_G\) in \(H^2\);
2. finite Riesz for \(\mathcal W_G\);
3. completion residual small enough after the \(Q^C\) Riesz loss.

This is the exact bridge to Agent 5.

---

# 4. Remez transfer with residuals

Similarly, suppose finite-atlas Remez gives for every predetermined

\[
E\subset I,\qquad |E|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
\]

that

\[
\int_E \left(|G_X|^2+|G_{2,X}|^2\right)
\ge
Q^{-C}
\int_I \left(|G_X|^2+|G_{2,X}|^2\right).
\]

Let

\[
W_2(q)=|\Gamma_G(q)|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)|^2,
\]

and

\[
W_{2,X}=|G_X|^2+|G_{2,X}|^2.
\]

Completion in

\[
L^2\times L^2
\]

implies

\[
\|W_2^{1/2}-W_{2,X}^{1/2}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le
Q^{-B}.
\]

Therefore, if the actual wall mass is non-negligible:

\[
\int_I W_2(q)\ge Q^{-A}|I|,
\]

then for \(B\) large enough,

\[
\int_I W_{2,X}\ge \frac12 Q^{-A}|I|.
\]

Remez gives:

\[
\int_E W_{2,X}\ge Q^{-C-A}|I|.
\]

Then completion gives:

\[
\int_E W_2(q)\ge Q^{-C-A'}|I|
\]

after absorbing residual error.

So vector Remez for actual walls follows from finite Remez plus wall completion.

This is the exact handoff to weighted transfer.

---

# 5. Completion residual must be smaller than the eventual wall lower

A key quantitative guardrail:

The completion error \(Q^{-B}\) must be chosen below the final lower scale. If Gate–1 gives a wall lower of size

\[
Q^{-A_0},
\]

and Riesz/Remez/weight transfer loses

\[
Q^{C_0},
\]

then need

\[
B\gg A_0+C_0.
\]

Otherwise the residual could swamp the wall mass.

Thus the completion theorem must be stated with arbitrary loss margin:

\[
\boxed{
\text{for every }B\text{ needed downstream, choose truncation/atlas depth giving }Q^{-B}.
}
\]

If the source completion only gives a fixed power and cannot be sharpened, the rank-two analytic route may fail quantitatively.

---

# 6. SourceResidualLedger must be scale-normalized

The norm

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2(I^*)}
\]

must be scale-normalized.

If \(I\) has length \(L\), define

\[
\|f\|_{H^2_{\mathrm{sc}}(I)}^2
=
\sum_{j=0}^2 L^{2j}\|f^{(j)}\|_{L^2(I)}^2,
\]

or the equivalent normalized-coordinate norm.

Then

\[
p'' 
\]

in the physical coordinate corresponds to scale-normalized second derivative loss.

This matters because the finite atlas Riesz constants depend on scale.

So the theorem should use:

\[
\boxed{
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\mathrm{sc}}(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

Similarly:

\[
\|\Gamma_G-G_X\|_{H^2_{\mathrm{sc}}(I)}\le Q^{-B}.
\]

This prevents hidden losses from small interval sizes.

---

# 7. Boundary-buffer condition

The reconstruction from \(p=q''\) to \(q\) and the Markov estimate require an interior buffer:

\[
I\Subset I^*,
\]

with

\[
\operatorname{dist}(I,\partial I^*)\ge R^{-C}|I|.
\]

This buffer is also needed for cutoffs and Remez.

Thus \(\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}\) should assume:

\[
\boxed{
I\subset I^*\text{ with }R^{-C}\text{-scale buffer.}
}
\]

If the buffer fails, derivative estimates can leak boundary terms and Riesz constants can blow up.

---

# 8. Refined final theorem

## \(\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}^{\mathrm{handoff}}\)

Let

\[
p=q'',
\qquad
p_X\in\mathcal P_I,
\qquad
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I.
\]

Assume:

1. Scale-normalized source completion:

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\mathrm{sc}}(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}.
\]

2. Affine modes are included or normalization-matched.

3. \(q_X\in\mathcal V_I\) reconstructs \(p_X\):

\[
q_X''=p_X.
\]

4. Polynomial atlas bounds:

\[
\|q_X\|_{C^4_{\mathrm{sc}}(I^*)}\le Q^C.
\]

5. Gamma/background/tail/smoothing residuals satisfy the same \(H^2_{\mathrm{sc}}\)-ledger.

6. \(I\Subset I^*\) has \(R^{-C}\)-scale buffer.

Then there exist

\[
G_X\in\mathcal W_G,
\qquad
G_{2,X}\in\mathcal W_{G2},
\]

with

\[
\mathcal W_G=\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

\[
\mathcal W_{G2}=D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I,
\]

such that

\[
\boxed{
\|\Gamma_G(q)-G_X\|_{H^2_{\mathrm{sc}}(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-G_{2,X}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B'}.
}
\]

This is the precise handoff theorem to finite-atlas Riesz.

---

# 9. Final status

The wall-completion module is now cleanly separated from the Riesz module.

Agent 4 supplies:

\[
\boxed{
\text{actual plain wall variables are close to finite wall spaces.}
}
\]

Agent 5 must supply:

\[
\boxed{
\text{finite wall spaces obey Markov/Remez/Riesz.}
}
\]

Agent 6 must supply:

\[
\boxed{
\text{predetermined coefficient-good cores transfer mass into }N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2.
}
\]

The analytic rank-two route is now modular and does not require pretending that derivative wall components are D4 outputs.

## Agent 4 continuation — checking nonlinear wall residuals in scale-normalized norms

No nomenclature changes. I continue \(\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}^{\mathrm{handoff}}\), focusing on the nonlinear estimates needed to pass from source completion

\[
p=q'',\qquad \|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\mathrm{sc}}(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}
\]

to wall-variable completion

\[
\|\Gamma_G(q)-G_X\|_{H^2_{\mathrm{sc}}(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-G_{2,X}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B'}.
\]

The correction remains: D4 supplies the plain wall only,

\[
\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\},
\]

not derivative wall components.

---

# 1. Scale-normalized setup

Let \(I\) have length \(L\), and use normalized coordinate

\[
x=\frac{t-t_0}{L}.
\]

Define scale-normalized derivatives

\[
D=L\partial_t.
\]

Then

\[
\|f\|_{H^k_{\sc}(I)}^2
=
\sum_{j=0}^k
\|D^j f\|_{L^2(I)}^2.
\]

The completion input is

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}\le \varepsilon,
\qquad
\varepsilon=Q^{-B}.
\]

The reconstruction step gives

\[
\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}(I)}
=
\|q-q_X\|_{H^4_{\sc}(I)}
\le
Q^C\varepsilon,
\]

assuming affine modes are included or matched and \(I\Subset I^*\) has an \(R^{-C}\)-scale buffer.

---

# 2. Product estimates in one dimension

On a normalized interval, \(H^2\) is an algebra:

\[
\|fg\|_{H^2_{\sc}}
\le
C
\|f\|_{H^2_{\sc}}
\|g\|_{H^2_{\sc}}.
\]

Also,

\[
H^1_{\sc}\hookrightarrow L^\infty
\]

with polynomial loss only from chart scaling already absorbed into the scale-normalized norm.

Assume atlas bounds

\[
\|q_X\|_{C^4_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^{C_0}.
\]

Then \(q_X\), \(q_X'\), \(q_X''\), and their scale-normalized derivatives through the required order are polynomially bounded.

This gives the basic product estimate:

\[
\|q_X^a r^b D^j r\|_{L^2}
\le
Q^C
\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}^b
\]

for the combinations appearing in \(R_G\) and \(R_{G2}\).

---

# 3. Estimate for \(R_G\)

Recall

\[
R_G
=
-6r''
+
(36-36q_X^2)r
-
36q_Xr^2
-
12r^3.
\]

In scale-normalized notation, physical \(r''\) has powers of \(L^{-2}\). These are harmless only if the wall definitions and norms are also scale-normalized consistently. Therefore the theorem should state \(R_G\) in the same scaled coordinates used for \(\Gamma_G\).

Assuming that normalization is fixed, the estimates are:

\[
\|r''\|_{H^2_{\sc}}
\le
Q^C\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}},
\]

\[
\|(36-36q_X^2)r\|_{H^2_{\sc}}
\le
Q^C\|r\|_{H^2_{\sc}},
\]

\[
\|q_Xr^2\|_{H^2_{\sc}}
\le
Q^C\|r\|_{H^2_{\sc}}^2,
\]

\[
\|r^3\|_{H^2_{\sc}}
\le
Q^C\|r\|_{H^2_{\sc}}^3.
\]

Hence:

\[
\boxed{
\|R_G\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I)}
\le
Q^C
\left(
\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}
+
\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}^2
+
\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}^3
\right).
}
\]

So if

\[
\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}\le Q^{-B},
\]

then

\[
\|R_G\|_{H^2_{\sc}}\le Q^{-B'}
\]

after taking \(B\) large.

---

# 4. Estimate for \(R_{G2}\)

Recall

\[
R_{G2}
=
r^{(4)}-6r''
+
6q_X^2 r''
+
12q_Xr q_X''
+
12q_Xr r''
+
6r^2q_X''
+
6r^2r''.
\]

The direct \(L^2\) estimates are:

\[
\|r^{(4)}\|_{L^2_{\sc}}
\le
Q^C\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}},
\]

\[
\|r''\|_{L^2_{\sc}}
\le
Q^C\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}},
\]

\[
\|q_X^2r''\|_{L^2}
\le
Q^C\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}},
\]

\[
\|q_Xr q_X''\|_{L^2}
\le
Q^C\|r\|_{L^2}
\le
Q^C\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}},
\]

\[
\|q_Xr r''\|_{L^2}
\le
Q^C\|r\|_{L^\infty}\|r''\|_{L^2}
\le
Q^C\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}^2,
\]

\[
\|r^2q_X''\|_{L^2}
\le
Q^C\|r\|_{L^\infty}\|r\|_{L^2}
\le
Q^C\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}^2,
\]

\[
\|r^2r''\|_{L^2}
\le
Q^C\|r\|_{L^\infty}^2\|r''\|_{L^2}
\le
Q^C\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}^3.
\]

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\|R_{G2}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le
Q^C
\left(
\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}
+
\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}^2
+
\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}^3
\right).
}
\]

Thus source completion in \(H^2_{\sc}\) for \(p=q''\), plus affine reconstruction, gives the required \(L^2\)-completion of the second wall coordinate.

---

# 5. Handoff to finite wall spaces

Given \(p_X\in\mathcal P_I\) and \(q_X\in\mathcal V_I\), define

\[
G_X=\Gamma_G(q_X),
\qquad
G_{2,X}=\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X).
\]

Then:

\[
G_X\in
\mathcal W_G
=
\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

and

\[
G_{2,X}\in
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]

The residual estimates above give:

\[
\boxed{
\|\Gamma_G(q)-G_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-G_{2,X}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B'}.
}
\]

This is exactly the handoff required by \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\).

---

# 6. Necessary assumptions, now explicit

The theorem requires all of the following.

## Source completion

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}.
\]

## Affine reconstruction

Either affine constants are matched, or

\[
\mathcal A_1\subset\mathcal V_I.
\]

## Interior buffer

\[
I\Subset I^*
\]

with \(R^{-C}\)-scale buffer.

## Atlas bounds

\[
\|q_X\|_{C^4_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^C.
\]

## Gamma/background completion

Gamma/background terms in \(p=q''\) are exact or completed in \(H^2_{\sc}\).

## Tail/cutoff completion

Tail, truncation, and smoothing residuals are \(H^2_{\sc}\)-small at the \(p\)-level.

---

# 7. Remaining danger: finite source model may be too large

Even if wall completion holds, the finite wall spaces

\[
\mathcal W_G,\qquad \mathcal W_{G2}
\]

may be high-dimensional or poorly conditioned.

That is not Agent 4’s completion problem; it belongs to \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\).

But Agent 4 must hand off the correct spaces:

\[
\mathcal W_G
=
\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

\[
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]

If these spaces are too large for Riesz/Remez, the analytic route fails at the finite-atlas stage, not at wall completion.

---

## Final status

The wall-variable completion theorem now has a proof-quality skeleton.

The essential analytic input is:

\[
\boxed{
q''\text{-source completion in }H^2_{\sc}(I^*)
}
\]

plus affine reconstruction. This gives:

\[
\boxed{
\Gamma_G\text{ completion in }H^2_{\sc},
\qquad
\Gamma_{G,2}\text{ completion in }L^2.
}
\]

This supports the corrected plain-wall rank-two route without assuming any derivative D4 channels.

## Agent 4 continuation — wall completion under \(p=q''\)-source charts: remaining edge cases

No nomenclature changes. I continue the \(\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}\) attack in the source variable

\[
p=q''.
\]

The corrected constraint remains: D4 gives only the plain wall

\[
\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.
\]

The derivative controls are analytic support estimates, not D4 wall channels. 

---

# 1. Current theorem skeleton

We want:

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{H^2_{\sc}(I)}(\Gamma_G(q),\mathcal W_G)\le Q^{-B},
\]

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(I)}(\Gamma_{G,2}(q),\mathcal W_{G2})\le Q^{-B}.
\]

Using

\[
p=q'',
\qquad
p_X\in\mathcal P_I,
\qquad
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^{-B},
\]

and

\[
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I,
\]

we set \(q_X\in\mathcal V_I\) with \(q_X''=p_X\). Then the finite wall spaces are

\[
\mathcal W_G
=
\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

\[
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
\]

The residual estimates are already reduced to product bounds for

\[
r=q-q_X.
\]

The remaining edge cases are affine modes, boundary/cutoff terms, gamma/background derivatives, and scale normalization.

---

# 2. Affine modes must enter \(\mathcal V_I\), not the residual

If

\[
(q-q_X)''=p-p_X,
\]

then

\[
q-q_X = a+bt+\mathcal I^2(p-p_X).
\]

Since

\[
\Gamma_G=-6q''+36q-12q^3
\]

depends on \(q\) itself, the affine component cannot be ignored.

Thus the wall completion theorem should explicitly require one of:

\[
a=b=0
\]

by normalization, or

\[
\mathcal A_1=\operatorname{span}\{1,t\}\subset\mathcal V_I.
\]

The second is safer. Then the residual \(r\) can be chosen with affine part removed:

\[
r\perp \mathcal A_1
\]

or equivalently \(q_X\) absorbs the affine mismatch.

This prevents a false failure where \(p\)-completion is perfect but \(\Gamma_G\) drifts because of an affine reconstruction mismatch.

---

# 3. Boundary leakage: require an interior reconstruction operator

The twice-integration operator

\[
\mathcal I^2
\]

must be localized from \(I^*\) to \(I\). If one reconstructs directly on \(I\) without buffer, boundary constants and cutoff derivatives can pollute \(H^4\) estimates.

So define an interior reconstruction operator

\[
\mathcal I^2_{I\Subset I^*}:\mathcal P_I\to \mathcal V_I
\]

with:

\[
I\Subset I^*,
\qquad
\operatorname{dist}(I,\partial I^*)\ge R^{-C}|I|.
\]

Then require:

\[
\|\mathcal I^2 e\|_{H^4_{\sc}(I)}
\le Q^C\|e\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}.
\]

This is the precise reconstruction estimate needed to turn

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}
\]

into

\[
\|q-q_X\|_{H^4_{\sc}(I)}\le Q^{-B+C}.
\]

Without the buffer, the theorem risks boundary artifacts.

---

# 4. Cutoff residuals must be controlled after two source derivatives

Let a smoothed/localized source be

\[
p_{\chi}=\chi p.
\]

Then

\[
p_{\chi}''
=
\chi p''
+
2\chi' p'
+
\chi'' p.
\]

So a cutoff that is harmless in \(L^2\) may not be harmless in \(H^2\). The source-residual ledger must require:

\[
\|(\chi-\chi_X)p\|_{H^2_{\sc}}
+
\|\chi' p\|_{H^1_{\sc}}
+
\|\chi'' p\|_{L^2}
\le Q^{-B}
\]

or, more simply, include cutoff-generated terms in \(\mathcal P_I\).

Recommended rule:

\[
\boxed{
\text{all cutoff/smoothing artifacts through two source derivatives are either in }\mathcal P_I\text{ or }H^2_{\sc}\text{-small.}
}
\]

This is important because \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) contains \(p''\).

---

# 5. Gamma/background terms must be exact or \(H^2_{\sc}\)-accurate

If

\[
p=q''=B_2+p_{\mathrm{osc}}+\cdots,
\]

then

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}=p''-6(1-q^2)p.
\]

Therefore \(B_2\) contributes through \(B_2''\).

So the ledger must state:

\[
B_2\in \mathcal P_I
\]

exactly, or

\[
\|B_2-B_{2,X}\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^{-B}.
\]

This requires accuracy for:

\[
B_2,\quad DB_2,\quad D^2B_2
\]

in scaled derivatives.

A zeroth-order asymptotic like

\[
B_2(t)\sim -\frac{1}{2t^2}
\]

is not sufficient unless its first two scaled derivative errors are also \(Q^{-B}\)-small on the interval.

---

# 6. Direct wall-variable completion can bypass explicit \(q_X\), but only with compatibility

An alternative completion theorem is to produce directly:

\[
G_X\in\mathcal W_G,
\qquad
G_{2,X}\in\mathcal W_{G2},
\]

such that

\[
\|\Gamma_G(q)-G_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}}\le Q^{-B},
\]

\[
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-G_{2,X}\|_{L^2}\le Q^{-B}.
\]

This bypasses explicit reconstruction of \(q_X\).

But it must also preserve the compatibility needed by \(\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}\). Since coercivity is applied to the actual \(q\), compatibility of \(G_X,G_{2,X}\) is not strictly required for coercivity. It is required only for finite-atlas Riesz/Remez.

So direct wall completion is acceptable if:

\[
G_X,G_{2,X}
\]

belong to finite spaces for which Riesz/Remez is proved, and residuals to actual walls are \(Q^{-B}\)-small.

Thus there are two valid forms:

## Source form

\[
p_X\in\mathcal P_I,\quad q_X\in\mathcal V_I,
\]

then build

\[
G_X=\Gamma_G(q_X),\quad G_{2,X}=\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X).
\]

## Wall form

Directly build

\[
G_X\in\mathcal W_G,\quad G_{2,X}\in\mathcal W_{G2}.
\]

The source form is stronger and more structured. The wall form is more flexible.

---

# 7. The handoff theorem should include both versions

## WallVariableCompletion\(_{2Y}\), source/wall form

Assume either:

### Source-form completion

There exist

\[
p_X\in\mathcal P_I,\qquad q_X\in\mathcal V_I
\]

with

\[
q_X''=p_X,
\]

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^{-B},
\]

and affine/boundary reconstruction controlled.

Then set:

\[
G_X=\Gamma_G(q_X),
\qquad
G_{2,X}=\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X).
\]

Or:

### Direct wall-form completion

There exist

\[
G_X\in\mathcal W_G,
\qquad
G_{2,X}\in\mathcal W_{G2}
\]

with direct residual bounds.

In either case, conclude:

\[
\boxed{
\|\Gamma_G(q)-G_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-G_{2,X}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B'}.
}
\]

This is the exact handoff to \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\).

---

# 8. Failure classification

The completion theorem fails in four distinct ways.

## A. Source derivative failure

\[
p-p_X
\]

is controlled in \(L^2\) but not \(H^2\). Then \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) is uncontrolled.

## B. Affine reconstruction failure

\[
p-p_X
\]

is small, but \(q-q_X\) has uncontrolled affine part. Then \(\Gamma_G\) can drift.

## C. Background derivative failure

Gamma/background terms are accurate only to zeroth order, not through two source derivatives. Then \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) has residual.

## D. Finite wall-space mismatch

The wall variables are approximable, but not by the spaces

\[
\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2}
\]

used by \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\). Then completion and Riesz do not connect.

Each failure has a different repair.

---

# 9. Final status

The corrected analytic completion module is now:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}
}
\]

with either source-form or direct wall-form completion.

The strongest source-form sufficient condition is:

\[
\boxed{
q''\text{ completed in }H^2_{\sc}
+
\text{affine reconstruction}
+
\text{gamma/tail/cutoff completion through two source derivatives}.
}
\]

This gives actual plain wall variables close to finite wall spaces:

\[
\mathcal W_G,\qquad \mathcal W_{G2}.
\]

It does not assume derivative D4 channels.

## Agent 4 continuation — final proof contract for \(\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}\)

No nomenclature changes.

I now compress the wall-completion module into its final proof contract. The correction remains in force:

\[
\boxed{
\text{D4 supplies only }H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
}
\]

No derivative wall component is a D4 wall unless a separate derivative-channel theorem is proved.

---

# 1. What the theorem must output

The theorem must produce finite wall representatives

\[
G_X\in\mathcal W_G,
\qquad
G_{2,X}\in\mathcal W_{G2},
\]

such that

\[
\boxed{
\|\Gamma_G(q)-G_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-G_{2,X}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

This is the exact handoff to \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\).

It is stronger and more useful than saying only that some source residual is small.

---

# 2. Preferred source-form construction

Use

\[
p=q''.
\]

Let

\[
p_X\in\mathcal P_I,
\qquad
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^{-B_0}.
\]

Let

\[
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I,
\]

where

\[
\mathcal A_1=\operatorname{span}\{1,t\}
\]

absorbs affine reconstruction modes.

Choose

\[
q_X\in\mathcal V_I,
\qquad
q_X''=p_X,
\]

with affine constants matched or included.

Then define

\[
G_X=\Gamma_G(q_X),
\qquad
G_{2,X}=\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X).
\]

The finite wall spaces are

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal W_G
=
\mathcal P_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^2\mathcal P_I+\mathcal P_I+
\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot\mathcal P_I.
}
\]

Then

\[
G_X\in\mathcal W_G,
\qquad
G_{2,X}\in\mathcal W_{G2}.
\]

---

# 3. Required residual ledger

The source residual

\[
r_p=p-p_X
\]

must satisfy a full \(H^2_{\sc}\)-ledger:

\[
\boxed{
\|r_p\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^{-B_0}.
}
\]

If

\[
p=B_2+p_{\mathrm{AFE}}+p_{\mathrm{dual}}+p_{\mathrm{tail}}+p_{\mathrm{smooth}}+p_{\mathrm{err}},
\]

then each residual component must be either included in \(\mathcal P_I\), exact, or \(H^2_{\sc}\)-small:

\[
\sum_{\bullet}
\|r_\bullet\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}
\le Q^{-B_0}.
\]

In particular:

\[
\boxed{
B_2\text{ must be exact or completed through two source derivatives.}
}
\]

Zeroth-order gamma/background approximation is insufficient because

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}=p''-6(1-q^2)p
\]

uses \(p''\).

---

# 4. Reconstruction estimate

The interior reconstruction operator must satisfy

\[
\boxed{
\|\mathcal I^2 r_p\|_{H^4_{\sc}(I)}
\le
Q^C\|r_p\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}.
}
\]

This requires

\[
I\Subset I^*
\]

with \(R^{-C}\)-scale buffer.

The affine part must be handled by one of:

\[
\mathcal A_1\subset\mathcal V_I,
\]

or explicit matching of

\[
(q-q_X)(t_0),\qquad (q-q_X)'(t_0).
\]

Without affine control, \(p\)-completion does not imply wall completion because \(\Gamma_G\) depends on \(q\) and \(q^3\), not only \(q''\).

---

# 5. Nonlinear residual estimate

Let

\[
r=q-q_X.
\]

The wall residuals are:

\[
R_G
=
-6r''
+
(36-36q_X^2)r
-
36q_Xr^2
-
12r^3,
\]

and

\[
R_{G2}
=
r^{(4)}-6r''
+
6q_X^2r''
+
12q_Xr q_X''
+
12q_Xr r''
+
6r^2q_X''
+
6r^2r''.
\]

Assume polynomial atlas bounds:

\[
\|q_X\|_{C^4_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^C.
\]

Then one-dimensional product estimates give

\[
\boxed{
\|R_G\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I)}
+
\|R_{G2}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le
Q^C
\left(
\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}
+
\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}^2
+
\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}}^3
\right).
}
\]

Combining with reconstruction:

\[
\|r\|_{H^4_{\sc}(I)}\le Q^C\|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}
\]

gives the target wall completion after taking \(B_0\) large enough.

---

# 6. Direct wall-form alternative

If source-form completion is unavailable, a weaker but admissible alternative is direct wall-form completion:

find

\[
G_X\in\mathcal W_G,
\qquad
G_{2,X}\in\mathcal W_{G2}
\]

such that

\[
\boxed{
\|\Gamma_G(q)-G_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-G_{2,X}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

This is enough for the corrected rank-two route.

However, this direct wall-form theorem must still use the same finite wall spaces that \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\) controls. Otherwise completion and Riesz do not connect.

---

# 7. Final theorem

## \(\mathrm{WallVariableCompletion}_{2Y}\)

Assume either the source-form construction or direct wall-form construction.

In the source-form case, assume:

1. \(p=q''\) has finite model \(p_X\in\mathcal P_I\) with

\[
\|p-p_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I^*)}\le Q^{-B_0}.
\]

2. Affine modes are included in

\[
\mathcal V_I=\mathcal A_1+\mathcal I^2\mathcal P_I
\]

or normalization-matched.

3. \(I\Subset I^*\) has \(R^{-C}\)-scale buffer.

4. Gamma/background/tail/smoothing residuals are exact, included in \(\mathcal P_I\), or \(H^2_{\sc}\)-small.

5. \(q_X\) has polynomial \(C^4_{\sc}\) bounds.

Then there exist

\[
G_X\in\mathcal W_G,
\qquad
G_{2,X}\in\mathcal W_{G2}
\]

such that

\[
\boxed{
\|\Gamma_G(q)-G_X\|_{H^2_{\sc}(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-G_{2,X}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

This is the exact completion module needed for the corrected plain-wall rank-two analytic route.

---

# 8. Failure routing

\[
p\text{-completion only in }L^2
\Rightarrow
\Gamma_{G,2}\text{ uncontrolled}.
\]

\[
\text{affine reconstruction uncontrolled}
\Rightarrow
\Gamma_G\text{ can drift}.
\]

\[
B_2\text{ or tail not completed through two source derivatives}
\Rightarrow
\Gamma_{G,2}\text{ residual persists}.
\]

\[
\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2}\text{ not Riesz/Remez controlled}
\Rightarrow
\text{completion holds but transfer fails}.
\]

---

## Final Agent 4 status

The wall-completion module is now fully specified.

The best natural target is:

\[
\boxed{
q''\text{-source completion in }H^2_{\sc}
+
\text{affine reconstruction}.
}
\]

This produces the finite wall representatives needed by \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\), without assuming any derivative D4 channels.
