## Edit 3 — add the decisive missing object \(R_{\sing}\)

**Location:** Immediately after the singular-discard operator definition above.

**Insert:**

```latex
\begin{definition}[Actual-source retained singular coherence space]
\label{def:actual-source-retained-singular-coherence-space}
The retained singular space \(R_{\sing,p}\) is not the full Taylor jet space and
is not an arbitrary low-mode space.  It is the finite singular coherence space
generated by the actual local explicit-formula approximation to
\(q''_{\can}\).  Concretely, on a transition--singular overlap patch \(p\), write
\[
  F_p(x)
  :=
  \lambda_p^3q''_{\can}(m_p+\lambda_p x).
\]
A candidate retained space \(R_{\sing,p}\) is admissible only if there exists
\[
  F_p^{\coh}\in R_{\sing,p}
\]
such that
\[
  F_p=F_p^{\coh}+R_p
\]
and
\[
  \boxed{
  \sum_p \rho_p\|R_p\|_{L^2}^2
  \le
  Q^{-B}
  \sum_p \rho_p\|F_p\|_{L^2}^2.
  }
\]
The projection \(P_{\sing,p}\) is the projection onto this actual-source
coherence space.
\end{definition}

\begin{remark}[Do not use a jet-complete singular lift]
\label{rem:do-not-use-jet-complete-singular-lift}
A Taylor-jet-complete singular lift through order \(9\) is functorial for the
D4 live carriers and tends to make
\[
  (I-P_{\sing})T_{\tr\to\sing}[\mathsf C_7]^{\tr}
  =
  (I-P_{\sing})T_{\tr\to\sing}[\mathsf C_{Y12}]^{\tr}
  =
  0.
\]
It therefore gives a rank-zero forbidden channel.  The singular retained space
must be actual-source coherent, not carrier-complete.  The finite audit is
\[
  T_{\tr\to\sing}[\mathsf C_7]^{\tr},
  \quad
  T_{\tr\to\sing}[\mathsf C_{Y12}]^{\tr}
  \stackrel{?}{\in} R_{\sing}.
\]
If both images lie in \(R_{\sing}\), the singular-discard channel is D4-blind.
If at least one has polynomially transverse projection to \(C_{\sing}\), finite
D4 has a genuine forbidden channel.
\end{remark}
```

---

## Edit 4 — add the singular-discard theorem as the downstream closure criterion

**Location:** In the downstream D4 / Stack C2 theorem area, after the wall factor definition.

**Insert:**

```latex
\begin{theorem}[Forbidden singular-discard criterion]
\label{thm:forbidden-singular-discard-criterion}
Assume there is an admissible retained singular coherence space
\(R_{\sing,p}\) and projection \(P_{\sing,p}\) such that the actual canonical
rows obey the global upper
\[
  \boxed{
  \sum_p
  \left\|
  (I-P_{\sing,p})T_{\tr\to\sing,p}J_{\tr,p}(q''_{\can})
  \right\|^2
  \le
  Q^{-B}\mu(\mathcal C).
  }
\]
Assume also that on the extracted Stack-C2 good set,
\[
  \boxed{
  \operatorname{rank}
  \left(
  (I-P_{\sing,p})T_{\tr\to\sing,p}
  E_{\car,p}^{\tr}L_{\clean,p}^{\tr}
  \right)
  \ge1
  }
\]
with polynomial lower singular value on its image.  Then finite D4 has a
forbidden positive atomic channel:
\[
  E_{\wall}^{\forb}(m)\ge Q^{-A}
\]
on the extracted wall-good set, while the actual global row contribution to the
same channel is \(Q^{-B}\)-small in aggregate.
\end{theorem}
```

---

## Edit 5 — add transition polynomial visibility and \(Y12\)-origin compatibility

**Location:** In the finite/O2O3 transition section, or after any remark about central/fold O2/O3.

**Insert:**

```latex
\begin{remark}[Transition \(C_{d_0}[y]\)-visibility]
\label{rem:transition-polynomial-visibility}
Near the fold, write
\[
  x=\frac12+\delta y,
  \qquad
  \delta=R^{-C}.
\]
The central O2/O3 block requires transition visibility of
\[
  \mathbf C_{d_0}[y]
  =
  \operatorname{span}\{1,y,\ldots,y^{d_0}\},
\]
not merely the first odd mode.  Equivalently, the transition retained space must
satisfy
\[
  \boxed{
  \mathbb C_{d_0}[y]\subset V_{\tr}
  }
\]
with \(R^C\)-condition number.  If the final wall degree is not fixed, use the
conservative installed block
\[
  \mathbb C_{14}[y]\subset V_{\tr}.
\]
This is a finite-dimensional Riesz audit.

The compatibility with \(Y12\)-normality is origin-filtered: the modes
\(1,y,\ldots,y^{d_0}\) are source-linear transition coordinate modes.  They do
not have the primitive signature
\[
  (o_N,o_W)
  =
  (D^2N_0,D\mathcal W_{\same})
\]
of the \(Y12\) carrier.  Therefore, provided \(T_{\tr\to\sing}\) transports
formed source expressions and preserves origin labels,
\[
  \Pi_YT_{\tr\to\sing}
  \bigl(
  \mathbb C_{d_0}[y]\text{-source-linear modes}
  \bigr)
  =
  0.
\]
\end{remark}
```

---

## Edit 6 — add the filtered transition compatibility theorem

**Location:** Immediately after Edit 5.

**Insert:**

```latex
\begin{theorem}[Filtered transition compatibility for \(Y12\)]
\label{thm:filtered-transition-compatibility-Y12}
Assume:
\begin{enumerate}
\item \(T_{\tr\to\sing}\) acts on formed labelled source expressions, rather
than recomputing \(N_0\) or \(\mathcal W\) from unlabelled scalar jets.
\item \(T_{\tr\to\sing}\) preserves the refined origin pair
\[
  (o_N,o_W)
\]
and the mixed/same-point/whitening-slot labels near the \(Y12\) sector.
\item The singular nuisance quotient is homogeneous for this same filtration.
\item \(E_2\) has no free primitive \(Y12\)-origin direction.
\end{enumerate}
Then
\[
  \Pi_YT_{\tr\to\sing}
  \bigl(
  \mathbb C_{d_0}[y]\text{-source-linear modes}
  \bigr)
  =
  0,
\]
while
\[
  \Pi_YT_{\tr\to\sing}[\mathsf C_{Y12}]
  \neq0
\]
whenever the local \(Y12\) carrier is live before transition.
\end{theorem}

\begin{remark}[Transition recomputation no-go]
\label{rem:transition-recomputation-no-go}
If \(T_{\tr\to\sing}\) recomputes the nonlinear normal form or moving
same-point whitening from raw transition variables, then source-linear
transition modes can generate primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin terms.  In that case
the origin-filter proof of \(Y12\)-normality fails, and one must replace
Theorem~\ref{thm:filtered-transition-compatibility-Y12} by a direct finite Schur
separation
\[
  \operatorname{dist}
  \left(
  T_{\tr\to\sing}[\mathsf C_{Y12}],
  \mathcal N_{\sing}+\mathcal A_{\actual}
  \right)
  \ge Q^{-A}.
\]
\end{remark}
```

```latex
the proof needs the full three-chart atlas loop
```

```latex
the candidate Lambda is the full atlas holonomy
```

```latex
The full atlas loop is no longer the primary candidate.  The current primary
finite-D4 forbidden operator is the local transition--singular discarded
projection
\[
  \Lambda_{\tr,\sing,p}
  =
  (I-P_{\sing,p})T_{\tr\to\sing,p}P_{\tr,p}.
\]
A returned two-chart loop or a full atlas loop may still be useful as a
diagnostic, but the positive atomic forbidden channel only requires the
discarded singular component and the global upper for actual rows.
```

## Edit 8 — add a short \(\alpha\)-admissibility caution if not already present

**Location:** In the finite/O2O3 determinant/alpha remarks.

**Insert:**

```latex
\begin{remark}[\(\alpha\)-twisting is conditional]
\label{rem:alpha-twisting-conditional}
A scalar \(\alpha\)-dependent phase can help determinant branch-count survival,
because exterior expansions contain factors \(e^{ir\Theta(\alpha)}\).  It does
not improve local graph/O2/O3 nondegeneracy if it merely multiplies an entire
complex branch by a scalar phase:
\[
  e^{i\Theta(\alpha)}V_-=V_-.
\]
Therefore \(\alpha\)-averaging is proof-critical only after an
\(\alpha\)-admissibility theorem: zero curves must persist, Stack-C2 quantities
must vary polynomially, the twist must have nontrivial speed, and the resulting
good \((m,\alpha)\)-points must specialize back to the physical target.  Without
this package, \(\alpha\)-twisting is a heuristic determinant-survival tool, not
a proof step.
\end{remark}
```

---

## Edit 9 — add stale/incorrect removal note for pure \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) wall if present

**Location:** Global search for any statement asserting the \(Y12\) wall is pure \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) without proof.

**Replace with:**

```latex
The \(Y12\) wall is, in general, the triangularized linear form
\[
  \beta_9^{\tri}\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}.
\]
It becomes pure \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) only after a finite quotient calculation proves
\[
  \beta_9^{\tri}=0
\]
in the relevant \(Y12\)-graded quotient.  Until then all anti-concentration and
degree audits must use the full line
\[
  \beta_9^{\tri}\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}.
\]
```

## Edit 10 — add final “unclear / must decide” note to the terminal dependency ledger

**Location:** In the terminal ledger / active input status table.

**Insert at the end:**

```latex
\begin{remark}[Final unresolved finite decisions]
\label{rem:final-unresolved-finite-decisions}
After the current cleanup, the remaining decisive finite decisions are:
\begin{enumerate}
\item Define \(R_{\sing}\) as an actual-source coherence space and test
\[
  (I-P_{\sing})T_{\tr\to\sing}
  \operatorname{span}\{[\mathsf C_7]^{\tr},[\mathsf C_{Y12}]^{\tr}\}.
\]
Rank \(0\) kills the singular-discard \(\Lambda\)-route; rank \(1\) gives a
rank-one forbidden wall; rank \(2\) gives the full rank-two forbidden wall.

\item Decide whether \(T_{\tr\to\sing}\) transports formed labelled carriers or
recomputes \(N_0/\mathcal W\).  Transport preserves the \(Y12\)-origin filter;
recomputation forces a direct Schur separation theorem.

\item Decide whether O2/O3 will be proved by full graph-block Riesz survival,
by determinant branch survival, or by an \(\alpha\)-admissible determinant
average.  Pure-branch graph non-polynomiality alone does not certify the full
canonical augmented matrix.

\item Keep \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) sealed unless an explicit atom-local live-fiber
fitting mechanism is exhibited.
\end{enumerate}
\end{remark}
```

---

These are the last essential additions I see. The main stale risk in the current architecture is overemphasizing a returned atlas holonomy when the stronger current candidate is the **positive discarded singular projection**. The second stale risk is allowing transition/O2O3 polynomial enrichment to look like a \(Y12\)-tangent; the filtered-origin theorem above prevents that.
